studyMetadata.title: Tsage Gaskiya (1)
studyMetadata.subtitle: The Foundations of Christian Belief
studyMetadata.description: 8 essential Bible studies to help you unlock the truth of God’s Word
studyMetadata.author: Rick Creighton
studyMetadata.shareMessage:
Check out this Bible Study Reader app.

Available soon on Google Play and the App Store.

theme.theme-annotation: Blue-dominant study theme — deep royal blue palette with subdued gold accent. Blue is the dominant colour throughout surfaces, borders, text, and interactive elements. Gold appears only as a quiet highlight. Authoritative and clear.

elements.text1: Akwai ra’ayoyi daban-daban akan Allah da kuma yaya Allah yake. Samun ganewar yaya Allah yake a zahirance. To dole kuma mu duba me Littafi Mai Tsarki ya faɗa yarda Allah ya bayyana kansa a gare mu acikin Littafinsa Mai Tsarki
elements.text2: Hol no Alla wayi tigi rigi? Yimɓe ina njogii miijooji kala — won heen ina cikka ko o woɗɗuɗo, mo anndaaka, won heen ko doole moƴƴe, walla laamɗo kosmos. Kono en ngalaa haaju e teskaade. Alla hollitii en hoore mum e nder deftere nde, ko ɗoon puɗɗortoɗen.
elements.text3: What is God really like? People have all sorts of ideas — some think of Him as distant and unknowable, others as a kind force, or a cosmic rule-giver. But we don’t have to guess. God has revealed Himself to us in the Bible, and that is where we begin.

elements.text1: Wanene Allah
elements.text2: Hol ko woni Alla?
elements.text3: Who is God?

elements.passageText1: <sup>3</sup>A kullum muna gode wa Allah, Uban Ubangijinmu Yesu Almasihu, duk sa’ad da muke yi muku addu’a,
elements.passageText3: <sup>3</sup>Kullum muna gode wa Allah, Uban Ubangijinmu Yesu Kiristi, sa’ad da muke addu’a dominku.
elements.passageText4: <sup>3</sup>Koo ndeye to e min ngaɗana’on do’aare e min ngetta Allah Nyaako Joomiraawo meeɗen Yeesu Almasiihu. E min ngettamo ngam mooɗon,
elements.passageText5: <sup>3</sup>We always give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,

elements.question1: Me wannan ayar ta gaya mana game da wanene Allah?
elements.question2: Hol ko oo aaye haalanta en hol ko woni Alla?
elements.question3: What does this verse tell us about who God is?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan ayar ta bayyana Allah a matsayin ’Uban Ubangijinmu Yesu Almasihu’, wanda hakan ke nuna cewa Allah ba wata ƙarfin da ba shi da rai ba ne, sai dai shi mutum ne mai rai wanda yake da dangantakar Uba da Ɗa tare da Yesu. Hakan kuma yana nuna cewa Allah wani ne da za mu iya gode masa da kuma magana da shi ta wurin addu’a.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya yi amfani da wata take ta musamman ga Allah a nan — me wannan take ke gaya maka game da irin dangantakar da Allah yake da ita tare da Yesu, da kuma me hakan ke ɗauke game da yanayin sa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Oo aaye hollitii Allah ko ’Nyaako Joomiraawo meeɗen Yeesu Almasiihu’, ko hollita Alla wanaa doole mo walaa yonki, ammaa o woni neɗɗo nguurndam mo jogii jokkondiral Baaba e Ɓiɗɗo e Yeesu. Ɗum kadi hollita Alla woni mo mbaɗaten njettoor e haaldirde e makko e nder do’aare.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus huutorii innde heɓɓundi sanne ngam Alla ɗoo — hol ko ndee innde anndinta ma e ko woni jokkondiral Alla e Yeesu, e hol ko ɗum hollita dow yontere muuɗum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: This verse identifies God as ’the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ’, revealing that God is not an impersonal force but a personal being who stands in a Father-Son relationship with Jesus. It also shows that God is someone to whom we can give thanks and speak to in prayer.
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses a very specific title for God here — what does that title tell you about the kind of relationship God has with Jesus, and what might that imply about His nature?

elements.text1: Wacce shaida ce akwai ta tabbatar da cewa akwai Allah
elements.text2: Hol ko holliti wonde Alla ina woodi?
elements.text3: What evidence is there that God exists?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Dubi yadda sararin sama yake bayyana ɗaukakar Allah!
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>Sammai suna shelar ɗaukakar Allah;
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>The heavens declare the glory of God; the sky displays his handiwork.

elements.question1: Me wannan ayar ta nuna game da duniyar halitta a matsayu n shaida ga Allah?
elements.question2: Hol ko ndee aaye hollirta e aduna tagooje ko seede Alla?
elements.question3: What does this verse suggest about the natural world as evidence for God?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan ayar tana nuna cewa halitta kanta shaida ce ta kasancewar Allah da ɗaukakarsa. Sararin sama ba ya tsaya tsaka-tsaki — yana ’bayyana’ wani abu game da Allah. Kamar yadda wata zane-zane ke nuna mai zanen ta, haka kuma duniyar halitta ke nuna Mahalicci. Shaida ta Allah ba a ɓoye ta ba — tana iya ganuwa a sama a kowace rana.
elements.questionHint1: Idan aka ce sama yana ’bayyana’ wani abu, me hakan ke nuna game da abin da mai lura da duniyar halitta ya kamata ya iya yanke hukunci a kai?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ndee aaye hollitii tagooje heɓɓude hoore mum ina seeda wonde Alla ina woodi e teddungal muuɗum. Ngurleeji ɗi ngalaa miijo — ina njannginiri feere e ko fayti e Alla. Ko nii ɗerewol hollita mo windii, ko non kadi aduna tagooje hollita Tagɗo. Seede Alla walaa nder suudu woo — ina yiya e dow men koo ɗume.
elements.questionHint2: To ngurleeji ngoni njannginta feere, hol ko ɗum hollita e ko yimɓe ndaari aduna tagooje mbaNnginata?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The verse suggests that creation itself is a witness to God’s existence and glory. The sky and heavens are not neutral — they actively ’declare’ and ’display’ something about God. Just as a piece of artwork points to an artist, the created world points to a Creator. Evidence for God is not hidden away; it is visible above us every day.
elements.questionHint3: If the heavens are said to ’declare’ something, what does that imply about what an observer of the natural world should be able to conclude?

elements.passageText1: <sup>13</sup>Kai ne ka halicci kowace gaɓa ta jikina, <sup>14</sup>Ina yabonka gama kai abin tsoro ne,
elements.passageText3: <sup>13</sup>Gama ka halicci ciki-cikina; <sup>14</sup>Ina yabonka domin yadda ka yi ni abin tsoro ne da kuma abin mamaki;
elements.passageText5: <sup>13</sup>Certainly you made my mind and heart; you wove me together in my mother’s womb. <sup>14</sup>I will give you thanks because your deeds are awesome and amazing. You knew me thoroughly;

elements.question1: Menene bayanin mai zabura game da ’saƙa’ yake nunawa game da hannun Allah a rayuwar ɗan adam?
elements.question2: Hol ko siftorde Jabuura’en nde ’woɗɗude’ hollirta ko fayti e naatgol Alla e nguurndam aadee?
elements.question3: What does the psalmist’s description of being ’woven together’ suggest about God’s involvement in human life?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Misalin ’saƙa’ yana nuna cewa rayuwar ɗan adam ba haɗari ba ce ko wani lamari na halitta kawai, sai dai aiki ne na gangan na Allah tare da kula. Kamar yadda masaƙi ke aiki da zaren ɗaya bayan ɗaya da niyyar gani da kula, haka Allah ya shiga cikin ƙirƙirar kowane mutum da kansa. Hakan yana nuna cewa Allah yana da cikakken sani da kuma kula da kowane mutum daga farkon rayuwarsa.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da abin da saƙa ke ɗauke da shi — za ka/ki ce aiki ne na sakaci da gudu-gudu, ko kuwa na gangan da kula? Me hakan ke gaya maka game da yadda Allah ya tsara ka/ki?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Siftorde ’woɗɗude’ hollita nguurndam aadee wanaa haɗeende walla ko waɗɗaani, ammaa ko baɗngal gollal Alla ceerngal e miijo. Ko nii foofoowo golli foofo e foofo e miijo, ko non Allah waɗi gollal muuɗum e tagude koo neɗɗo. Ɗum hollita Alla jogii ande ceerngal e nunaare neɗɗo koo gooto daga fuɗɗorde nguurndam muuɗum.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo ko woɗɗude haani — mbaaɗaa wiyde ko gollal ladde mo faayre, koo ko gollal miijo e nunaare? Hol ko ɗum anndinta ma e no Alla tagii ma?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The image of being ’woven together’ suggests that human life is not an accident or a random biological event, but an intentional, careful act of craftsmanship by God. Just as a weaver works deliberately, thread by thread, God was personally involved in forming each person. This points to God’s intimate knowledge of and care for every individual human being from the very beginning of their existence.
elements.questionHint3: Think about what weaving involves — would you describe it as a careless, random process, or a deliberate and careful one? What does that tell you about how God formed you?

elements.passageText1: <sup>30</sup>Yesu ya yi waɗansu mu’ujizai da yawa dabam dabam a gaban almajiran, waɗanda ba a rubuta a littafin nan ba. <sup>31</sup>Amma an rubuta waɗannan ne, domin ku ba da gaskiya Yesu shi ne Almasihu, Ɗan Allah, ta wurin gaskatawa kuma ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>30</sup>Yesu ya yi waɗansu abubuwan banmamaki da yawa a gaban almajiransa da ba a rubuta a wannan littafi ba. <sup>31</sup>An dai rubuta waɗannan ne don ku gaskata Yesu ne Kiristi, Ɗan Allah kuma ta wurin gaskatawa ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>30</sup>Yeesu waɗii ko hayɗinii feere‑feere ɗuɗɗum yeeso tokkuɓemo ɗum windaaka nder ɗerewol ngo’ol. <sup>31</sup>Ammaa ɗu’um ɗum windaa ngam koolooɗon Yeesu woni Almasiihu Ɓiɗɗo Allah, nden laawol hoolaakimo keɓon yonki ki re’ataa barka inde maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>30</sup>Now Jesus performed many other miraculous signs in the presence of the disciples, which are not recorded in this book. <sup>31</sup>But these are recorded so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.

elements.question1: Me ya sa Yohanna ya ce ya rubuta waɗannan alamu? Me wannan ya gaya mana game da manufar Littafi Mai Tsarki?
elements.question2: Hol ko waɗi Yuhanna wiyde o winndii ɗee maandeeji? Hol ko ɗum anndini en e faandaare Biibal ngal?
elements.question3: Why does John say he recorded these signs? What does this tell us about the purpose of the Bible?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna ya ce ya rubuta waɗannan alamu domin masu karatu su ba da gaskiya cewa Yesu shi ne Almasihu, Ɗan Allah, kuma ta wurin gaskatawa su sami rai a cikin sunansa. Hakan yana gaya mana cewa Littafi Mai Tsarki ba tarihi kawai ba ne ko tarin koyarwar ɗabi’a — yana da manufa ta musamman ta kawo mutane zuwa bangaskiya da kuma, ta wurin bangaskiya, zuwa rai.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya fara gaya mana kai tsaye abin da yake fatan ganin a masu karantawa — za ka iya ganin burin biyu da ya ambata, kuma ta yaya suke da alaƙa da juna?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yuhanna wi’i o winndii ɗee maandeeji ngam joolooɓe ɓen njaaɓa koolugol Yeesu woni Almasiihu Ɓiɗɗo Allah, nden laawol hoolaakimo keɓon yonki ki re’ataa barka innde maako. Ɗum anndina en Biibal ngal wanaa tan ɗerewol laamu walla keɓngo naataneeji laawol — o jogii faandaare njannginde yimɓe nguurndam tan. Windaama ngam waɗde yimɓe njaaɓa hoolagol, nde laawol nden, yonki.
elements.questionHint2: Yuhanna hollita tiiɗuɗo ko faandaare maako e joolooɓe ɓen — mbaaɗaa yi’ude faandaare ɗiɗi mo holliti, e no ɗi njokki e rewde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John says he recorded these signs so that readers would come to believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and through that belief find life in his name. This tells us that the Bible is not merely a historical record or a collection of moral teachings — it has an intentional, evangelistic purpose. It is written to bring people to faith and, through faith, to life.
elements.questionHint3: John tells us directly what outcome he was hoping for in his readers — can you spot the two goals he mentions, and how are they connected to each other?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>A zamanin dā, Allah ya yi wa kakannin kakanninmu magana ta hanyoyi masu yawa iri iri, ta bakin annabawa, <sup>2</sup>amma a zamanin nan na ƙarshe, sai ya yi mana magana ta wurin Ɗansa, wanda ya sa magājin kome, wanda ta wurinsa ne kuma ya halicci duniya. <sup>3</sup>Shi ne hasken ɗaukakar Allah, ainihin kamannin zatinsa kuma, shi kuma yana a riƙe da dukan abubuwa ta ikon faɗarsa. Bayan da ya tsarkake zunubanmu, sai ya zauna a dama da Maɗaukaki a can Sama.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>A dā Allah ya yi wa kakanni-kakanninmu magana ta bakin annabawa a lokuta dabam-dabam da kuma a hanyoyi iri-iri, <sup>2</sup>amma a waɗannan kwanaki a ƙarshe ya yi magana da mu ta wurin Ɗansa, wanda ya naɗa magājin dukan abubuwa, wanda kuma ta wurinsa ya halicci duniya. <sup>3</sup>Ɗan shi ne hasken ɗaukakar Allah, shi ne kuma ainihin kamanninsa, yana riƙe da dukan abubuwa ta wurin kalmarsa mai iko. Bayan ya yi tsarkakewa don zunubai, sai ya zauna a hannun daman Maɗaukaki a sama.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>Ndenno Allah metanii maama’en meeɗen nde ɗuɗɗum e ɗate ɗuɗɗe daga konduɗe annabo’en. <sup>2</sup>Ammaa nder balɗe ɗe’e ɗe re’uki duuniyaaru o metiranii’en Ɓiɗɗo maako, mo o suɓi warta donoowo koo ɗume. Ɗonmaa barka maako Allah tagi duuniyaaru. <sup>3</sup>E mo holla jayngol teddungal Allah, nden nder koo ɗume e mo nandi e Allah. E mo jogorii koo ɗume wolde maako goodunde bawɗe. Gaɗa o waɗii laawol yaafaaki hakke, o jooɗii ton dow to jungo nyaamo ngo Allah Joomu Mangu.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>After God spoke long ago in various portions and in various ways to our ancestors through the prophets, <sup>2</sup>in these last days he has spoken to us in a son, whom he appointed heir of all things, and through whom he created the world. <sup>3</sup>The Son is the radiance of his glory and the representation of his essence, and he sustains all things by his powerful word, and so when he had accomplished cleansing for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Allah ya yi mana magana, bisa ga wannan nassin? Me yake gaya mana game da dangantakar Yesu da Allah?
elements.question2: Hol no Alla haaldirta e men, fawaade e ndee ɗoo tonngoode? Hol ko nde haalanta en e jokkondiral Iisaa e Alla?
elements.question3: How has God spoken to us, according to this passage? What does it tell us about Jesus’ relationship to God?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bisa ga wannan nassi, Allah ya yi magana a zamanin da ta bakin annabawa ta hanyoyi da dama da kuma lokuta daban-daban, amma yanzu ya yi mana magana a ƙarshe ta wurin Ɗansa Yesu. Wannan nassi yana yin da’awa masu girma game da dangantakar Yesu da Allah: shi ne ’hasken ɗaukakar Allah’ da kuma ’ainihin kamannin zatinsa’, ma’ana ganin Yesu shi ne ganin Allah a hakika. Yesu kuma ya halicci duniya, yana riƙe da dukan abubuwa, kuma ya tsarkake zunubai — duk waɗannan suna nuna cewa shi yana da ainihin asalin Allah.
elements.questionHint1: Wannan nassi yana bambanta yadda Allah ya yi magana ’a zamanin da’ da yadda ya yi magana ’a zamanin nan na ƙarshe’ — me ya sha bamban tsakanin hanyoyin biyu, kuma me bayani game da Ɗan a ayar 3 ke gaya maka game da yadda yake da alaƙa da Allah kansa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Fawaade e ndee tonngoode, Alla haaldiri e ɓesngu meeɗen daga ada e nder ɗate ɗuɗɗe e laabi ɗuɗɗi daga konduɗe annabo’en, ammaa jooni o haaldiranii’en haa timmi e nder Ɓiɗɗo maako Yeesu. Ndee tonngoode waɗii miijooji mawɗi dow jokkondiral Yeesu e Alla: o woni ’jayngol teddungal Allah’ e ’nandi e Allah haa timmi’, ko hollita yi’ugol Yeesu ko yi’ugol Alla goonga. Yeesu kadi tagi duuniyaaru, jogorii koo ɗume, o waɗii yaafaaki hakke — fow ɗi hollita o jogii yontere ceerngal Allah.
elements.questionHint2: Ndee tonngoode hollita laawol Alla haaldiri ’ndenno’ e laawol o haaldiri ’jooni nder balɗe ɗe re’uki’ — hol ko waylii hakkunde laabi ɗiɗi ɗi, e hol ko siftorde Ɓiɗɗo on nder aaya 3 anndinta ma e ko Ɓiɗɗo on jokki ɓuri heen e Alla hoore muuɗum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: According to this passage, God spoke in the past through the prophets in many different ways, but has now spoken definitively and finally through His Son, Jesus. The passage makes extraordinary claims about Jesus’ relationship to God: he is the ’radiance of his glory’ and the ’representation of his essence’, meaning that to see Jesus is to see what God is truly like. Jesus also created the world, sustains all things, and has now accomplished the cleansing of sins — pointing to a unique divine identity.
elements.questionHint3: The passage contrasts how God spoke ’long ago’ with how he has spoken ’in these last days’ — what is different about the two ways, and what does the description of the Son in verse 3 tell you about how closely he is linked to God himself?

elements.text1: Yaya kamanin Allah yake?
elements.text2: Hol no Alla wayi?
elements.text3: What is God like?

elements.passageText1: <sup>3</sup>Suna ta kiran junansu suna cewa,
elements.passageText3: <sup>3</sup>Suna kiran juna,
elements.passageText5: And one called to another and said: 3’Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of Heaven’s Armies! His majestic splendor fills the entire earth!’

elements.question1: Me ke nunar da cewa Allah yana da tsarki? Me ya sa ka/ki tsammaci an maimaita wannan kalmar sau uku?
elements.question2: Hol ko woni maanda Alla mawnineede wonde ’ceniiɗo’? Ngam kadi a miijii ndee wolde ina ruttoo laabi tati?
elements.question3: What does it mean for God to be described as ’holy’? Why do you think that word is repeated three times?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Cewa Allah yana da tsarki yana nufin ya rabu gaba ɗaya — cikakke a ɗabi’a, tsarkakakke, kuma ya bambanta gaba ɗaya da komai a duniyar halitta. Tsarki ba ɗaya daga cikin halayensa kawai ba ne — yana magana ne game da cikakken bambancin sa da girmarsa. Maimaita sau uku (‘mai tsarki, mai tsarki, mai tsarki’) shi ne mafi ƙarfin jaddadawa a harshen Ibraniyanci — ba kawai yana cewa Allah mai tsarki ƙwarai ba, sai dai cewa tsarki ne ke bayyana kasancewarsa ta mafi girman matakin da za a iya gani. Babu wani hali ko sifa na Allah da aka yabe ta hanyar ninka sau uku a cikin Littafi Mai Tsarki.
elements.questionHint1: A yaren yau da kullum, idan kana/kina son jaddada cewa wani abu gaskiya ne ƙwarai, me kake/kike yi — kuma ta yaya wannan ra’ayin ya shafi dalilin da ya sa mala’iku suka maimaita kalmar ’mai tsarki’ sau uku ba sau ɗaya?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Alla wonde ’ceniiɗo’ hollita o woni ceerungal haa timmi — o timmi e laawol moralnaawol, o ceniiɗo, o waylii e koo ɗume nder aduna tagooje. Ceniingal wanaa tan goɗɗo nder sifaaji maako fow — o haala ceerungal muuɗum haa timmi e teddungal muuɗum. Ruttugol laabi tati (‘ceniiɗo, ceniiɗo, ceniiɗo’) ko jaddugal gasi ɓuri heen hakkunde luumooje Ibiraaninkooje — wanaa tan ina wi’a Alla ceniiɗo ɓuri, ammaa ceniingal timmundi koo ɗume woni yontere muuɗum e darja mawnirde haa timmi. Walaa goɗɗo nder sifaaji Alla mo jettooranaa e laawol ngo ngal nder Biibal ngal.
elements.questionHint2: E haala hannde, to ndaɗɗa jaddugol ko goonga sanne, hol ko mbaɗataa — e no miijo ngoo naatata e seedtugol ngam ɗume mala’ika’en ɓen nguuri wolde ’ceniiɗo’ laabi tati hoto tan gooto?
elements.sampleAnswer3: For God to be ’holy’ means He is utterly set apart — morally perfect, pure, and completely unlike anything in the created world. Holiness is not just one quality among many but speaks to His absolute otherness and majesty. The threefold repetition (‘holy, holy, holy’) is the strongest form of emphasis in Hebrew — it is not just saying God is very holy, but that holiness defines His very being to the highest possible degree. No other attribute of God is praised in this tripled way in Scripture.
elements.questionHint3: In everyday language, if you want to stress how extremely something is true, what do you do — and how might that same idea apply to why the angels repeat the word ’holy’ three times rather than just once?

elements.passageText1: <sup>7</sup>Da kun san ni, da kun san Ubana ma. Amma daga yanzu kun san shi, kun kuma gan shi.’ <sup>8</sup>Sai Filibus ya ce masa, ‘Ya Ubangiji, ka nuna mana Uban, bukatarmu ta biya ke nan.’ <sup>9</sup>Yesu ya ce masa, ‘Na daɗe tare da ku haka, amma har yanzu ba ka san ni ba, Filibus? Duk wanda ya gan ni, ai, ya ga Uban. Yaya za ka ce, ‘Nuna mana Uban’’?
elements.passageText3: <sup>7</sup>Da kun san ni, da za ku san Ubana ma. Daga yanzu, kun san shi kun kuma gan shi.’ <sup>8</sup>Filibus ya ce, ‘Ubangiji, ka nuna mana Uban, wannan kuwa zai biya bukatarmu.’ <sup>9</sup>Yesu ya amsa ya ce,  ‘Ba san ni ba, Filibus, duk da daɗewan nan da na yi tare da ku? Duk wanda ya gan ni, ai, ya ga Uban. Yaya za ka ce, ‘Ka nuna mana Uban’?
elements.passageText4: <sup>7</sup>O wi’iɓe, ‘Daa on andiyam, daa on andi Nyaako am Mo Dow maa. Daga jooni on andimo on ngi’iimo.’ <sup>8</sup>Filibus wi’imo, ‘Moodibbo, hollumin Nyaako Mo Dow on, non heƴiimin.’ <sup>9</sup>Yeesu nootii wi’i, ‘Filibus, ko neeɓi e mi wondi e mooɗon, ammaa fuu e non a andaayam? Koo moye giiɗoyam yi’ii Nyaako Mo Dow. Ngam ɗume mbi’ataa mi holla’on Nyaako Mo Dow?
elements.passageText5: <sup>7</sup>If you have known me, you will know my Father too. And from now on you do know him and have seen him.’ <sup>8</sup>Philip said to him, ‘Lord, show us the Father, and we will be content.’ <sup>9</sup>Jesus replied, ‘Have I been with you for so long, and you have not known me, Philip? The person who has seen me has seen the Father! How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?’

elements.question1: Me Yesu ya yi da’awa game da dangantakar tsakaninsa da Uba? Me hakan ke nunar wa mu game da yadda muke fahimtar Allah?
elements.question2: Hol ko Iisaa haali e jokkondiral hakkunde makko e Baaba on? Hol ko ɗum firti e no npaamiren Alla?
elements.question3: What does Jesus claim about the relationship between Himself and the Father? What does that mean for how we understand God?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu yana yin da’awa mai ban mamaki: cewa duk wanda ya gan shi ya ga Uba. Ba sai faɗa wata saƙa daga Allah ba ne, ko wani mai koyar da ilimin Allah — shi ne ainihin kamanninsa da kasancewarsa. Hakan yana nufin idan muke son sanin yaya Allah yake, ba sai mun duba wani wuri daban da Yesu ba: tausayinsa, gaskiyarsa, yadda ya bi da mutane, shiriyarsa ta ba da ransa — duk waɗannan suna bayyana halin Allah Uba.
elements.questionHint1: Filibus ya roƙi a nuna masa Uba kamar dai Uba yana wani wuri daban — me ya sa Yesu ya yi mamaki da wannan tambayar, kuma me amsar sa ke ɗauke da shi game da inda ake iya samun Uba ta hakika?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu wi’i ko kiɗɗo sanne: fuu yi’i mo yi’ii Baaba on. O wanaa tan jannguɗo saƙa daga Allah, koo mo janngina e Allah — o woni nandi e Alla goonga e wonaare muuɗum. Ɗum hollita to ndaɗɗa andugol no Alla wa’i, haani mbaɗen ndaari feere woo saka Yeesu: nunaare muuɗum, goonga muuɗum, no o darni yimɓe, taƴugol muuɗum e yonki muuɗum — fow ɗi hollita yontere Allah Baaba.
elements.questionHint2: Filibus rowtii hollugol Baaba on ko nii Baaba on ina won feere — ngam ɗume Yeesu yi’i ndee tiiɗunde, e hol ko jaabawol muuɗum hollita e to Baaba on mbaaɗaa yi’ugol goonga?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus makes a remarkable claim: that to have seen Him is to have seen the Father. He is not merely a messenger from God, or someone who teaches about God — He is the very image and presence of God. This means that if we want to know what God is like, we do not need to look further than Jesus: His compassion, His honesty, His treatment of people, His willingness to lay down His life — all of these reveal the character of God the Father.
elements.questionHint3: Philip asks to be shown the Father as if the Father were somewhere else — why does Jesus find this question so surprising, and what does His answer imply about where the Father can actually be found?

elements.passageText1: <sup>2</sup>Kafin a kafa tuddai, <sup>4</sup>A gare ka shekara dubu, kamar kwana ɗaya ne,
elements.passageText3: <sup>2</sup>Kafin a haifi duwatsu <sup>4</sup>Gama shekaru dubu a gabanka
elements.passageText5: <sup>2</sup>Even before the mountains were born, or you gave birth to the earth and the world, from eternity to eternity you are God. <sup>4</sup>Yes, in your eyes a thousand years are like yesterday that quickly passes, or like one of the divisions of the nighttime.

elements.question1: Me wannan nassi ke gaya mana game da dangantakar Allah da lokaci?
elements.question2: Hol ko ndee tonngoode haalanta en e jokkondiral Alla e saha?
elements.question3: What does this passage tell us about God’s relationship to time?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan nassi yana gaya mana cewa Allah yana wanzuwa a waje da lokaci kuma kafin lokaci kansa — ya kasance Allah kafin ƙasa ko duwatsu su wanzu, kuma zai ci gaba da kasancewa Allah har abada. Ayar 4 tana nuna cewa Allah ba ya jin lokaci kamar yadda muke ji: abin da ya zama shekara dubu a gare mu kamar kwana ɗaya ne a gare shi. Allah ba ya ƙarƙashin lokaci kamar yadda muke — yana ganin dukan tarihi kamar yanzu ne a gare shi, wanda hakan ke nunar da shi ba ya kama masa mamaki, ba ya hanzari, kuma ba ya tsugunne tare da jinkiri ba.
elements.questionHint1: Mai zabura ya ce Allah ya wanzu kafin duwatsu ko ƙasa su ’haife’ — me hakan ke gaya maka game da ko Allah yana da farko, da kuma ta yaya hakan zai iya shafar yadda ya ji lokaci idan aka kwatanta da mu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ndee tonngoode hollita Alla wondi daga ada e dow saha — o woni Allah daga Adam daga ƙaɓel leydi walla duwɗe njoli, nden o wontata Allah faa abada. Aaya 4 hollita Alla haɗataa saha ko nii mbaɗaten — ko mbaɗaten shakooje ujunere e yeeso muuɗum ko nii hannde seɗɗunde waylirta. Alla jokkaaka saha ko nii mbaɗaten — o ndaara tariiji fow ko nii jooni e yeeso muuɗum, ko hollita o fawataako hayre, o jeyataa e hawagol, o fawataako e jiimol.
elements.questionHint2: Jabuura’en hollita Alla wonii daga ɓanndu ƙaɓel leydi walla duuniyaaru ’njoli’ — hol ko ɗum anndinta ma e to Alla fuɗɗiti koo wiyat, e no ɗum waylitata laawol o nanndi saha ko nii mbaɗaten?
elements.sampleAnswer3: This passage tells us that God exists outside of and before time itself — He was God before the earth or mountains existed, and He will continue to be God forever. Verse 4 shows that God does not experience time as we do: what feels like a thousand years to us is like a single day to Him. God is not constrained by time the way we are; He sees all of history as present to Him at once, which means He is never caught off guard, never in a hurry, and never late.
elements.questionHint3: The psalmist says God existed even before the mountains or the earth were ’born’ — what does that tell you about whether God had a beginning, and how might that affect the way He experiences time compared to us?

elements.passageText1: <sup>17</sup>Kowace kyakkyawar baiwa, da kowace cikakkiyar kyauta, daga Sama suke, sun sauko ne daga wurin Uban haskoki, wanda ba sauyawa ko wata alamar sākewa a gare shi faufau.
elements.passageText3: <sup>17</sup>Kowane abu mai kyau da kuma kowace cikakkiyar kyauta daga sama suke, sun sauko ne daga wurin Uban haskoki, wanda ba sauyawa ko wata alamar sākewa a gare shi faufau.
elements.passageText4: <sup>17</sup>Koo ngale dokkal bongal e koo ɗume caahu jamum fuu daga to Allah Nyaako Mo Dow ɗum ƴiwoyta, taguɗo naange e lewru e koode. Kanko o waylitataako, o nandaa e ɗowdi goosotoondi.
elements.passageText5: <sup>17</sup>All generous giving and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom there is no variation or the slightest hint of change.

elements.question1: Me ke nunar da cewa babu ’sauyawa ko wata alamar sākewa’ a Allah? Me ya sa hakan yana da muhimmanci?
elements.question2: Ko woni maana ’walaa waylitaaki koo seeɗa’ haa Allah? Ngam ɗume ɗum mari nafa sanne?
elements.question3: What does it mean that there is ’no variation or the slightest hint of change’ in God? Why is that significant?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yana nufin halin Allah, ƙaunarsa, alkawuransa, da kirkinsa ba su taɓa canzawa ko girgiza ba. Ba kamar alaƙar mutum ba — inda mutane za su iya kasancewa da kirki a ranar guda kuma a yi sanyi a wata rana — Allah yana dawwama gaba ɗaya. Wannan yana da muhimmanci ƙwarai domin yana nufin za mu iya dogara da shi gaba ɗaya. Ƙaunarsa ga mu a yau tana da ƙarfi iri ɗaya da takan kasance jiya kuma za ta kasance gobe. Alkawuransa ba su ƙare ba kuma ba a sake rubuta su. Ba mu da buƙatar damu cewa Allah ya canza ra’ayinsa game da mu.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da lokacin da canjin yanayin wani ko kararyar alkawarinsa ya sa ya yi muku wahala a dogara da shi — ta yaya cikakken rashin canzawa na Allah zai yi bambanci ga yadda kake/kike dangantaka da shi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ɗum hollita yontere Alla, yidde muuɗum, alkawaaruuji muuɗum, e moƴƴere muuɗum njaaɓataako waylitugol koo seeɗa. Ko woɗɗi e jokkondirali aadee — to yimɓe mbaawi wonde moƴƴuɓe hannde e ɓenndi tammbi — Alla woni timmiɗo haa timmi. Ɗum ina nafa sanne ngam hollita mbaawi hoolagol e makko haa timmi. Yidde muuɗum e men hannde woni darja hono nde wonnoo janngo e hono nde wontata jaango. Alkawaaruuji muuɗum cokataako koo wayyitaaka. Haani mbaaɗen haawi to Alla waylitii miijo muuɗum e men.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e sahaa ngo yontere waylitere walla alkawol ngo’o yefitaaɗo waɗi hawrugol hoolagol ɗum woyni — no Alla wonde haa timmi waylitataako waɗata feere e no njokkoɗon e makko?
elements.sampleAnswer3: It means that God’s character, His love, His promises, and His goodness never shift or fluctuate. Unlike human relationships — where people can be kind one day and cold the next — God is completely consistent. This is enormously significant because it means we can trust Him fully. His love for us today is exactly as strong as it was yesterday and will be tomorrow. His promises do not expire or get revised. We never have to worry that God has changed His mind about us.
elements.questionHint3: Think about a time when someone’s changing moods or broken promises made it hard to trust them — how might God being completely unchanging make a difference to how you relate to Him?

elements.passageText1: <sup>7</sup>Ina zan tafi in tsere wa Ruhunka? <sup>8</sup>Idan na hau cikin samaniya kana can, <sup>9</sup>In na tashi sama, na tafi, na wuce gabas, <sup>10</sup>Kana can domin ka bi da ni,
elements.passageText3: <sup>7</sup>Ina zan tafi daga Ruhunka? <sup>8</sup>In na haura zuwa sammai, kana a can; <sup>9</sup>In na tashi a fikafikan safiya, <sup>10</sup>can ma hannunka zai bishe ni,
elements.passageText5: <sup>7</sup>Where can I go to escape your Spirit? Where can I flee to escape your presence? <sup>8</sup>If I were to ascend to heaven, you would be there. If I were to sprawl out in Sheol, there you would be. <sup>9</sup>If I were to fly away on the wings of the dawn, and settle down on the other side of the ocean, <sup>10</sup>even there your hand would guide me, your right hand would grab hold of me.

elements.question1: Me wannan nassi ke gaya mana game da kasancewar Allah? Shin hakan yana ba ka/ki ta’aziyya ko yana zama ƙalubale a gare ka/ki?
elements.question2: Ɗume ayaure nde’e wolwata dow wonki Allah haa koo moye? Ɗum ɗon weltina ma koo ɗum ɗon torra ma?
elements.question3: What does this passage tell us about God’s presence? Is that a comforting or a challenging thought for you?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan nassi yana gaya mana cewa Allah yana ko’ina — ba wuri a duniya, daga sama zuwa kasan matattu, inda Allah yake rasa. Kasancewarsa ba za a guduwa daga gare shi ba. Ko wannan zai ba da ta’aziyya ko ƙalubale ya dogara ne akan yadda muke ji game da Allah. Ga wanda yake dogara da Allah kuma yana matuƙar bukata da taimakonsa, wannan yana ba da ta’aziyya mai zurfi — komai nisa ko kaɗaici da muke ji, hannun sa har yanzu yana bishenmu. Amma hakan kuma na iya zama ƙalubale ko bayyanawa, domin yana nufin babu abin da ke cikin rayuwarmu da ke ɓoye daga gare shi.
elements.questionHint1: Mai zabura yana lissafin mawuyacin saɓanin abubuwa — sama da ƙasar matattu, kusa da nesa — don yin wata magana game da kasancewar Allah. Wane kammalawa yake tasowa zuwa gare shi, kuma shin sanin cewa Allah yana ko’ina yana jin kamar labari mai daɗi ko gaskiya mara daɗi a gare ka/ki da kanka/kanki?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ndee tonngoode hollita Alla ina won ko’e — goɗɗo fuu nder duuniyaaru, daga ngurleeji faa ɗaɓɓe maayooɓe, to Alla alaa. Wonki muuɗum ina saamaaka. Ko ɗum weltinata koo torrata haani e no mbaɗaten njogii miijo e Alla. Ngam mo hooliima e Alla nde yiɗirta ballal muuɗum, ɗum weltina haa timmii — ko saamuɗen woɗɗere koo kaɗnguure, jungo muuɗum ina jogorii en tawi. Ammaa ɗum kadi mbaawi torugol koo hollugol, ngam hollita walaa ko nder nguurndam meeɗen mo suuɗii e yeeso muuɗum.
elements.questionHint2: Jabuura’en hollita saɓaneeji — ngurleeji e ɗaɓɓe maayooɓe, ɓuri ɓuuɗde e ɓuri woɗɗude — ngam waɗde tiiɗunde e wonki Alla. Hol ko o taƴanta e makko, e to sannde Alla ina won ko’e woni kabaaru moƴƴo koo goongaol tormingaol e ma hoore ma?
elements.sampleAnswer3: This passage tells us that God is omnipresent — there is literally nowhere in the universe, from the heights of heaven to the depths of Sheol, where God is absent. His presence is inescapable. Whether that is comforting or challenging likely depends on how we feel about God. For someone who trusts God and longs for His help, it is deeply comforting — no matter how far or alone we feel, His hand is still guiding us. But it can also feel challenging or exposing, because it means nothing in our lives is hidden from Him.
elements.questionHint3: The psalmist lists extreme opposites — heaven and Sheol, near and far — to make a point about God’s presence. What conclusion is he building towards, and does knowing God is always present feel like good news or an uncomfortable truth to you personally?

elements.passageText1: <sup>8</sup>Wanda ba ya ƙauna, bai san Allah ba sam, domin Allah shi ne ƙauna.
elements.passageText3: <sup>8</sup>Duk wanda ba ya ƙauna, bai san Allah ba, domin Allah ƙauna ne.
elements.passageText4: <sup>8</sup>Fuu mo walaa yidde, o andaa Allah, ngam Allah o yidde.
elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>The person who does not love does not know God, because God is love.

elements.question1: Yohanna ya ce ’Allah shi ne ƙauna’ — ba kawai Allah yana ƙauna ba, sai dai ƙauna ne ke bayyana shi. Me bambancin hakan ke yi?
elements.question2: Yaya wi’i ’Allah woni yidde’ — naa tan Allah ɗon yiɗi, ammaa yidde on laati kanko. Ɗume ɗum waylitata?
elements.question3: John says ’God is love’ — not just that God loves, but that love defines who He is. What difference does that make?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Cewa Allah yana ƙauna yana cewa abin da yake yi ne — aikin da zai iya zaɓa ko watsi da shi. Amma cewa Allah shi ne ƙauna yana nufin ƙauna tana wanzuwa tare da kasancewarsa; ba ya iya daina ƙauna fiye da yadda zai iya daina kasancewa Allah. Yana nufin ƙaunarsa ba amsa ce ga mutumin da ya cancanta a ƙaunace shi ba — tana gudana ne daga wanene yake. Hakan kuma yana nufin ƙauna ba ji ko sha’awa kawai ba ce da Allah yake da ita, sai dai maɓulɓulan kasancewarsa, wanda yana bamu dalili na dogara cewa ƙaunarsa ta dawwama, ba tare da sharadi ba, kuma ba za ta ƙare ba.
elements.questionHint1: Yi la’akari da bambancin cewa ’malami yana da kirki’ da ’malami wani lokaci yana nuna kirki’ — wacce ta fi magana game da ainihin asalinsa, kuma ta yaya wannan bambanci ya shafi abin da Yohanna yake da’awa game da Allah?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Wiyde Allah ɗon yiɗi ko haala ko o waɗata — gollal mo mbaawi suɓugol koo ƴettugol. Ammaa wiyde Allah woni yidde hollita yidde nde wowlaani e wonaare muuɗum; o waawataa heccugol yiddude fay ko nii o wawataaki heccugol wonde Alla. Ɗum hollita yidde muuɗum wanaa jaabawol e men ngonɗen kaɓatooɓe yiddugol — ina ƴiwa daga ko o woni. Ɗum kadi hollita yidde wanaa tan miijo walla miiɗo Alla jogii, ammaa ɓernde yontere muuɗum, ko hokka en gorol hoolagol yidde muuɗum woni newniiɗo, waylitataako, e timmaani.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e saɓaare hakkunde wi’ugol ’jannguɗo o moƴƴi’ e ’jannguɗo o moƴƴinay koo addan’ — hol ko ɓuri yiyde e yontere muuɗum timmunde, e no mbaydi nden nawdata e ko Yaya wi’i e Alla?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Saying God loves is saying something He does — an action He can choose or withhold. But saying God is love means love is inseparable from His very being; He cannot stop being loving any more than He can stop being God. It means His love is not a response to us being lovable — it flows from who He is. This also means that love is not just a feeling God has but the core of His identity, which gives us grounds to trust that His love is permanent, unconditional, and inexhaustible.
elements.questionHint3: Consider the difference between saying ’a teacher is kind’ and ’a teacher sometimes acts kindly’ — which says more about their core identity, and how does that distinction apply to what John is claiming about God?

elements.text1: Tayaya mu ka san cewar Allah yana ƙaunar mu?
elements.text2: Noy mbe’en andirta Allah ɗon yiɗi en?
elements.text3: How do we know that God loves us?

elements.passageText1: <sup>8</sup>Amma kuwa Allah yana tabbatar mana da ƙaunar da yake yi mana, wato tun muna masu zunubi, Almasihu ya mutu dominmu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>8</sup>Amma Allah ya nuna mana ƙaunarsa a wannan. Tun muna masu zunubi tukuna, Kiristi ya mutu dominmu.
elements.passageText4: <sup>8</sup>Ammaa laawol ngol Allah hollii’en yidde maako woni, carel ko ngonɗen nder hakke, Almasiihu maayii ngam meeɗen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>But God demonstrates his own love for us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Bulus ya ce Allah ya nuna ƙaunarsa? Me ke jan hankali game da lokacin — ‘tun muna masu zunubi’?
elements.question2: Noy Bulus wi’i Allah holli yidde muuɗum? Ɗume woni ko kayni dow wakkati man — ‘carel ko ngonɗen nder hakke’?
elements.question3: How does Paul say God has demonstrated His love? What is striking about the timing — ‘while we were still sinners’?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce Allah ya nuna ƙaunarsa ta wurin mutuwar Almasihu a madadin mu. Abin da ke jan hankali ƙwarai shi ne lokacin: Allah bai jira mu gyara rayuwarmu ba, mu sami falalarsa, ko mu zama cancantaccen ƙaunarsa. Almasihu ya mutu dominmu tun muna masu zunubi — har yanzu muna tawaye ga Allah. Hakan yana juya zato na ɗan adam cewa soyayya dole ta cancanta. Ƙaunar Allah ba lada ba ce ga kyakkyawan ɗabi’a; ita ce kyauta ce ta farko da ta ƴanci, wadda aka baiwa waɗanda ba su yi komai ba don su cancanta.
elements.questionHint1: A mafi yawan alaƙar mutum, muna nuna kirki ga waɗanda suka nuna kirki a gare mu — don haka me ke ban mamaki ko wahala fahimta game da lokacin da Bulus ya ce Allah ya zaɓa bayyana ƙaunarsa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi’i Allah hollii yidde muuɗum e nder maayugol Almasiihu ngam meeɗen. Ko kayni haa timmii ko wakkati ngoo: Alla jaaɓaay en mbaɗaten dilli nguurndam meeɗen, keɓen baarka muuɗum, koo mbaɗaten ndaNngataa yiddugol muuɗum. Almasiihu maayii ngam meeɗen tawi ngonɗen nder hakke — ngonɗen tawi e fiyakugol Alla. Ɗum waylitita taaɗiinde aadirini yidde dina haani waɗaneeɗo. Yidde Alla wanaa jaafal kaa gollal moƴƴe; o woni yidde newniiɗo, o fuɗɗii e kanko koo, nde hokki ɓeen ngo’i ko ngaɗii ɗume fuu ngam keɓugol.
elements.questionHint2: E jokkondirali aadee fow, mbaɗaten hollata moƴƴere ɓeen hollitii en moƴƴere — ko ɗume wayi kaa sappungol koo yo’ori e wakkati heɓɓuɗo Bulus wi’i Alla suɓii hollugol yidde muuɗum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says God demonstrated His love through the death of Christ on our behalf. What is especially striking is the timing: God did not wait for us to clean up our lives, earn His favour, or become worthy of His love. Christ died for us while we were still sinners — still in rebellion against God. This overturns the human assumption that love must be deserved. God’s love is not a reward for good behaviour; it is a free, initiating love given to those who had done nothing to merit it.
elements.questionHint3: In most human relationships, we tend to show kindness to people who have been kind to us — so what is surprising or unexpected about the specific moment Paul says God chose to demonstrate His love?

elements.passageText1: <sup>9</sup>Ta haka aka bayyana ƙaunar Allah gare mu, da Allah ya aiko da Ɗansa makaɗaici a duniya, domin mu sami rai madawwami ta wurinsa. <sup>10</sup>Ta haka ƙauna take, wato, ba mu ne muka ƙaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ƙaunace mu, ya aiko Ɗansa, hadayar sulhu saboda a gafarta zunubanmu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>9</sup>Ga yadda Allah ya nuna ƙaunarsa a cikinmu, Allah ya aiki makaɗaici Ɗansa zuwa duniya domin mu rayu ta wurinsa. <sup>10</sup>Wannan ita ce ƙauna, wato, ba mu ne muka ƙaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ƙaunace mu, ya kuma aiko Ɗansa yă zama hadaya ta kafara saboda zunubanmu.
elements.passageText4: <sup>9</sup>Allah hollii’en yidde muuɗum nde o liloyi Ɓiɗɗo maako go’oto tan mo o woodi nder duuniyaaru, ngam keɓen yonki ki re’ataa barka Ɓiɗɗo on. <sup>10</sup>Raa no yidde nde goonga wa’i, naa’ enen ngiɗi Allah, ammaa Allah yiɗi’en o liloyi Ɓiɗɗo maako ngam warta cakkiri yaafaaki hakke meeɗen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>9</sup>By this the love of God is revealed in us: that God has sent his one and only Son into the world so that we may live through him. <sup>10</sup>In this is love: not that we have loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice for our sins.

elements.question1: Bisa ga Yohanna, wane ne ya ɗauki matakin farko a wannan dangantaka — Allah ko mu? Me hakan ke gaya mana game da yanayin ƙaunar Allah?
elements.question2: Dow ko Yaya wi’i, moy fuɗɗi yidde nde’e — Allah koo enen? Ɗume ɗum hollata en dow no yidde Allah wa’i?
elements.question3: According to John, who took the initiative in this relationship — God or us? What does that tell us about the nature of God’s love?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna yana bayyana a sarari cewa Allah ne ya ɗauki matakin farko — ƙauna an bayyana ta ne ba a matsayin ƙaunarmu ga Allah ba, sai dai ƙaunarsa a gare mu. Allah ya zo da farko. Ya aika da Ɗansa kafin mu neme shi, domin yin hulɗa da zunubin da ya raba mu da shi. Hakan yana gaya mana cewa ƙaunar Allah ba ta amsa kira ba kuma ba tare da sharadi ba — ita ce ƙauna ta asali. Ba mu jawo ƙaunar Allah a gare mu ta wurin imani da himmarmu ba; ya ƙaunace mu kyauta kuma ya aiki akan wannan ƙauna tare da babbar farashin da ya biya, ta aike da Ɗansa makaɗaici hadaya.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya ce ’ba mu ne muka ƙaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ƙaunace mu’ — wane ne ya ce ya yi matakin farko, kuma me bambancin da hakan zai yi a gare ka/ki da kanka/kanki sanin cewa dangantakar ta fara ne da Allah, ba da kai/ke ba?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya hollita tiiɗuɗo Alla fuɗɗii — yidde nde hollitaama ɗoo wanaa yidde meeɗen e Alla, ammaa yidde muuɗum e men. Alla fuɗɗii. O lilii Ɓiɗɗo maako ɓe mbaɗen ndaɗɗii e makko, ngam waɗde laawol hakke ngo waylitiri men e makko. Ɗum anndina en yidde Alla wanaa jaabawol koo waylitirteeɗo — ɗum woni yidde fuɗɗiɗo e ƴettaaki sariya. En ngaNnditaay yidde Alla e men e nder hoolagol meeɗen; o yiɗi’en newniiɗo o gollorii e ndee yidde e nder nafa mawnii, lilugol Ɓiɗɗo maako ceerngal ko cakkiri.
elements.questionHint2: Yaya wi’i ’naa’ enen ngiɗi Allah, ammaa o yiɗi’en’ — hol ko o wi’i fuɗɗii, e no waylituɗo ko waɗata e ma hoore ma sande andugol jokkondiral ngol fuɗɗii e Alla, hoto e maa?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John is explicit that God took the initiative — love is defined here not as our love for God, but as His love for us. God moved first. He sent His Son before we sought Him, to deal with the sin that separated us from Him. This tells us that God’s love is not reactive or conditional — it is originating and unconditional. We do not draw God’s love out of Him by our devotion; He loved us freely and acted on that love at great cost, sending His only Son as a sacrifice.
elements.questionHint3: John says ’not that we have loved God, but that he loved us’ — who does he say made the first move, and what practical difference might it make to you personally to know that the relationship started with God, not with you?

elements.text1: Gaskiya al’amari
elements.text2: Goongaaji mawɗi
elements.text3: Key truths

elements.text1:
Wauta/Wawanci ne a yi ma tunanin cewar babu Allah

’Wawa ya ce a zuciyar sa, ‘Ba Allah.’’

Zabura 14:1a

Allah ya ƙaunar mu kuma akwai buƙatar mu ma kula da lura da ƙaunarsa

’Duba irin gagarumar ƙaunar da Uba ya nuna mana, da cewar a kira mu ƴaƴan Allah! Wanda hakan kuma muke!’

1 Yahaya 3:1a
elements.text2:
Ɗum kaayeefi neɗɗo miijoo walaa Allah.

’Kaayeejo wi’i nder ɓernde muuɗum, ‘Walaa Allah.’’ — Jabuura 14:1a

Allah ɗon yiɗi en, o ɗon yiɗi kadi mbe’en njaabo yidde maako.

’Ndaaree no yidde mawnde Baaba hokki en, haa mbe’en njetee ɓiɓɓe Allah! Kanjum kadi mbe’en laati!’ — 1 Yaya 3:1a
elements.text3:
It is foolish to think that there is no God.

’The fool says in his heart, ‘There is no God.’’ — Psalm 14:1a

God loves us and longs that we might respond to His love.

’See what great love the Father has lavished on us, that we should be called children of God! And that is what we are!’ — 1 John 3:1a

elements.text1: Abubuwa tunani akai
elements.text2: Ko haani miijaago dow muuɗum
elements.text3: To think about

elements.question1: Tayaya za ka/ki iya kokarin gamsar da tabbatar wa ɗan’uwa ko aboki akwai Allah?
elements.question2: Noy mbaɗata ngam njaɓina soobaajo ma wonde Allah ɗon woodi goonga?
elements.question3: How would you try to convince a friend that God does actually exist?

elements.question1: Idan aka tambaya, ‘Yaya kamarnin Allah yake?’ Waɗanne abubuwa ko halaye zaka/ki ambata?
elements.question2: To a yamaama, ‘Noy Allah wa’i?’, ɗume njetata dow sifaaji maako?
elements.question3: If asked the question, ‘What is God like?’, what would be the main characteristics you would mention?

chapters.chapterTitle3: Jesus Christ, the Son of God

elements.text1: Akwai banbanci ga yarda wasu suka cewar, Wai Yesu ba tun fil’azal yake ba amma ya kasance ne kawai lokacin da Maryamu ta haife shi a Baitalami. Yesu yana nan tun kafin farkon komai.
elements.text2: Heewɓe ɗon miijoo Yeesu kanko woni moodibbo mawɗo malla neɗɗo koƴo mo wuuri duuɓi ujunnaaje ɗiɗi nane. Ammaa Linjiila ɗon holla en neɗɗo ɓuri ɗum mawnugo. Haayeefi, Yeesu fuɗɗaaki haa Betalami — o ɗon woodi gila nane. Nder janngde nde’e en ɗon ndaara moy woni Yeesu goonga.
elements.text3: Many people think of Jesus as a great teacher or a good man who lived two thousand years ago. But the Bible presents Someone far greater. Surprisingly, Jesus did not begin at Bethlehem — He has always existed. In this study we look at who Jesus really is.

elements.text1: Me muka sani game da Yesu kafin zuwansa duniya?
elements.text2: Ɗume en anndi dow Yeesu ko o wara duniyaaru?
elements.text3: What do we know about Jesus before He came to earth?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Tun fil’azal akwai Kalma, Kalman nan kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman nan kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne tun fil’azal yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Dukan abubuwa sun kasance ta gare shi ne, ba kuma abin da ya kasance na abubuwan da suka kasance, sai ta game da shi.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>Tun farar farawa akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa yana nan tare da Allah, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Yana nan tare da Allah tun farar farawa. <sup>3</sup>Ta wurin Kalman ne aka halicci dukan abubuwa. In ba tare da shi ba, babu abin da aka yi wanda aka yi.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi’eteeɗo Wolde e ɗon, e mo wondi e Allah, nden o Allah. <sup>2</sup>Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi’eteeɗo Wolde on e wondi e Allah. <sup>3</sup>O tagii koo ɗume. Walaa goɗɗum nder fuu ko tagaa ɗum um tagiraayi laawol maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God. <sup>2</sup>The Word was with God in the beginning. <sup>3</sup>All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.
elements.note: In this passage the ’Word’ is referring to Jesus.

elements.question1: Yohanna yana kiran Yesu ’Kalma’. Me wannan nassi ke gaya mana game da inda Yesu yake kafin zuwan sa duniya?
elements.question2: Yaya ɗon nodda Yeesu ’Wolde’. Ɗume aayaaje ɗee ɗon kaala dow to Yeesu wonnoo ko o wara duniyaaru?
elements.question3: John calls Jesus ’the Word’. What does this passage tell us about where Jesus was before He came to earth?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Kafin zuwan sa duniya, Yesu — Kalma — ya riga ya wanzu. Yana tare da Allah tun fil’azal, ma’ana koyaushe yana cikin kusancin dangantaka da Uba. Fiye da haka, shi kansa Allah ne gaba ɗaya. Yohanna kuma yana gaya mana cewa dukan abubuwa an halicci su ta wurinsa, ma’ana Yesu ba halitta ba ne da ya bayyana a wani lokaci a tarihi, sai dai Mahalicci na har abada wanda ya kasance kafin komai ya wanzu.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya ce Kalma yana nan ’tun fil’azal’ — shin wannan yana nuna cewa Yesu yana da farko, ko yana nuna cewa ya riga ya kasance kafin komai ya fara? Me kuma Yohanna ya ce game da ransa kafin halitta?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ko o wara duniyaaru, Yeesu — Wolde on — e ɗon woodi gila. O wondi e Allah daga ko duuniyaaru tagee, ko hollita o wonnoo gila e nder jokkondiral ɓuri ɓuuɗde e Baaba on. Ɓuri ɗum, o woni Allah kanko tigi haa timmi. Yaya kadi hollita koo ɗume tagaa e laawol maako, ko hollita Yeesu wanaa ko tagaa o yiitii e saha goɗɗo nder tariik, ammaa o woni Tagɗo mo wonnoo daga ko ada ko tagaa woni fuu.
elements.questionHint2: Yaya wi’i Wolde on wonnoo ’daga ko duuniyaaru tagee’ — ɗum hollita Yeesu fuɗɗiti, koo o ɗon woodi ɓe ada ko koo ɗume fuɗɗiti? Ɗume kadi Yaya wi’i dow kuugal maako ko ada ko tageefo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Before coming to earth, Jesus — the Word — already existed. He was with God in the beginning, meaning He has always been in close relationship with the Father. More than that, He was fully God Himself. John also tells us that all things were created through Him, meaning Jesus was not a created being who appeared at some point in history, but the eternal Creator who was present before anything else existed.
elements.questionHint3: John says the Word was ’in the beginning’ — does that suggest Jesus had a starting point, or that He was already there before anything began? What else does John say about His role before creation?

elements.passageText1: <sup>15</sup>Shi ne surar Allah marar ganuwa, magāji ne tun ba a halicci kome ba, <sup>16</sup>don ta gare shi ne aka halicci dukkan kome, abubuwan da suke a sama da abubuwan da suke a ƙasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa, ko kursiyai, ko masu mulki, ko masarauta, ko masu iko, wato, dukkan abubuwa an halicce su ne ta wurinsa, shi ne kuma makomarsu. <sup>17</sup>Shi ne a gaba da dukkan abubuwa, dukkan abubuwa kuma shi ne yake riƙe da su.
elements.passageText3: <sup>15</sup>Kiristi shi ne surar da ake gani na Allah wanda ba a iya gani, ɗan fari a kan dukan halitta. <sup>16</sup>Ta wurinsa aka halicci dukan abu, abubuwan da suke cikin sama da abubuwan da suke a duniya, waɗanda ake gani, da waɗanda ba a gani, ko kursiyoyi ko ikoki ko masu mulki ko hukumomi; an halicci dukan abu ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa ne. <sup>17</sup>Ya kasance kafin dukan abu, kuma a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harhaɗe.
elements.passageText4: <sup>15</sup>Ngikku Almasiihu mo ngi’eten ɗum go’o e ngikku Allah mo yi’ataake. E mo ɗon ko Allah taga koo ɗume, o mawɗo dow koo ɗume ko tagaa. <sup>16</sup>Barka maako Allah tagi koo ɗume gonɗum dow e ley, ko yi’etee e ko yi’ataake, hawti e malaa’ika’en, e ginni, e koo ɗume gooduɗum laamu e bawɗe. Koo ɗume kanko tagiɗum, nden ngam maako koo ɗume tagaa. <sup>17</sup>Almasiihu e ɗon ko koo ɗume tagee, barka maako koo ɗume e yaha yeeso.
elements.passageText5: <sup>15</sup>He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation. <sup>16</sup>For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities—all things were created through him and for him. <sup>17</sup>And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.

elements.question1: Me Bulus ya ce game da rawar da Yesu ya taka a halitta? Me ayar 17 ke nuna game da dangantakarsa da duniya a yau?
elements.question2: Ɗume Bulus wi’i dow kuugal Yeesu nder tageefo? Ɗume aaya 17 ɗon holla dow no o wonndi e tageefo hannde kadi?
elements.question3: What does Paul say Jesus’ role was in creation? What does verse 17 suggest about His ongoing relationship to the universe?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce dukan abubuwa — a sama da a ƙasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa — an halicci su ta wurin Yesu, kuma dominsa. Ransa a halitta ya cika: babu abin da ya wanzu sai ta game da shi. Ayar 17 ta kai ma’ana ta fi nisa, ta ce a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harhaɗe. Wannan yana nufin Yesu ba kawai Mahalicci ba ne, sai dai shi ne mai riƙe da halitta har yanzu — duniya tana ci gaba da wanzuwa da tsari saboda shi. Ba mai nisa ba ne da ya fara abubuwa ya kuma tafi; yana da hannu cikin riƙe dukan abubuwa a wannan lokaci.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya yi amfani da laakari uku a ayar 16 — dukan abubuwa an halicci su ’ta’, ‘ta wurin’, da kuma ’domin’ Yesu. Me wannan haɗin uku ke nuna game da faɗin hannu nsa a halitta, kuma ta yaya ayar 17 ke tsawaita wannan ra’ayin zuwa yanzu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi’i koo ɗume — gonɗum dow e ley, ko yi’etee e ko yi’ataake — tagaa e Yeesu, e nder maako, e ngam maako. Kuugal maako nder tageefo wonnii haa timmi: walaa ko woni fuu ko tagiraayi laawol maako. Aaya 17 yaaɓi dow non, wi’i nder maako koo ɗume e yaha yeeso. Ɗum hollita Yeesu wanaa tan Tagɗo, ammaa kanko woni kadi mo riworaa tageefo hannde — duuniyaaru e yaade yeeso e nder harɗondiral ngam maako. O wanaa neɗɗo woɗɗo fuɗɗii ko woodi o yaɓɓi; o riworaa koo ɗume e nder wakkati ngoo goongo.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus huutorii laaɓi tati nder aaya 16 — koo ɗume tagaa ’e’, ‘e nder’, e ’ngam’ Yeesu. Ɗume naatgol ɗi tati ɗi jokkondirɗi hollita e no o naatoyi nder tageefo, e noy aaya 17 yuɓɓitata miijo ngoo haa hannde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says that all things — in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible — were created by Jesus, through Jesus, and for Jesus. His role in creation was total: nothing came into existence apart from Him. Verse 17 then goes further, saying that in Him all things ’hold together’. This means Jesus is not just the originator of creation but its ongoing sustainer — the universe continues to exist and cohere because of Him. He is not a distant figure who started things and stepped away; He is actively involved in holding everything together at this very moment.
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses three prepositions in verse 16 — all things were created ’by’, ‘through’, and ’for’ Jesus. What does adding those three together suggest about the scope of His involvement in creation, and how does verse 17 extend that idea into the present?

elements.text1: Don me aka kira shi Yesu?
elements.text2: Ngam ɗume o noddiree Yeesu?
elements.text3: Why was He called Jesus?

elements.passageText1: <sup>21</sup># Luk 1.31  Za ta haifi ɗa, za ka kuma sa masa suna Yesu, domin shi ne zai ceci mutanensa daga zunubansu."
elements.passageText3: <sup>21</sup>Za tă haifi ɗa, za ka kuma sa masa suna Yesu, # 1.21  Yesu  da Girik shi ne  Yoshuwa da Ibraniyanci,  wanda yake nufin  Ubangiji  yana ceto.  gama shi ne zai ceci mutanensa daga zunubansu."
elements.passageText4: <sup>21</sup>O danyay ɓiɗɗo gorko nden a noddaymo Yeesu, ngam o hisnay yimɓe maako daga hakke maɓɓe."
elements.passageText5: <sup>21</sup>She will give birth to a son and you will name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.

elements.question1: Me sunan ’Yesu’ ke nufi, kuma me hakan ke gaya mana game da manufar zuwansa?
elements.question2: Ɗume innde ’Yeesu’ nufata, nden ɗume ɗum ɗon holla en dow ko waɗi o wari?
elements.question3: What does the name ’Jesus’ mean, and what does that tell us about the purpose of His coming?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Mala’ika ya bayyana ma’anar sunan kai tsaye: Yesu yana nufin ’zai ceci mutanensa daga zunubansu’. Sunan kansa sanarwar aikin da zai yi ne. Yesu bai zo da farko a matsayin mai ’yantar da siyasa, ko malami na ɗabi’a, ko mai yin mu’ujizai ba — ya zo ne don magance matsalar zunubi. Sunansa yana gaya mana cewa cetonsa daga zunubi shi ne tsakiyar manufar rayuwarsa, kuma an tsara wannan ceto an kuma sanar da shi kafin a haife shi.
elements.questionHint1: Mala’ika ya bayyana dalilin sunan tare da kalmar ’domin’ — wane matsala ta musamman ya ce Yesu zai zo don magance ta, kuma shin hakan yana ban mamaki ga ka/ki idan ka/ki yi la’akari da abin da mutane na iya tsammani mai ceto ya zo da yi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Mala’ika hollitii maana innde nde tiiɗuɗo: Yeesu nufata ’o hisnay yimɓe maako daga hakke maɓɓe’. Innde maako hoore mum woni tintinol kuugal maako. Yeesu waraa tan ko libiroowo siyaas, ko moodibbo laawol moralnaawol, koo ko waɗoowo hayɗi — o warii ngam waɗde laawol e hakke. Innde maako hollita en hisnugol daga hakke ko faandaare nder nguurndam maako, e ngal faandaare nde taƴanaa, e nde waajowaa ɓe ada ko o danyanaa.
elements.questionHint2: Mala’ika on holliti daliil innde nde e wolde ’ngam’ — ko woni caɗeele heɓɓunde o wi’i Yeesu wari ngam waɗde laawol e maɗɗe, e ɗum hayɗina ma ko ɓe ndaari yimɓe ɓen keɓugol ko hisnooɗo waɗata?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The angel explains the name directly: Jesus means ’he will save his people from their sins’. The name itself is a mission statement. Jesus did not come primarily as a political liberator, a moral teacher, or a miracle worker — He came to deal with the problem of sin. His name tells us that rescue from sin is the central purpose of His life, and that this rescue was planned and announced before He was even born.
elements.questionHint3: The angel gives a reason for the name with the word ’for’ — what specific problem does He say Jesus will come to solve, and does that surprise you given what people might have expected a Saviour to do?

elements.text1: Tayaya muka san gaskiyar cewa Yesu cikkaken mutum ne?
elements.text2: Noy en anndata wonde Yeesu kanko woni neɗɗo goonga?
elements.text3: How do we know that Jesus is truly human?

elements.passageText1: <sup>7</sup>Sai ta haifi ɗanta na fari, ta rufi shi da zanen goyo, ta kuma kwantar da shi a wani komi dabbobi, don ba su sami ɗaki a masaukin ba. <sup>40</sup>Ɗan yaron kuwa ya girma, ya kawo ƙarfi, yana mai matuƙar hikima. Alherin Allah kuwa yana tare da shi.
elements.passageText3: <sup>7</sup>ta kuwa haifi ɗanta na fari. Ta nannaɗe shi a zane ta kuma kwantar da shi cikin wani kwami, don ba su sami ɗaki a masauƙi ba. <sup>40</sup>Yaron kuwa ya yi girma ya yi ƙarfi; ya cika da hikima, alherin Allah kuwa yana bisansa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>7</sup>Sey o danyi ɓiɗɗo gorko, afo maako, o saawimo nder wudere wooɗunde, o fukkinimo nder to ɗum waɗanta marle nyamndu, ngam walaa njayri nder suudu hoɓɓe. <sup>40</sup>Yeesu mawni, sembiɗi, faamu heewimo, nden barka Allah e dow maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>7</sup>And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in strips of cloth and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the village inn. ... <sup>40</sup>The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom, and the favor of God was upon him.

elements.question1: Waɗanne bayanai a cikin waɗannan ayoyi ke nuna cewa Yesu ya fuskanci yarinta na ainihin ɗan adam?
elements.question2: Ɗume aayaaje ɗee ɗon holla wonde Yeesu mawniri no ɓikkon neɗɗo mawnirta goonga?
elements.question3: What details in these verses show that Jesus experienced a genuinely human childhood?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Ayar 7 tana nuna cewa an haifi Yesu ta hanyar jiki ta yau da kullum — an rufe shi da zane kamar kowace jariri kuma an kwantar da shi a kwamin dabbobi. Ya shiga duniya cikin rauni da talauci, ba cikin ɗaukakar sama ba. Ayar 40 kuma tana gaya mana ya girma ya kawo ƙarfi, kuma ya cika da hikima a cikin lokaci. Wannan tsarin yaron ɗan adam ne na yau da kullum: ci gaba, girma, da balagar hankali a hankali. Yesu bai haifa yana cikakke ko ya san dukan abubuwa a cikin gogewar sa ta mutum ba — ya girma kamar yadda muke girma.
elements.questionHint1: Ayar 40 ta ce Yesu ya ’girma’ ya kuma ’kawo ƙarfi’ — shin waɗannan kalmomi suna nuna wani abu da ya faru a lokaci ɗaya ko a hankali a tsawon lokaci, kuma me hakan ke gaya maka game da ko yarinyarsa ta yi kama da tamu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Aaya 7 hollita Yeesu danyanaa e laawol ɓandu goonga — o saawanaa nder wudere ko nii ɓii jananɗo koo gooto e o fukkaa nder to ɗum waɗanta marle nyamndu. O natti nder duuniyaaru e tampere e yimirɗo, hoto nder teddungal ngurleeji. Aaya 40 nden hollita o mawni o sembiɗi, e faamu heewii e makko e nder wakkati. Ɗum woni mbaydi ɓii neɗɗo goonga: yuɓɓere, mawningol, e mawnugol haa timmi e nder sahaa. Yeesu danyanaa wanaa timmiɗo koo andiraaɗo koo ɗume e nder golle muuɗum ko neɗɗo — o mawni ko nii mbaɗaten mawnirta.
elements.questionHint2: Aaya 40 wi’i Yeesu ’mawni’ e ’sembiɗi’ — ɗee kelmeeji hollita ko waɗi hannde tan koo ko waɗi e nder sahaa, e ɗume ɗum anndinta ma e to yarinyaaru maako wonndi e meeɗen?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Verse 7 shows that Jesus was born in the ordinary, physical way — wrapped in cloth like any newborn infant and laid in a manger. He entered the world in vulnerability and poverty, not in heavenly splendour. Verse 40 then tells us He grew and became strong, and was filled with wisdom over time. This is the pattern of a normal human childhood: development, growth, and gradual maturation. Jesus was not born fully formed or all-knowing in His human experience — He grew up just as we do.
elements.questionHint3: Verse 40 says Jesus ’grew’ and ’became strong’ — do those words suggest something that happened all at once or over time, and what does that tell you about whether His childhood was similar to ours?

elements.passageText1: <sup>2</sup>har kwana arba’in, Iblis yana gwada shi. A kwanakin nan bai ci kome ba. Da suka ƙare kuwa ya ji yunwa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>2</sup>inda kwana arba’in, Iblis ya gwada shi. Bai ci kome ba a cikin kwanakin nan, a ƙarshensu kuwa ya ji yunwa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>2</sup>Ton o waɗi balɗe 40 Ceyɗan e foondamo, o nyaamaayi goɗɗum nder balɗe ɗen. Nden gaɗa maaje, weelo nangimo.
elements.passageText5: Jesus was led by the Spirit in the wilderness, 2where for forty days he endured temptations from the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and when they were completed, he was famished.

elements.question1: Me ke nunar da cewa Yesu ’ya ji yunwa’? Ta yaya hakan ke tabbatar da cewa ɗan adam ne?
elements.question2: Ɗume ɗum nufata wonde Yeesu ’weelo nangimo’? Noy ɗum ɗon tabbintina wonde kanko woni neɗɗo?
elements.question3: What does it mean that Jesus ’was famished’? How does this confirm His humanity?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Cewa Yesu ’ya ji yunwa’ yana nufin ya fuskanci ainihin yunwar jiki mai tsanani — irin wadda ke zuwa bayan kwana arba’in ba tare da abinci ba. Ba misali ba ne; gaskiyar jiki ce. Mai iko na Allah ba zai ji yunwar jiki ba, amma Yesu ya ji. Hakan yana tabbatar da cewa jikin sa na mutum na ainihi ne kuma yana ƙarƙashin iyakokin jiki iri ɗaya da namu: yana buƙatar abinci, ya ji zafin rashinsa, kuma jikinsa ya raunana saboda gogewar. Ɗan adamtakarsa ba sutura ba ce — na ainihi ne.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da yadda yunwa take ji bayan rasa abinci na cin abinci ɗaya kawai — me fuskantar ta bayan kwana arba’in ke gaya maka game da ko Yesu yana da ainihin jikin ɗan adam mai buƙatun jiki na gaskiya?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Wonde Yeesu ’weelo nangimo’ hollita o nanngi weelo tigi ɓandu — no nangirta gaɗa balɗe 40 o nyaamaayi goɗɗum. Ɗum wanaa kisal; ko goongawal ɓandu. Alla tan wanaa ɓandu waawataa nangugol weelo ɓandu, ammaa Yeesu nangi. Ɗum tabbintina ɓandu muuɗum neɗɗo goonga e ɓandu ngo feeɓtonoo e ko ngo hiɓi no mbaɗaten e meeɗen nii: o haajii nyaamdu, o nanngi caɗeele walugol e makko, e ɓandu maako tampii e golle ɗen. Neɗɗam muuɗum wanaa wudere o saawii — ko goonga.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e no weelo wa’i gaɗa ɗaɓɓitugol nyaamdu hannde gooto tan — ɗume nangugol ɗum gaɗa balɗe 40 anndinta ma e to Yeesu ina jogii ɓandu neɗɗo goonga e haajuuji ɓandu goonga?
elements.sampleAnswer3: That Jesus ’was famished’ means He experienced real, intense physical hunger — the kind that comes from forty days without food. This is not a metaphor; it is a physiological reality. A purely divine being would not experience bodily hunger, but Jesus did. This confirms that His human body was genuine and subject to the same physical limitations as ours: He needed food, He felt the pain of going without it, and His body was weakened by the experience. His humanity was not a costume — it was real.
elements.questionHint3: Think about what hunger feels like after missing just one meal — what does experiencing it after forty days tell you about whether Jesus had a real human body with real physical needs?

elements.passageText1: <sup>23</sup>Da ya shiga jirgi, almajiransa suka bi shi. <sup>24</sup>Sai ga wani babban hadiri ya taso a teku, har raƙuman ruwa suka fara shan kan jirgin, amma yana barci.
elements.passageText3: <sup>23</sup>Sai ya shiga jirgin ruwa, almajiransa kuwa suka bi shi. <sup>24</sup>Ba zato ba tsammani, sai wani babban hadari ya taso a tafkin, har raƙuman ruwan suka sha kan jirgin. Amma Yesu yana barci.
elements.passageText4: <sup>23</sup>Yeesu natti komiwal, tokkuɓemo maa tokkimo. <sup>24</sup>Sey hendu cembindu ummoyii dow mbeela ngan, geelooɗi ndiyam puɗɗi piiki komiwal ngal, ndiyam natti nder maagal haa naaki ngal ɓadii heewa ndiyam, ammaa Yeesu e nder maagal e ɗaanii.
elements.passageText5: <sup>23</sup>As he got into the boat, his disciples followed him. <sup>24</sup>And suddenly a great storm developed on the sea, so that the waves began to swamp the boat; but he was asleep.

elements.question1: Me ke da muhimmanci ga ɗan adam game da bayanan cewa Yesu yana barci cikin hadari?
elements.question2: Ɗume mawninkaaku neɗɗum holli nder fii Yeesu o ɗaanii nder henndu mbeela mawndu?
elements.question3: What is humanly significant about the detail that Jesus was asleep during the storm?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Barci ɗaya daga cikin muhimman alamomin kasancewa ɗan adam ne — yana nuna gajiyawar jiki da buƙatar jiki na hutawa. Gaskiyar cewa Yesu yana barci a cikin jirgin, ko a cikin babban hadari mai tsanani, yana gaya mana ya yi aiki sosai har ya gaji da ainihi. Bai yi kamar yana gaji ba; yana buƙatar hutawa da gaske. Wannan ƙaramin bayani a cikin ƙarfi amma a hankali yana tabbatar da cewa ɗan adamtakarsa ta ainihi ce: ya gaji kamar yadda muke gaji.
elements.questionHint1: Don me ɗan adam ya buƙaci barci kwata-kwata, kuma me buƙatar barci ke gaya maka game da irin jikin da mutum yake da shi — shin wanda ba ya taɓa gajiya ta ainihi zai iya faɗuwa cikin barci mai zurfi cikin hadari mai haɗari?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ɗaaningol woni goɗɗo nder maandeeji mawɗi ɓurtiniiɗi neɗɗam — o hollita tampere ɓandu e haaju ɓandu e noppi. Goongawal wonde Yeesu ɗaanii nder komiwal, haa e nder iyeende mawnde, hollita o golliino haa timmi o tampii goonga. O waɗaayii hayka tampii; o haajii noppi goonga. Ngal bayesal famɗungal e nder jom weli e tiiɗi e tabbintina neɗɗam maako goonga: o tampoyii ko nii mbaɗaten tampirta.
elements.questionHint2: Ngam ɗume aadee haajii ɗaaningol, e ɗume haaju ɗaaningol anndinta ma e ko woni ɓandu neɗɗo — mbaaɗaa mo tampataako goonga waawugol ɗaaningol e nder iyeende caɗende?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Sleep is one of the most fundamental marks of being human — it reflects physical tiredness and the body’s need for rest. The fact that Jesus was asleep in the boat, even in the middle of a violent storm, tells us He had been working hard enough to be genuinely exhausted. He was not pretending to be tired; He actually needed rest. This small detail quietly but powerfully confirms that His humanity was authentic: He got tired just as we do.
elements.questionHint3: Why do human beings need to sleep at all, and what does needing sleep tell you about the kind of body a person has — could someone who never truly got tired fall into a deep sleep during a dangerous storm?

elements.passageText1: <sup>32</sup>Sai soja suka zo suka karya ƙafafun na farkon nan, da kuma na ɗayan da aka gicciye tare da shi. <sup>33</sup>Amma da suka zo wurin Yesu, suka kuma ga ya riga ya mutu, ba su karya ƙafafunsa ba.
elements.passageText3: <sup>32</sup>Sai sojoji suka zo suka karya ƙafafun mutum na farko da aka gicciye tare da Yesu, da kuma na ɗayan. <sup>33</sup>Amma sa’ad da suka zo kan Yesu suka tarar ya riga ya mutu, ba su karya ƙafafunsa ba.
elements.passageText4: <sup>32</sup>Ngam non sooji’en ɓen njahi to gaafaale ɗen. Ɓe ngewi koyɗe arandeejo, nden ɓe ngewi koyɗe ɗiɗaɓo on. <sup>33</sup>Ammaa nde ɓe ngari to Yeesu ɓe ngi’i o yaadake o maayii, ɓe ngewaayi koyɗe maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>32</sup>So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who had been crucified with Jesus, first the one and then the other. <sup>33</sup>But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.

elements.question1: Me ya sa da muhimmanci cewa Yesu ya mutu a gaskiya a kan gicciye? Me Yohanna yake son mu fahimta a nan?
elements.question2: Ngam ɗume ɗum mawni goonga wonde Yeesu maayii dow gaafaangal? Ɗume Yaya yiɗi en paama ɗon?
elements.question3: Why is it significant that Jesus actually died on the cross? What does John want us to understand here?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna ya rubuta cewa sojoji — ƙwararru wanda aikinsu shi ne tabbatar da mutuwa — sun tabbatar da cewa Yesu ya riga ya mutu. Ba su da wata dalilin kara masa rai kuma suna da dalilin tabbatar da mutuwarsa. Gaskiyar cewa ya mutu da gaske yana da muhimmanci a matakai biyu. Da farko, yana tabbatar da cikakken ɗan adamtakarsa: mai iko na Allah da ya bayyana kawai kamar ɗan adam ba zai iya mutuwa ta gaskiya ba. Na biyu, yana tabbatar da cewa tashin matattu ya kasance tashin matattu na gaskiya daga mutuwa ta ainihi, ba farfadowa daga rashin sani ba. Yohanna yana ba da shaida ta ido cewa mutuwar ta ainihi ce, domin mai karatu ya iya dogara da cewa tashin matattu ya kasance na ainihi haka.
elements.questionHint1: Sojoji sun karya ƙafafun waɗanda suka gicciye su don hanzarta mutuwarsu — don haka me ya sa da muhimmanci cewa ba su buƙatar yin hakan ga Yesu, kuma me suka tabbatar ta hanyar barin shi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya windii sooji’en ɓen — ɓe golliiɓe joomi kuugal maɓɓe ko tabbintugol maayde — tabbintii wonde Yeesu yaadake o maayii. Walaa sabaabu e maɓɓe ngam yoɓugol mo e woodi sabaabu fow ngam tabbintugol. Goongawal wonde o maayii ko mawni e laabi ɗiɗi. Aranɗe, o tabbintina neɗɗam muuɗum haa timmi: Alla tan wanaa ɓandu mo baylanaa ko nii neɗɗo waawataa maayde goonga. Ɗiɗaɓe, o tabbintina umminaawal on wonnoo umminaawal goonga daga maayde goonga, hoto fayde daga ɓurtundiral. Yaya hokkata seede neɗɗo yi’uɗo wonde maayde nde goonga, ngam joolooɗo on waawa hoolugo umminaawal kadi ko goonga.
elements.questionHint2: Sooji’en ɓen ngewi koyɗe woɓɓe ngam hanyugol maayde maɓɓe — ngam ɗume non ɗum mawni wonde ɓe ngewaayi koyɗe Yeesu, e ɗume ɓe tabbintoyii en goonga nde ɓe yeƴƴiri mo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John records that the soldiers — professionals whose job was to ensure death — confirmed that Jesus was already dead. They had no reason to spare Him and every reason to make sure. The fact that He was genuinely dead is significant on two levels. First, it confirms His full humanity: a divine being who merely appeared human could not truly die. Second, it establishes that the resurrection was a real resurrection from actual death, not a recovery from unconsciousness. John is providing eyewitness evidence that the death was real, so that the reader can trust that the resurrection was equally real.
elements.questionHint3: The soldiers broke the legs of the others to speed their deaths — so why is it significant that they did not need to do this to Jesus, and what were they effectively confirming by leaving Him alone?

elements.text1: Tayaya muka san gaskiyar Yesu Allah ne?
elements.text2: Noy en anndata wonde Yeesu kanko woni Allah goonga?
elements.text3: How do we know that Jesus is truly God?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Tun fil’azal akwai Kalma, Kalman nan kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman nan kuwa Allah ne.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>Tun farar farawa akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa yana nan tare da Allah, Kalman kuwa Allah ne.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi’eteeɗo Wolde e ɗon, e mo wondi e Allah, nden o Allah.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God.

elements.question1: Yohanna ya ce Kalma ’Allah ne’. Ta yaya wannan ayar ke zama harsashi don fahimtar wanene Yesu?
elements.question2: Yaya wi’i Wolde nden ’woni Allah kanko tigi’. Noy aayaare nde’e wallata en faamugo moy woni Yeesu?
elements.question3: John says the Word ’was fully God’. How does this verse set the foundation for understanding who Jesus is?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna ya buɗe Linjila sa tare da haɗin kai da farkon littafin Farawa — ‘tun fil’azal’ — kuma ya sanya Yesu (Kalma) a can nan da nan, kafin wani abu ya wanzu. Sa’an nan ya yi jerin da’awa guda biyu a numfashi ɗaya: Kalma yana tare da Allah (yana nuna dangantaka ta mutum ta daban) kuma Kalma Allah ne gaba ɗaya (yana nuna yanayi na Allah da aka raba). Wannan ayar tana kafa daga farko cewa ba za a fahimci Yesu a matsayin babban mutum kawai ko wata ƙananan halitta ta Allah ba — shi Allah ne gaba ɗaya, kuma sauran Linjila ta Yohanna an rubuta ta don nuna me hakan ke nufi.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya ce Kalma yana nan tare da Allah kuma yana nan shi ne Allah — shin waɗannan da’awa biyu suna saɓo da juna, ko za su iya tare nuna wani abu game da Yesu da ya fi sauƙi ko ma’ana biyu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya yuɓɓitii Linjiila maako e faayre nde nandi e Farayse — ‘daga ko duuniyaaru tagee’ — nden e ɗon ɓaddi Yeesu (Wolde on), ada ko koo ɗume woni fuu. O wi’ii nden miijooji ɗiɗi e numfaari gooto: Wolde on wondi e Allah (hollita jokkondiral neɗɗu ceerngal) e Wolde on woni Allah kanko tigi (hollita yontere Allah njogiraa nder maako). Ndee aayaare nde tabbinta daga fuɗɗorde wonde Yeesu waawataa faamugo tan ko neɗɗo mawɗo koo ko goɗɗo Allah ɓurɗo famɗugo — o Allah haa timmi, e Linjiila Yaya fow windaama ngam hollugol ɗume ɗum hollita.
elements.questionHint2: Yaya wi’i Wolde on wondi e Allah e woni Allah kanko tigi — ɗee da’aaje ɗiɗi ɗi njottitaani, koo mbaawi jokkondirde ngam hollugol ko fayti e Yeesu nde ɓuri hewaango to naa’ gooto koo goɗɗo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John opens his Gospel with a deliberate echo of Genesis — ‘In the beginning’ — and immediately places Jesus (the Word) there, before anything else existed. He then makes two statements in one breath: the Word was with God (pointing to a distinct, personal relationship) and the Word was fully God (pointing to a shared divine nature). This verse establishes from the outset that Jesus cannot be understood as merely a great man or even a lesser divine being — He is fully God, and the rest of John’s Gospel is written to demonstrate what that means.
elements.questionHint3: John says the Word was both ’with God’ and ’was fully God’ — do those two statements contradict each other, or could they together be pointing to something about Jesus that is more complex than a simple either/or?

elements.passageText1: <sup>5</sup>Da Yesu ya ga bangaskiyarsu sai ya ce wa shanyayyen, "Ɗana, an gafarta maka zunubanka." <sup>6</sup>To, waɗansu malaman Attaura na nan zaune, suna ta wuswasi a zuciya tasu, <sup>7</sup>suna cewa, "Don me mutumin nan yake faɗar haka? Ai, sāɓo yake! Wa yake da ikon gafarta zunubi banda Allah kaɗai?" <sup>8</sup>Nan da nan, da Yesu ya gane a ransa suna ta wuswasi haka a zuci, sai ya ce musu, "Don me kuke wuswasi haka a zuciyarku? <sup>9</sup>Wanne ya fi sauƙi, a ce wa shanyayyen, ‘An gafarta maka zunubanka’, ko kuwa a ce, ‘Tashi ka ɗauki shimfiɗarka ka yi tafiya’? <sup>10</sup>Amma don ku sakankance Ɗan Mutum na da ikon gafarta zunubi a duniya" sai ya ce wa shanyayyen, <sup>11</sup>"Na ce maka, tashi ka ɗauki shimfiɗarka ka tafi gida." <sup>12</sup>Sai ya tashi nan da nan, ya ɗauki shimfiɗarsa, ya fita a gaban idon kowa, har suka yi mamaki duka, suka ɗaukaka Allah suna cewa, "Ba mu taɓa ganin irin haka ba!"
elements.passageText3: <sup>5</sup>Da Yesu ya ga bangaskiyarsu, sai ya ce wa shanyayyen, "Saurayi, an gafarta zunubanka." <sup>6</sup>To, waɗansu malaman dokoki da suke zaune a wurin, suka yi tunani a zuciyarsu, suna cewa, <sup>7</sup>"Me ya sa mutumin nan yake magana haka? Yana saɓo! Wane ne zai iya gafarta zunubai, in ba Allah kaɗai ba?" <sup>8</sup>Nan da nan, Yesu ya gane a ruhunsa abin da suke tunani a zuciyarsu, sai ya ce musu, "Me ya sa kuke tunanin waɗannan abubuwa? <sup>9</sup>Wanne ya fi sauƙi, a ce wa shanyayyen, ‘An gafarta zunubanka,‘ ko a ce, ‘Tashi, ɗauki tabarmarka ka yi tafiya’? <sup>10</sup>Amma don ku san cewa, Ɗan Mutum yana da iko a duniya yă gafarta zunubai." Sai ya ce wa shanyayyen, <sup>11</sup>"Ina ce maka, tashi, ɗauki tabarmarka, ka tafi gida." <sup>12</sup>Sai ya tashi, ya ɗauki tabarmarsa, ya yi tafiyarsa a gabansu duka. Wannan ya ba wa kowa mamaki, suka kuma ɗaukaka Allah suna cewa, "Ba mu taɓa ganin abu haka ba!"
elements.passageText4: <sup>5</sup>Nde Yeesu yi’i hoolaare maɓɓe, o wi’i jaraaɗo on, "Higo, a yaafanaama hakkeeji maaɗa." <sup>6</sup>Woɓɓe moodiɓɓe Attawra e njooɗii ɗon e ɓe nyuma nder ɓerɗe maɓɓe, <sup>7</sup>e ɓe mbi’a, "Ngam ɗume neɗɗo o’o metirta nihi? E mo fottidina hoore maako e Allah! Moye waawata yaafaaki hakke to naa’ Allah tan?" <sup>8</sup>Ɗon e ɗon Yeesu faami nder ɓernde muuɗum e ɓe nyuma dow fii ɗu’um nder ɓerɗe maɓɓe, o wi’iɓe, "Ngam ɗume nyumoton iri nyumooji ɗi’i nder ɓerɗe mooɗon? <sup>9</sup>Ko ɓuri hoyki, ɗum wi’a jaraaɗo o’o, ‘A yaafanaama hakke maaɗa,‘ koo ɗum wi’amo, ‘Umma hoccu daago maaɗa, yaaɓu?’ <sup>10</sup>Ammaa e mi yiɗi andon Ɓii Neɗɗo e woodi laamu nder duuniyaaru yaafoo hakke." Ngam non o wi’i jaraaɗo on, <sup>11</sup>"E mi wi’umaa, umma, koccaa daago maaɗa, kootaa wuro." <sup>12</sup>Sey o ummii, o hocci daago maako, o wurtii, o dilli yeeso koo moye. Fii ɗu’um hayɗini fuu yimɓe gonɓe ɗon, ɓe manti Allah, ɓe mbi’i, "Min meeɗaayi yiiki iri fii ɗu’um!"
