elements.text1: Acikin kowa ce tattaunawa da wanda ke da kokwanto cewar akwai Allah. Maganar halitta ta kan yawan tasowa. Acikin wannan binciken ba lallai ya yiwu a iya magance duka mahawara game da halitta ba amma zamu duba bayani halitta bisa ga Littafi Mai Tsarki.
elements.text2: Koo nde yimɓe ɗon mboldata dow wooduɗo Allah koo walaa, tefere dow tagu duuniyaaru ɗon yalta. Nder jannge ɗu’um, min mawaata min kauna koo ɗume dow tagu, ammaa ko ɓuri maandaade woni ko Allah e hoore mum wolwi dow mu’um. Ngam majjum, nder jannge ɗo, min njaa haa habaru mo Deftere Allah hokkata — nden ka kadi e woodi ko wolwata ko a tamyaayi.
elements.text3: Questions about creation come up whenever people discuss whether God exists. There is far more to explore here than one study can cover, but what matters most is what God Himself says about it. So in this study we go to the Biblical account — and it has more to say than you might expect.

elements.text1: Wanene ya kasance cikin Halitta
elements.text2: Moyeeji njaadi nder Tagu ngun?
elements.text3: Who was involved in the Creation?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>A sa’ad da Allah ya fara halittar Sama da duniya,
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>A farko-farko, Allah ya halicci sama da ƙasa.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

elements.passageText1: <sup>12</sup>Ni ne wanda ya halicci duniya,
elements.passageText3: <sup>12</sup>Ai, ni ne wanda ya yi duniya
elements.passageText5: <sup>12</sup>I made the earth, and created mankind upon it. I—my own hands stretched out the heavens, and I gave orders to all the host of stars.

elements.question1: Farawa 1:1 da Ishaya 45:12 suna gaya mana menene game da rawar da Allah Uba ya taka wajen halitta?
elements.question2: Ko Fuɗɗoode 1:1 e Ishaya 45:12 mbi’ata en dow kuugal Allah Baaba nder tagu duuniyaaru?
elements.question3: What do Genesis 1:1 and Isaiah 45:12 tell us about God the Father’s role in creation?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Dukan ayoyin biyu suna nuna Allah a matsayin Halicci na gaskiya kuma asalin komai. Farawa 1:1 ya faɗi wannan a sarari cikakke — ‘A farkon farko, Allah ya halicci sama da ƙasa’ — ba tare da barin wuri ga tunanin cewa halitta ta faru ba tare da Shi ba ko kafin Shi. Ishaya 45:12 ya ƙara bayani kai tsaye: Allah Yana magana a mutum na farko, yana cewa ’Ni ne wanda ya halicci duniya’ kuma ’hannuwana ne suka shimfiɗa sammai’. Bai ba wani iko aikin halitta ba; Ya yi shi da Kansa, da cikakken iko — har zuwa inda Ya ba taurari umarni. Hoton da aka nuna shine na Allah wanda shi ne mai halitta na mutum, mai ikon mallaka, kuma da niyya wanda ya kasance asalin dukan abin da ya wanzu.
elements.questionHint1: A cikin Ishaya 45:12, Allah yana amfani da kalmomin ’Ni’ da ’hannuwana’ — menene wannan yare mai kai tsaye da na mutum ke nuna game da yadda Ya shiga wajen halitta, da kuma ko Ya buƙaci taimakon wani?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Siɗɗe ɗiɗi ɗuye ɗon hollita Allah hono Tagotoowo goɗɗo e fuɗɗorde ko fuu. Fuɗɗoode 1:1 ɗon wi’a ɗum tiiɗnde e kala nganaaka — ‘Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi’eteeɗo, Allah tagi dow e ley’ — walaa ɗoyngal ngal ko taguuji waɗii ko yanaatii Allah koo ɓuri maako. Ishaya 45:12 ɗon ɓura ɗum hollitaade tiiɗunde: Allah ɗon wolwoo e hoore mum, wi’a ’Mi waɗi leydi’ nden ’Juuɗe am ɗe njiilay dow’. O hokkii goɗɗo ɗum kuugal tagu; O waɗi kañum e hoore mum, bee kala laamu — haa nde O hokki taaraabiiji ɗi umaruje. Hoyre nde hollirtee woni hoyre Allah tagotoowo, jom laamu, nde waɗi koo ɗume bee nuunɗal.
elements.questionHint2: Nder Ishaya 45:12, Allah ɗon huutoo wolde ’Mi’ e ’Juuɗe am’ — ko woni ko wolde ɗiɗe ɗe jolnitiiɗe ɗe hollata dow no O neli hoore mum nder tagu, nden kadi dow ko o waawaa waɗde ɗum bee ballal goɗɗo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Both passages present God as the sole Creator and originator of everything that exists. Genesis 1:1 states this simply and absolutely — ‘In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth’ — leaving no room for creation to have happened without Him or before Him. Isaiah 45:12 adds a note of personal directness: God speaks in the first person, saying ’I made the earth’ and ’my own hands stretched out the heavens’. He did not delegate creation to another power; He made it Himself, with full authority — even giving orders to the stars. The overall picture is of a God who is the personal, sovereign, and intentional originator of all that exists.
elements.questionHint3: In Isaiah 45:12, God uses the words ’I’ and ’my own hands’ — what does that personal, direct language suggest about how involved He was in the act of creation, and whether He needed anyone else’s help?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Tun fil’azal akwai Kalma, Kalman nan kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman nan kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne tun fil’azal yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Dukan abubuwa sun kasance ta gare shi ne, ba kuma abin da ya kasance na abubuwan da suka kasance, sai ta game da shi.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>Tun farar farawa akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa yana nan tare da Allah, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Yana nan tare da Allah tun farar farawa. <sup>3</sup>Ta wurin Kalman ne aka halicci dukan abubuwa. In ba tare da shi ba, babu abin da aka yi wanda aka yi.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi’eteeɗo Wolde e ɗon, e mo wondi e Allah, nden o Allah. <sup>2</sup>Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi’eteeɗo Wolde on e wondi e Allah. <sup>3</sup>O tagii koo ɗume. Walaa goɗɗum nder fuu ko tagaa ɗum um tagiraayi laawol maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God. <sup>2</sup>The Word was with God in the beginning. <sup>3</sup>All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.

elements.passageText1: <sup>16</sup>don ta gare shi ne aka halicci dukkan kome, abubuwan da suke a sama da abubuwan da suke a ƙasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa, ko kursiyai, ko masu mulki, ko masarauta, ko masu iko, wato, dukkan abubuwa an halicce su ne ta wurinsa, shi ne kuma makomarsu. <sup>17</sup>Shi ne a gaba da dukkan abubuwa, dukkan abubuwa kuma shi ne yake riƙe da su.
elements.passageText3: <sup>16</sup>Ta wurinsa aka halicci dukan abu, abubuwan da suke cikin sama da abubuwan da suke a duniya, waɗanda ake gani, da waɗanda ba a gani, ko kursiyoyi ko ikoki ko masu mulki ko hukumomi; an halicci dukan abu ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa ne. <sup>17</sup>Ya kasance kafin dukan abu, kuma a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harhaɗe.
elements.passageText4: <sup>16</sup>Barka maako Allah tagi koo ɗume gonɗum dow e ley, ko yi’etee e ko yi’ataake, hawti e malaa’ika’en, e ginni, e koo ɗume gooduɗum laamu e bawɗe. Koo ɗume kanko tagiɗum, nden ngam maako koo ɗume tagaa. <sup>17</sup>Almasiihu e ɗon ko koo ɗume tagee, barka maako koo ɗume e yaha yeeso.
elements.passageText5: <sup>16</sup>For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities—all things were created through him and for him. <sup>17</sup>And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.

elements.question1: Yahaya 1:1-3 da Kolosiyawa 1:16-17 suna bayyana menene game da rawar Yesu wajen halitta? Shin wannan ya canza yadda kake tunanin Shi?
elements.question2: Ko Yahaya 1:1-3 e Kolosiyanko’en 1:16-17 ɗon kollita dow kuugal Yeesu nder tagu? Ɗu’um ɗon waylita no a miijorto dow Maako naa?
elements.question3: What do John 1:1-3 and Colossians 1:16-17 reveal about Jesus’ role in creation? Does this change how you think about Him?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yahaya 1:1-3 yana gaya mana cewa ba komai aka halicci ba tare da Yesu ba — Shi ba halittu bane amma shi ne wanda ta wurinsa dukan halitta ta fito. Kolosiyawa 1:16-17 ya ƙara bayani: dukan abubuwa an halicci su ba kawai ta wurinSa ba amma daSa kuma dominSa — ma’ana cewa dukan tsarin halitta yana da Yesu a matsayin asalinsa da makasudi. Aya ta 17 ta ƙara cewa Shi ’yana a gaba da dukan abubuwa’ kuma ’a cikinSa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harhaɗe’, wato duniya tana ci gaba da wanzuwa saboda ƙarfinSa na kiyayewa na ci gaba. Ga mutane da yawa da suke tunanin Yesu a matsayin malami ko mai warkarwa kawai, wannan hoton Shi a matsayin Haliccia kuma Mai Kiyaye dukan abubuwa yana canza yadda suke fahimtar wane ne Shi.
elements.questionHint1: Kolosiyawa 1:16 yana cewa an halicci komai ba kawai ’ta wurin’ Yesu ba amma kuma ’dominSa’ — me yake nufi ga halitta ya kasance an yi shi dominSa, kuma yadda wannan ya kwatanta da yadda kuke tunaninSa a al’ada?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya 1:1-3 ɗon hollita en ko walaa ɗume tagaa ko yanaatii Yeesu — O waayi taguɗo, ammaa woni mo tagu fuu waɗiraayi laawol mum. Kolosiyanko’en 1:16-17 ɗon ɓura ɗum maantinde: koo ɗume tagaa ko kanko kañum tagɗum, nden ngam maako kañum koo ɗume tagaa — nde waɗi kala nganaaka taguɗo renndoroo Yeesu ko fuɗɗorde mum e makaaɗe mum. Siɗɗo 17 ɗon ɓura wi’de o e ɗon ko koo ɗume tagee, nden barka maako koo ɗume e yaha yeeso, woni duuniyaaru nde e jokki e yaha ngam baawɗe maako nde hollinatnoo koo adde. Ngam yimɓe heewɓe miijotooɓe Yeesu hono jannginooɗo koo naa garaniiɗo, hoyre nde hollita mo hono Tagotoowo e Garoowo koo ɗume ɗon waylita no ɓe mbaawa annditaade ko kanko woni.
elements.questionHint2: Kolosiyanko’en 1:16 ɗon wi’a koo ɗume tagaa ko e Yeesu ko ’ngam maako’ kañum — ko woni ma’ana tagu waɗaama ngam Yeesu, nden no ɗum seedintini e no a miijortoo Maako koo adde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: John 1:1-3 tells us that nothing was created apart from Jesus — He was not a created being but the one through whom all creation came into existence. Colossians 1:16-17 deepens this further: all things were created not just through Him but by Him and for Him — meaning the entire created order has Jesus as both its origin and its goal. Verse 17 then adds that He is ’before all things’ and that in Him all things ’hold together’, meaning the universe continues to exist and cohere because of His ongoing sustaining power. For many people, thinking of Jesus primarily as a teacher or healer, this picture of Him as the Creator and Sustainer of all things significantly changes how they understand who He is.
elements.questionHint3: Colossians 1:16 says everything was created not just ’through’ Jesus but also ’for’ him — what does it mean for creation to have been made for Jesus, and how does that compare with the way you normally think about Him?

elements.passageText1: <sup>2</sup>duniya ba ta da siffa, sarari ce kawai, duhu kuwa yana lulluɓe da fuskar zurfin teku, Ruhun Allah kuma yana shawagi bisa ruwayen.
elements.passageText3: <sup>2</sup>To, ƙasa dai ba ta da siffa, babu kuma kome a cikinta, duhu ne kawai ya rufe ko’ina, Ruhun Allah kuwa yana yawo a kan ruwan.
elements.passageText5: <sup>2</sup>Now the earth was without shape and empty, and darkness was over the surface of the watery deep, but the Spirit of God was moving over the surface of the water.

elements.passageText1: <sup>30</sup>Amma sa’ad da ka hura musu numfashi, sai su rayu,
elements.passageText3: <sup>30</sup>Sa’ad da ka aika da Ruhunka,
elements.passageText5: <sup>30</sup>When you send up your breath, they are created, and you replenish the surface of the ground.

elements.question1: Farawa 1:2 da Zabura 104:30 suna bayyana menene game da rawar Ruhu Mai Tsarki wajen halitta? Me yake nufi cewa mutumin uku uku na Triniti duka sun shiga wajen halitta?
elements.question2: Ko Fuɗɗoode 1:2 e Jabuura 104:30 kollata dow kuugal Ruuhu Ceniiɗo nder tagu? Nden kadi ko woni ma’ana ko neɗɗo tati (Tatiraajo) fuu njaadi nder tagu ngun?
elements.question3: What do Genesis 1:2 and Psalm 104:30 reveal about the Holy Spirit’s role in creation? What does it mean that all three persons of the Trinity were involved?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Farawa 1:2 yana sanya Ruhu Allah daidai a farkon — yana shawagi bisa ruwayen da ba su da siffa, babu komai kafin Allah ya fara yin halitta cikin tsari. Hoton yana nuna kasancewar mai aiki da mai shiri, wanda ya shiga daga farko. Zabura 104:30 yana nuna cewa aikin Ruhu na halitta ba iyakancewa ne ga aiki na farko kawai ba: lokacin da Allah Ya aika da RuhuNSa, ana halicci halittu kuma ƙasa tana sabuntawa. Ruhu yana shiga cikin halitta ta ci gaba da sabunta, ba kawai a farkon abin da ya faru ba. Shigar mutane uku ukun Triniti — Uba, Ɗa, da Ruhu — wajen halitta yana gaya mana cewa halitta ba aikin wani memba ɗaya ba ne da ya yi shi a shi kaɗai, amma aiki haɗin kai na dukan Allahanci. Duniya ba ta fito daga wani ɓangare na Allah kawai ba amma daga cikar wanda Allah yake.
elements.questionHint1: Farawa 1:2 yana nuna Ruhu yana nan daidai a farkon, kafin komai ya tsara — kuma Zabura 104:30 yana nuna Shi yana shiga wajen kawo rayuwa sabuwa. Waɗannan lokuttan biyu tare suna nuna menene game da faɗin shiga Ruhu wajen halitta?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Fuɗɗoode 1:2 ɗon ɗoyngana Ruuhu Allah haa fuɗɗorde tigi — o neli dow ndiyam ko walaa sifaa, baawɗe, ko fuu ɓaawo Allah fuɗɗata tagu e diidol. Hoyre nde hollirtee ɗon hollita kasandiral mo jarɗinaaɗo, mo neli laawol maako daga fuɗɗorde. Jabuura 104:30 ɗon hollita ko kuugal Ruuhu nder tagu ɗon walaa taraani e waato fuɗɗoode tan: nde Allah neli Ruuhu Mum, taguuji tagaa nden leydi ɗon hisantaa koy. Ruuhu ɗon neli laawol maako nder tagu e saabu maare, walaa e waato fuɗɗoode tan. Tawa Tatiraajo fuu — Baaba, Ɓii, e Ruuhu — njaadi nder tagu ɗon hollita en tagu waayi ko taaɓal goɗɗo e hoore mum waɗaa e hoore mum, ammaa taaɓal haɗiiɗel ngel ɗuurondiri e Allah. Duuniyaaru nde yaltaay e gure go’o nder Allah, ammaa e heɓi-heɓi maako fuu.
elements.questionHint2: Fuɗɗoode 1:2 ɗon hollita Ruuhu e ɗon ko koo ɗume tagee bi’eteeɗo, ko koo fuu sifaaka — nden Jabuura 104:30 ɗon hollita mo neli laawol maako nder wuurde sabuɗe yaɗde. Ko woni ko dakiika ɗiɗi ɗuye ɗe heewi hollitaade dow hebitaare ko Ruuhu neli laawol maako nder tagu?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Genesis 1:2 places the Spirit of God at the very beginning — moving over the formless, empty waters before God speaks creation into order. The image suggests a hovering, active presence, ready and involved from the outset. Psalm 104:30 shows that the Spirit’s creative work is not limited to the initial act: when God sends His breath (Spirit), living creatures are created and the ground is renewed. The Spirit is involved in ongoing creation and replenishment, not just in the original event. The involvement of all three persons of the Trinity — Father, Son, and Spirit — in creation tells us that creation was not the work of one member acting alone, but a unified act of the whole Godhead. The world did not come from one dimension of God but from the fullness of who God is.
elements.questionHint3: Genesis 1:2 shows the Spirit present at the very start, before anything was formed — and Psalm 104:30 shows Him involved in bringing new life into being. What do those two moments together suggest about the scope of the Spirit’s involvement in creation?

elements.text1: Menene aka halitta?
elements.text2: Ko Allah tagi?
elements.text3: What did God create?

elements.question: It’s worth reading the whole passage before answering the question below…
elements.answer:
1In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. 2Now the earth was without shape and empty, and darkness was over the surface of the watery deep, but the Spirit of God was moving over the surface of the water. 3God said, ‘Let there be light.’ And there was light! 4God saw that the light was good, so God separated the light from the darkness. 5God called the light ’day’ and the darkness ’night.’ There was evening, and there was morning, marking the first day.

6God said, ‘Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters and let it separate water from water.’ 7So God made the expanse and separated the water under the expanse from the water above it. It was so. 8God called the expanse ’sky.’ There was evening, and there was morning, a second day.

9God said, ‘Let the water under the sky be gathered to one place and let dry ground appear.’ It was so. 10God called the dry ground ’land’ and the gathered waters he called ’seas.’ God saw that it was good. 11God said, ‘Let the land produce vegetation: plants yielding seeds and trees on the land bearing fruit with seed in it, according to their kinds.’ It was so. 12The land produced vegetation—plants yielding seeds according to their kinds, and trees bearing fruit with seed in it according to their kinds. God saw that it was good. 13There was evening, and there was morning, a third day.

14God said, ‘Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to separate the day from the night, and let them be signs to indicate seasons and days and years, 15and let them serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to give light on the earth.’ It was so. 16God made two great lights—the greater light to rule over the day and the lesser light to rule over the night. He made the stars also. 17God placed the lights in the expanse of the sky to shine on the earth, 18to preside over the day and the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. God saw that it was good. 19There was evening, and there was morning, a fourth day.

20God said, ‘Let the water swarm with swarms of living creatures and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky.’ 21God created the great sea creatures and every living and moving thing with which the water swarmed, according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. 22God blessed them and said, ‘Be fruitful and multiply and fill the water in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth.’ 23There was evening, and there was morning, a fifth day.

24God said, ‘Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: cattle, creeping things, and wild animals, each according to their kind.’ It was so. 25God made the wild animals according to their kinds, the cattle according to their kinds, and all the creatures that creep along the ground according to their kinds. God saw that it was good.

26Then God said, ‘Let us make humankind in our image, after our likeness, so they may rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air, over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over all the creatures that move on the earth.’ 27God created humankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them, male and female he created them.

28God blessed them and said to them, ‘Be fruitful and multiply! Fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air and every creature that moves on the ground.’

31God saw all that he had made—and it was very good! There was evening, and there was morning, the sixth day.

2:1The heavens and the earth were completed with everything that was in them. 2By the seventh day God finished the work that he had been doing, and he ceased on the seventh day all the work that he had been doing. 3God blessed the seventh day and made it holy because on it he ceased all the work that he had been doing in creation.


(NET - see context ↗)

elements.question1: Daga Farawa 1:1–2:3, jera manyan abubuwa shida da Allah ya halitta. Wane tsari ko tsarin gini kuke lura da shi a cikin kwanakin shida?
elements.question2: Daga Fuɗɗoode 1:1–2:3, winndu kulle joweego mawɗe ɗe Allah tagi. Ko woni mbadi koo sifaka mo a maandii dow balɗe joweego ɗen?
elements.question3: From Genesis 1:1–2:3, list six main things God created. What pattern or structure do you notice across the six days?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Manyan abubuwa shida da Allah ya halitta: (1) haske da rabuwar rana da dare; (2) sama da rabuwar ruwa; (3) ƙasa mai bushe, teku, da tsirrai; (4) rana, wata, da taurari; (5) halittu na teku da tsuntsaye; (6) dabbobin ƙasa da ’yan adam. Tsari mai ƙarfi ya fito a cikin kwanakin shida: kwanaki uku na farko sun tsara muhallin ko ’sarari’ (haske, sama, ƙasa/teku), kuma kwanakin uku masu zuwa sun cika waɗannan sarari da mazaunansu masu dacewa (fitilu sun cika sama, kifi da tsuntsaye sun cika teku da sama, dabbobi da ’yan adam sun cika ƙasa). Kowace rana tana ƙarewa da ’Allah ya ga cewa yana da kyau’, kuma jerin duka sun ƙare da ’mai kyau ƙwarai’. Tsarin yana tafiya daga rashin siffa da babu komai zuwa da tsari da cike — yana nuna halitta a matsayin da niyya, da tsari, da manufa.
elements.questionHint1: Ka duba kwanaki 1-3 sannan kwanaki 4-6 gefe da gefe — shin kuna lura da alaka tsakanin abin da aka halicci a rana ta 1 da rana ta 4, rana ta 2 da rana ta 5, rana ta 3 da rana ta 6? Wannan yin biyu yana nuna menene game da tsarin labarun?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Kuuje joweego mawɗe ɗe Allah tagi: (1) ndaygu e seebaare hannde e kiikiide; (2) dow e seebaare ndiyam; (3) leydi toowɗi, maayo e riiweendi; (4) naange, lewru e taaraabiiji; (5) taguuji ndiyam e tiiɗo; (6) taguuji leydi e yimɓe. Mbadi haariiɗi ɗon yalta nder balɗe joweego ɗen: balɗe tati fuɗɗoode ɗen ɗon sifaa yoorno’en koo ’ɗoyngal’ (ndaygu, dow, leydi/maayo), nden balɗe tati goɗɗe ɗen ɗon hisana yoorno’en ɗen e keerol maɓɓe ɗen (fitiilaaji ɗon hiisana dow, liɗɗi e tiiɗo ɗon hiisana maayo e dow, taguuji e yimɓe ɗon hiisana leydi). Kala ɗoon nyalaande ɗon ƴetoo e ’Allah yi’i ɗum woni boɗɗum’, nden kala nganaaka ɗon wiɗɗitoo e ’boɗɗum sanne’. Mbadi ɗon yaŋa daga walaa sifaa e baawɗe haa e diidol e hebliiɗol — hollitde tagu gaa o waɗi bee nuunɗal, diidol, e yiɗo.
elements.questionHint2: Yiy balɗe 1-3 nden balɗe 4-6 taaɗi — aɗa yii ko woodi jokki tawi ko tagaa nyalaande 1 e nyalaande 4, nyalaande 2 e nyalaande 5, nyalaande 3 e nyalaande 6? Ko woni ko ɗimɓinde ɗiɗi ɗuye hollata dow sifaa labaari ngari ngun?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Six main things God created: (1) light and the separation of day and night; (2) the sky and the separation of waters; (3) dry land, seas, and vegetation; (4) the sun, moon, and stars; (5) sea creatures and birds; (6) land animals and human beings. A clear pattern emerges across the six days: the first three days form the environments or ’spaces’ (light, sky, land/sea), and the next three days fill those spaces with their corresponding inhabitants (luminaries fill the sky, fish and birds fill sea and sky, animals and humans fill the land). Each day ends with ’God saw that it was good’, and the whole sequence concludes with ’very good’. The pattern moves from formless and empty to ordered and filled — showing creation as deliberate, structured, and purposeful.
elements.questionHint3: Look at days 1-3 and then days 4-6 side by side — do you notice a relationship between what is created on day 1 and day 4, day 2 and day 5, day 3 and day 6? What does that pairing suggest about the structure of the account?

elements.text1: Tayaya Allah ya halicci duniya?

elements.question1: Allah yana halitta ta hanyar magana kawai. Menene wannan yake gaya mana game da ƙarfinSa da ikon mallakaSa a kan duniyar da Ya halitta?
elements.question2: Allah ɗon taga bee wolde mum tan. Ɗume ɗum holli en dow baawɗe maako e laamu maako dow tagu duuniyaaru?
elements.question3: God creates simply by speaking. What does this tell us about His power and authority over the created world?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Tsarin da ya maimaita ’Allah ya ce... sai ya kasance’ a cikin Farawa 1 yana nuna cewa kalmar Allah kaɗai ta isa ta kawo komai cikin wanzuwa. Bai buƙaci kayan aiki, ƙoƙari, kayan aiki, ko taimako — yana magana kawai kuma abin da ya kasance yana amsa nan take kuma cikar. Wannan yana gaya mana cewa mulkin Allah a kan halitta yana da cikakken iko: duniyar da aka halicci ba ta wanzu daban da Shi ba amma tana ƙarƙashin umarninSa gaba ɗaya. Babu dan adam, babu ƙarfin halitta, babu komai a cikin duniya da ke da iko mai kwatankwacin na Allah — halitta ta zo cikin wanzuwa ta kalmarSa, tana ci gaba da wanzuwa saboda ƙarfinSa na kiyayewa, kuma tana amsa gareshe gaba ɗaya.
elements.questionHint1: Lokacin da dan adam ya ba da umarni, yana yiwuwa a yi masa ko a ƙi masa — amma lokacin da Allah ya yi magana a Farawa 1, menene koyaushe yakan faru nan take, kuma wannan amsa nan take cikakkiya tana gaya mana menene game da irin ikon da Shi yana da shi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Mbadi ko maimayiraaɗi ’Allah wi’i... ndaa ɗum waɗi’ nder Fuɗɗoode 1 ɗon hollita ko wolde Allah kaɗum joɗii ngam wadde koo ɗume. O haajanaay alluuje, semmbe, koolooji, koo naa ballal — O wolwoo tan nden ko waɗirnoo ɗon jaaboo e nde nde nde nde e heɓi-heɓi. Ngam majjum ɗon hollita en laamu Allah dow tagu fotnoo bee huuɓunde: tagu walaa e dañde hoore mum ko yanaataa Mo, ammaa woni e yeeso kala umarni mum. Walaa neɗɗo, walaa semmbe taguɗo, walaa ɗume nder duuniyaaru ko woodi semmbe naange ko nde nde nde nde e Allah — tagu yalti nder wuurnde bee wolde mum, e yaha e jokki e yaha ngam semmbe maako garowol, nden o jaaboo mo heɓi-heɓi.
elements.questionHint2: Nde neɗɗo hokki umarni, ko waawi waɗeede koo naa waawetee — ammaa nde Allah wolwi nder Fuɗɗoode 1, ko woni ko koyɗo e waɗeede hannde nde nde, nden no jaabo ngoo nde nde e heɓi-heɓi hollata ko woni hono laamu ngo o woodi?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The repeated pattern of ’God said... and it was so’ throughout Genesis 1 shows that God’s word alone is sufficient to bring anything into existence. He does not need materials, effort, tools, or assistance — He simply speaks and reality responds instantly and completely. This tells us that God’s authority over creation is absolute: the created world does not exist independently of Him but is entirely subject to His command. No human being, no force of nature, and nothing in the universe has power comparable to God’s — creation came into being by His word, continues to exist by His sustaining power, and answers to Him entirely.
elements.questionHint3: When a human being gives an order, it might or might not be obeyed — but when God speaks in Genesis 1, what always happens immediately, and what does that instant, total response tell you about the kind of authority He has?

elements.text1: Menene Allah ya yi tunani bayan ya gama hallatar komai
elements.text2: Ɗume Allah miijori dow koo ɗume ko o tagi?
elements.text3: What did God think about everything He had created?

elements.passageText1: <sup>31</sup>Allah kuwa ya ga dukan abin da ya yi, ga shi kuwa yana da kyau ƙwarai. Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na shida ke nan.
elements.passageText3: <sup>31</sup>Allah ya ga dukan abin da ya yi, yana da kyau ƙwarai. Yamma ta kasance, safiya kuma ta kasance, kwana ta shida ke nan.
elements.passageText5: <sup>31</sup>God saw all that he had made—and it was very good! There was evening, and there was morning, the sixth day.

elements.question1: Allah Ya bayyana halittarSa a matsayin ’mai kyau ƙwarai’. Menene wannan yake gaya mana game da duniya kamar yadda Allah Ya ƙirƙira ta a farko — kuma me yasa hakan yana da mahimmanci ga yadda muke tunanin duniya yanzu?
elements.question2: Allah wi’i tagu maako woni ’boɗɗum sanne’. Ɗume ɗum holli en dow no duuniyaaru wa’ino haa fuɗɗoode — nden kadi ko woni mahimmanki majjum haa no min miijorto dow duuniyaaru jonta?
elements.question3: God declares His creation ’very good’. What does that tell us about the world as God originally made it — and why does that matter for how we think about the world now?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bayyanar ’mai kyau ƙwarai’ yana gaya mana cewa duniya kamar yadda Allah Ya ƙirƙira ta ba ta da lahani, tana karye, ko tana da tsaka tsaki ta ɗabi’a — ta yi daidai da niyya ta Allah kuma ta nuna halinsA. Ba wai wahala, ba mutuwa, ba ɓarna. Wannan yana da mahimmanci sosai ga yadda muke tunanin duniya yanzu, domin yana nufin ciwo, ɓarkewar, da mugunta da muke gani ba fasalolin halitta na asali ba ne — su ne baƙi da suka shigo. Duniya kamar yadda take yanzu an lalata ta, ba an tsara ta haka ba. Wannan yana ba mu dalili na yin baƙin ciki ga abin da ya ɓace da kuma bege ga abin da Allah Ya yi alkawarin mayarwa — yana nufin kuma ba zamu iya zargin Allah da yanayin duniya kamar yadda take yanzu ba.
elements.questionHint1: Idan Allah Ya kira halitta ta farko ’mai kyau ƙwarai’ amma duniyar da muke gani a yau tana ɗauke da wahala da ɓarkewar, menene wannan bambancin yana nuna game da ko Allah Ya tsara abubuwa haka, ko wani abu ya tafi kuskure tun daga nan?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Wolwude ’boɗɗum sanne’ ɗon hollita en ko duuniyaaru hono Allah tagiri nde fuɗɗoode waayi duuniyaaru ko walaa aibu, ko karɗaaki, koo naa ko walaa nuunɗal — nde waɗii daidaari e nuunɗal Allah nden hollitii sifa mum. Walaa weloore, walaa maayde, walaa bonande. Ngam majjum ɗon woodiri heewi ngam no min miijorto dow duuniyaaru jonta, ngam ɗum ɗon waɗa en anndan ko cewɗi, boniiɗi e ko boni min yiɗi yi’de waawaa gaa tagaama ɗum fuu — ɓe waɗi taaɓal goppuɗo, walaa ɗum tagiraayi hono ndeen. Ngam majjum ɗon hokka en dalil ngam gullande ko bonii nden miilinde ko Allah seli e suɓtinaade welto — ɗum kadi hollita min mbaawa zallude Allah dow ko duuniyaaru jonta woni.
elements.questionHint2: Si Allah wi’i tagu fuɗɗoode ’boɗɗum sanne’ ammaa duuniyaaru min yiɗi yi’de jonta ɗon wada weloore e bonande, ko woni ko ndiyam ɗiɗam ɗuye hollata dow ko Allah tagiri ɗum hono ndeen, koo si ko ɗume bonii caggal?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The declaration ’very good’ tells us that the world as God originally made it was not flawed, broken, or morally neutral — it was perfectly aligned with God’s intention and reflected His character. There was no suffering, no death, no corruption. This matters enormously for how we think about the world now, because it means the pain, brokenness, and evil we see are not original features of creation — they are intrusions. The world as it now is has been damaged, not designed that way. This gives us reason to grieve what has been lost and hope for what God has promised to restore — it also means we cannot blame God for the state of the world as it currently exists.
elements.questionHint3: If God called the original creation ’very good’ but the world we see today contains suffering and brokenness, what does that difference suggest about whether God designed things this way, or whether something has gone wrong since?

elements.text1: Menene dalilin da ya sa Allah halacci ƴan adam
elements.text2: Ko woni dalila tagu duuniyaaru?
elements.text3: Why did God create the world?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Dubi yadda sararin sama yake bayyana ɗaukakar Allah!
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>Sammai suna shelar ɗaukakar Allah;
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>The heavens declare the glory of God; the sky displays his handiwork.

elements.question1: Bisa ga wannan aya, wane manufa halitta take yi? Kuna iya tunanin wani lokaci da yanayi ya nuna muku hanyar zuwa ga Allah?
elements.question2: Bana no ayaare nde wolwi, ko woni kuugal ngal tagu waɗata? Aɗa waawi siftorde wakkati mo tagu duuniyaaru kollini ma laawol Allah?
elements.question3: According to this verse, what purpose does creation serve? Can you think of a moment when the natural world has pointed you towards God?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bisa ga wannan aya, halitta tana yin manufar bayyana ɗaukakar Allah da nuna ayyukansa. Sammai ba shaidun shiru ba ne — suna shelar wani abu game da Mahaliccinsu. Halitta, a wani ma’ana, tana ba da shaida ta dindindin, ta duniya, ga wanzuwar Allah, ƙarfinSa, da kyaunsA, wadda kowane mutum a kowace zamanin da al’ada zai iya samu. Mutane da yawa suna gano cewa lokuttan da ke cikin duniyar halitta — sararin sama mai yawa da daren, tsaunin tudun, bakin teku, ban mamaki na wata halittar mai rai — suna haifar da jin tsoron Allah wanda a hankali yana nuna wuce abin kansa zuwa ga wanda Ya ƙirƙira shi. Wannan shi ne daidai abin da mawaƙin ya ce halitta an tsara ta ta yi.
elements.questionHint1: Zabura yana cewa sammai ’suna shelar’ wani abu — kamar dai halitta tana sadar da saƙo mai aiki. Menene saƙon da take shelarsa, kuma shin kun taba ganin kanku kuna jin wannan saƙon lokacin da kuke kalloni wani abu a duniyar halitta?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bana no ayaare nde, tagu ɗon waɗa kuugal ngal hollitde teddungal Allah e hollitde taaɓal juuɗe mum. Dow ɗon walaa shayiduuji muumiiɓe — ɓe noldata ko ɗume e Tagotoowo maɓɓe. Tagu, e laawol goɗɗo, woni seedtere nde ɗon e keewal, nde jaajde, ngam woodugol Allah, semmbe mum e mbele mum, nde kala neɗɗo e kala sahaa e kala senngo mbaawa yiyde. Yimɓe heewɓe ɗon yiytoo ko nder dakiika ɗe nder duuniyaaru tagɗo — dow faatiraaɗo e kiikiide, duwanyi mawɗi, kenngol maayo, yiɗtaari taguɗo wuuruɗo — ɗon waɗa en miilanaago majjum ko yawtii ɗum haa Mo tagiri ɗum. Ko nden nden nden nden Jabuuraajo ɗon wi’i tagu waɗaama ngam waɗde.
elements.questionHint2: Jabuura ɗon wi’a dow ɗon ’noldata’ ko ɗume — hono nde nde tagu ɗon wolwi fow habaaru. Ko woni habaaru ngo ɗon nolditee, nden aɗa waawi siftorde wakkati mo a yi’i ɗum a miilanaago ɗum nde a yi’i ko ɗume nder duuniyaaru tagɗo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: According to this verse, creation serves the purpose of declaring God’s glory and displaying His handiwork. The heavens are not silent witnesses — they actively proclaim something about their Maker. Creation is, in a sense, a constant, universal testimony to God’s existence, power, and beauty, available to every person in every age and culture. Many people find that moments in the natural world — a vast night sky, a mountain range, a coastline, the intricacy of a living creature — produce a sense of awe that points instinctively beyond the thing itself to the one who made it. This is precisely what the psalmist says creation is designed to do.
elements.questionHint3: The psalm says the heavens ’declare’ something — as if creation is actively communicating a message. What is the message it is declaring, and have you ever found yourself sensing that message when looking at something in the natural world?

elements.passageText1: <sup>7</sup>Su mutanena ne, na kaina,
elements.passageText3: <sup>7</sup>kowa da ake kira da sunana,
elements.passageText5: <sup>7</sup>everyone who is called by my name, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed and made.

elements.question1: Ishaya yana cewa Allah Ya tsara Ya kuma yi mu dominSa da ɗaukakarSa. Me yake nufi a rayuwa dominSa da ɗaukaka a aikace?
elements.question2: Ishaya wi’i Allah tagi en ngam teddungal mum. Ko woni ma’ana ’wuurdude ngam teddungal Allah’ nder kuuɗe meeɗen nde kowa nde?
elements.question3: Isaiah says God formed and made us for His glory. What does it mean to live for God’s glory in practice?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Rayuwa dominSa da ɗaukakar Allah yana nufin rayuwa ta yadda ta sa Allah ya zama ɗaukakakke — wanda ke jan hankalin zuwa ga halinsA, kyaunsA, da darajarSa maimakon zuwa ga kanmu. A aikace, ba game da wani aiki mai ban mamaki guda ɗaya ba amma game da alkiblar rayuwa gaba ɗaya: zaɓuɓɓukan da muke yi, yadda muke bi da mutane, abubuwan da muke nema da ba da fifiko, da kuma ko rayuwarmu tana nuna wa sauran hanyar zuwa ga Allah ko ta nisantar da su. Tun da an ƙirƙira mu cikin surar Allah (Farawa 1:27), rayuwa dominSa da ɗaukaka yana nufin kuma nuna halinsA — ƙaunarSa, adalcinSa, amintarSa, da tausayinSa — a rayuwarmu ta yau da kullun. Manufar wanzuwarmu, bisa ga Ishaya, ba cika kanmu ba ne amma rayuwa mai mayar da hankali ga Allah.
elements.questionHint1: Idan an halicci mu ’dominSa da ɗaukakar Allah’ yana nufin rayuwarmu an yi ta nunawa zuwa gareSa, wane irin abubuwa mutum zai iya yi — ko guje wa yi — a rayuwar yau da kullun wanda zai nuna ko ya sabawa wannan manufar?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Wuurde ngam teddungal Allah ɗon waɗa wuurde e laawol ngal kollirata Allah hono boɗɗuɗo — nde njollitata sifa mum, mbele mum e tiindi mum ko yanaatii hoore meeɗen. Nder taaɓal, ɗum walaa e taaɓal juumtaaɗo go’o tan ammaa e ñiiɓdoore wuurnam gaa fuu: suɓe min njokkata, no min mbaawa yimɓe, ɗume min njokkata e min njokkita e ɗume min ndokkata yeeso, nden kadi ko wuurnam meeɗen kollita yimɓe laawol haa Allah koo yaltinaata ɓe. Tawa en tagaama e sifa Allah (Fuɗɗoode 1:27), wuurde ngam teddungal mum ɗon kadi waɗa hollitde sifa mum — yiɗi mum, tiiɗi mum, innitare mum e suuɗo mum — nder kuuɗe meeɗen nde kowa nde. Sabu wuurnam meeɗen, bana Ishaya wolwi, walaa e heɓde heɓtinde hoore meeɗen ammaa e wuurde e maayanaago Allah.
elements.questionHint2: Si tagaama ’ngam teddungal Allah’ ɗon waɗa wuurnam meeɗen taraani kollinaade Mo, ko woni ko woni ɗe neɗɗo mbaawi waɗde — koo naa waɗanaa waɗde — nder kuuɗe nde kowa nde ɗe nde mbaawi hollinaade koo waylitaade sabu ngam majjum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: To live for God’s glory means to live in a way that makes God look great — that draws attention to His character, His goodness, and His worth rather than to ourselves. In practice, this is not about a single dramatic act but about the orientation of an entire life: the choices we make, the way we treat people, the things we pursue and prioritise, and whether our lives point others towards God or away from Him. Since we are made in God’s image (Genesis 1:27), living for His glory also means reflecting His character — His love, justice, faithfulness, and compassion — in our everyday lives. The purpose of our existence, according to Isaiah, is not self-fulfilment but God-centred living.
elements.questionHint3: If being created ’for God’s glory’ means our lives are meant to point to Him, what kinds of things might a person do — or avoid doing — in ordinary daily life that would either reflect or contradict that purpose?

elements.text1: Menene ya sa muka zama da mahimmanci?
elements.text2: Ko waɗi Allah tagi yimɓe?
elements.text3: Why did God create human beings?

elements.passageText1: <sup>27</sup>Haka nan fa, Allah ya halicci mutum cikin siffarsa, cikin siffar Allah, Allah ya halicci mutum, namiji da ta mace ya halicce su.
elements.passageText3: <sup>27</sup>Saboda haka Allah ya halicci mutum cikin siffarsa.
elements.passageText5: <sup>27</sup>God created mankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them, male and female he created them.

elements.question1: Me yake nufi cewa an halicci ’yan adam ’cikin surar Allah’? Yadda wannan yake bambanta mu daga sauran halitta?
elements.question2: Ko woni ma’ana woni yimɓe tagaama bee nanndola Allah? No ɗum ɗon seedintini en bee taguuji luttuɗi fuu?
elements.question3: What does it mean that human beings are made ’in the image of God’? How does this set us apart from the rest of creation?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: An halicci mutum cikin surar Allah yana nufin cewa ’yan adam sun nuna wani abu na halin Allah na gaskiya — tunaninsA, ma’anar ɗabi’arSa, ƙarfinSa don dangantaka, ƙirƙira, da ƙauna. Babu wata halittar da aka bayyana haka a Farawa. Ana halicci dabbobi ’bisa ga irin su’, amma ana halicci ’yan adam bisa ga surar da kamannin Allah Himself, wanda ke sa mu cikin wata rukunin gaba ɗaya. Yana nufin ba kawai mu halittu ne na halitta ba amma mutane na mutumci da an tsara su don dangantaka da Allah, masu iyawar saninsA, wakilcinsA a cikin duniya, da kuma a riƙe da su da lissafin ɗabi’a a gare Shi. Yana nufin kuma cewa kowace rayuwa ta dan adam tana da daraja ta asali wadda ba za a iya cire ta ba, domin surar Allah ce da ake ɗauka a kowace mutumin.
elements.questionHint1: Farawa ta bayyana dabbobi ana halicce su ’bisa ga irin su’ amma ta bayyana ’yan adam daban — an halicce su cikin surar da kamannin Allah Himself. Wannan bayani daban yana nuna menene game da irin dangantakar da Allah Ya yi niyya da ’yan adam idan aka kwatanta da sauran halitta?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Tagaama bee nanndola Allah ɗon waɗa yimɓe ɗon hollitata ko ɗume nder sifa Allah e hoore mum — annditaare mum, nuunɗal ɗabi’a mum, semmbe mum ngam jokkirde, ƴeewre e yiɗi. Walaa taguɗo goɗɗo nder Fuɗɗoode hollirtee hono ɗum. Taguuji ɗon tagaa ’bana kaaje mum’, ammaa yimɓe tagaa bana sifa e nanndola Allah Hoore mum, nde waɗi en nder ngenaaji goɗɗi heɓi-heɓi. Ngam majjum ɗon waɗa en walaa e jokooji hoodere tan ammaa en yimɓe jolnitooɓe tagaama ngam jokkirde e Allah, ɗi mbaawi annditaade Mo, hollinaade Mo nder duuniyaaru, nden kadi en mbaawi dogirde biro mum. Ɗum kadi ɗon waɗa en anndan wuurnam kala yimɓe woodi tiindi maɓɓe nde yaltaani, ngam sifa Allah woni nde ɗon ɗaɓɓirte e neɗɗo fuu.
elements.questionHint2: Fuɗɗoode ɗon hollita taguuji tagaa ’bana kaaje mum’ ammaa ɗon hollita yimɓe hano goɗɗo — tagaa bee sifa e nanndola Allah Hoore mum. Ko woni ko bayyan goɗɗo ɗo hollita dow hono jokkirde nde Allah yiɗi nde jokkirde nde o yaɗi yimɓe bee taguuji luttuɗi fuu?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Being made in the image of God means that human beings uniquely reflect something of God’s own nature — His rationality, His moral sense, His capacity for relationship, creativity, and love. No other creature in Genesis is described this way. The animals are made ’according to their kinds’, but human beings are made according to God’s own image and likeness, which places us in an entirely different category. It means we are not merely biological organisms but personal beings designed for relationship with God, capable of knowing Him, representing Him in the world, and being held morally accountable to Him. It also means that every human life has an inherent dignity that cannot be stripped away, because it is God’s image that is borne in each person.
elements.questionHint3: Genesis describes animals being made ’according to their kinds’ but describes humans differently — made in the image and likeness of God Himself. What does that different description suggest about the kind of relationship God intends to have with human beings compared to the rest of creation?

elements.text1: An halicce mu acikin kamannin Allah. Haka nan fa, Allah ya halicci mutum cikin siffarsa, cikin siffar Allah, Allah ya halicci mutum, namiji da ta mace ya halicce su. Farawa 1:27
elements.text2: Goonga woni tagu meeɗen bee nanndola Allah ɗon seedintina yimɓe bee taguuji duuniyaaru luttuɗi. Ɗum ɗon holla min wonaa min njalti tan bee masiibe koo sifaaji ɓanduum — min taguuji waɗaaɗi ngam jamanu bee Allah, mo waawi hollaade sifaaji maako nder mbuurnam meeɗen.
elements.text3: The fact that we are made in God’s image sets human beings apart from the rest of creation. It means we are not simply the product of chance or biology — we are creatures made for relationship with God, capable of reflecting His character in the way we live.

elements.text1: Wane irin aiki Allah ya ba ƴan adam?
elements.text2: Ko woni kuugal ngal Allah hokki yimɓe?
elements.text3: What responsibility has God given human beings?

elements.passageText1: <sup>26</sup>Allah kuma ya ce, ‘Bari mu yi mutum cikin siffarmu, da kamanninmu, su mallaki kifayen da suke a cikin teku, da tsuntsayen sararin sama, da dabbobi, da dukan duniya, da kowane abu mai rarrafe da yake rarrafe bisa ƙasa.’
elements.passageText3: <sup>26</sup>Sa’an nan Allah ya ce, ‘Bari mu yi mutum cikin siffarmu, cikin kamanninmu, bari kuma su yi mulki a bisa kifin teku, tsuntsayen sama, da kuma dabbobi, su yi mulki a bisa dukan duniya, da kuma a bisa dukan halittu, da masu rarrafe a ƙasa.’
elements.passageText5: <sup>26</sup>Then God said, ‘Let us make mankind in our image, after our likeness, so they may rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air, over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over all the creatures that move on the ground.’

elements.passageText1: <sup>15</sup>Ubangiji Allah ya ɗauki mutumin ya zaunar da shi cikin gonar Aidan ya noma ta, ya kiyaye ta.
elements.passageText3: <sup>15</sup>  Ubangiji  Allah ya ɗauko mutumin ya sa shi cikin Lambun Eden domin yă nome shi, yă kuma lura da shi.
elements.passageText5: <sup>15</sup>The Lord God took the man and placed him in the orchard in Eden to care for it and to maintain it.

elements.question1: Wane ayyuka guda biyu waɗannan ayoyin suke ba ’yan adam game da halitta ta Allah? Yadda kuke tunanin muna cika su a yau?
elements.question2: Kuuje ɗiɗi ɗuye ayeeji ɗi hokki yimɓe dow tagu Allah? No a miijorto en ɗon mballita ɗum jonta?
elements.question3: What two responsibilities do these verses give to human beings in relation to God’s creation? How well do you think we are fulfilling them today?
elements.answerPlaceholder: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Ayyukan biyu sune: (1) mulkin halitta — yin mulki da kula a madadin Allah a matsayin wakilansa a duniya; da kuma (2) kula da kulawa da kiyayewa — yaren Farawa 2:15 na mai gonar ko mai kula, yana noma da kiyaye abin da aka ba shi amana. Waɗannan sun haɗu, ba sun sabawa juna ba: an ba ikon mulkin a cikin mahallin kula mai alhakin, ba cin zarafi ba. Game da yadda muke cika waɗannan ayyuka a yau — mutane da yawa za su faɗi a gaskiya cewa lalatar muhalli mai yaɗuwa, asarar nau’in halittu, da cin zarafi na albarkatun halitta yana nuna gazawar sarrafa da Allah Ya nufa. Umarnin mulki ba taba kasancewa izni ba ne na lalata; shi ne kira na hidima ga halitta a madadin Allah.
elements.questionHint1: Farawa 1:26 yana ba ’yan adam ikon ’mulki a kan’ halitta, yayin da Farawa 2:15 ke cewa an sanya mutumin a cikin lambun don ’ya nomata, ya kuma lura da ita’ — shin waɗannan ra’ayoyin guda biyu suna gane muku kamar abu guda ɗaya, ko suna ƙara girma daban daban ga yadda sarrafa mai alhakin yake kama da shi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Kuuje ɗiɗi ɗuye ɗen ngoni: (1) laamude dow tagu — waɗde laamu e rewirde e nder tagu ngam Allah hono wakiiluuɓe mum nder duuniyaaru; nden (2) rokkirde e kaɗɗude — wolde Fuɗɗoode 2:15 woni nde nde gardotoowo koo naa ndema-ndemiiɗo, nde nelata e kaɗɗudande ko njokkaa maako bee amana. Kuuje ɗiɗi ɗuye ɗen ɗon haɗa, walaa ɗen ɗon sabu e yeeso: laamu ɗon hokkaama nder catal rokkirde e ɗaɓɓirgal, walaa nder cinngal. Dow no min mbaawa mballitaade kuuje ɗuye jonta — yimɓe heewɓe mbaawi wi’de e goonga ko ɓonn’ude muuɗol ngol yaɗetee, bonde kaaje e cinngal alaajiiji leydi ɗon hollita ko ngaaliidam garoɗam e gardagol ngal Allah yiɗi. Umarni ngal laamude waayi tabitirde walaa yaltinataake; ɗum woni weƴe ngel rokkirde tagu ngam Allah.
elements.questionHint2: Fuɗɗoode 1:26 ɗon hokka yimɓe baawɗe ngam ’laamude dow’ tagu, nde Fuɗɗoode 2:15 ɗon wi’a neɗɗo ɗon neli nder lammburu ngam ’rokkirde nde nden kaɗɗude’ — aɗa miijorto ko ɗiɗi ɗuye ɗi ñaawantaa ka hono fuu ɗum koo naa ɓe ɓura hollitde ɗimɓinde ngata nde no gardagol jolnitaaɗo wa’i?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The two responsibilities are: (1) to rule over creation — exercising authority and oversight on God’s behalf as His representatives in the world; and (2) to care for and maintain it — the language of Genesis 2:15 is that of a gardener or steward, tending and preserving what has been entrusted. These are complementary, not contradictory: the authority to rule is given in the context of responsible care, not exploitation. As for how well we are fulfilling these responsibilities today — many would argue honestly that the widespread environmental damage, species loss, and exploitation of natural resources reflects a failure of the stewardship God intended. The mandate to rule was never a licence to destroy; it was a call to serve creation on God’s behalf.
elements.questionHint3: Genesis 1:26 gives humans authority to ’rule over’ creation, while Genesis 2:15 says the man was placed in the garden to ’care for it and maintain it’ — do those two ideas feel like the same thing to you, or do they add different dimensions to what responsible stewardship looks like?

elements.text1: Gaskiya al’amari
elements.text2: Goongaaji mawɗi
elements.text3: Key truths

elements.text1:
A sa’ad da Allah ya halicci duniya, ya ambaci komai acikinta mai kyau ne, hakika ba bu wani aibu ko kaɗan tattare da halittar sa

’Allah kuwa ya ga dukan abin da ya yi, ga shi kuwa yana da kyau ƙwarai. Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na shida ke nan.’

Farawa 1:31

An halicce mu acikin kamannin Allah

’Haka nan fa, Allah ya halicci mutum cikin siffarsa, cikin siffar Allah, Allah ya halicci mutum, namiji da ta mace ya halicce su.’

Farawa 1:27
elements.text2:
Nde Allah tagi duuniyaaru, o wi’i koo ɗume fuu ’boɗɗum sanne’; goonga, ɗum walaa aibu sam.

’Allah yi’i ko o waɗi fuu, ndaa ɗum boɗɗum sanne. Kiikiide waɗi, mimmange waɗi, woni nyalaande joweego’o.’ — Fuɗɗoode 1:31

En tagaama bee nanndola Allah.

’Halla no Allah tagi neɗɗo bee sifa mum, bee sifa Allah o tagi mo. Worbe e rewɓe o tagi ɓe.’ — Fuɗɗoode 1:27
elements.text3:
When God made the world, He declared that it was ’very good’; indeed, it was perfect.

’God saw all that he had made—and it was very good! There was evening, and there was morning, the sixth day.’ — Genesis 1:31

We are made in God’s image.

’So God created mankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them; male and female he created them.’ — Genesis 1:27

elements.text1: Abubuwa tunani akai
elements.text2: Kuuje miijotooka
elements.text3: To think about

elements.question1: Yaya ka ke ji a yayin da ka san cewar an halicce ka a kamanni/surar Allah?
elements.question2: No a nanta nder ɓernde ma nde a anndi a tagaama bee nanndola Allah?
elements.question3: What does it mean to you to know that you are made in God’s image?

elements.question1: Tayaya zamu kula da abinda Allah ya halitta?
elements.question2: No min mbaawata kakkilanugo tagu Allah?
elements.question3: How can we look after God’s creation?

elements.text1: Acikin binciken mu a na ƙarshe mun fahimci cewar duniya da Allah ya hallita cikakkiya ce ba aibu acikin ta. Acikin wannan binciken zamu dubi akan zunubi; ta yarda ya zo duniya, da kuma cikas ɗin da ya kawo bisa duniya da kuma yarda Allah ke duban zunubi.
elements.text2: Nder janngde men ɓennunde, min nji’i duuniyaaru ndu Allah tagi boɗɗum sanne — walaa ko yoŋki. Nder janngde nde, min ɗon ndaara dow junuuba: hoto o iwi, ko o waɗata en, e no Allah jaaborta mo.
elements.text3: In the last study we saw that the world God made was very good — perfect. So what went wrong? In this study we look at sin: where it came from, what it does to us, and how God responds to it.

elements.text1: A wane matsayi duniya take yanzu?
elements.text2: No duuniyaaru wa’i jonta?
elements.text3: What state is the world in now?

elements.passageText1: <sup>14</sup>Na ga dukan abin da ake yi a duniya, duk aikin banza ne da ɓata lokaci kawai. <sup>15</sup>Ba za a iya miƙar da abin da ya tanƙware ba, ba kuma za a iya ƙidaya abin da ba shi ba.
elements.passageText3: <sup>14</sup>Na ga dukan abubuwan da ake yi a duniya, dukansu ba su da amfani, naushin iska ne kawai. <sup>15</sup>Abin da yake tanƙwararre ba zai miƙu ba;
elements.passageText5: <sup>14</sup>I have seen everything that is done under the sun, and look, all is vanity and chasing after wind. <sup>15</sup>What is bent cannot be straightened, and what is lacking cannot be counted.

elements.question1: Wane hoton rayuwa mai rubuta littafin Mai Hadishi ya zana game da rayuwa ƙarƙashin rana? Menene ra’ayin ka/ki game da bayaninsa?
elements.question2: Nandola ɗume winndoowo Waajotooɗo hollata dow mbuurnam les naange? No a jaaborta dow bayyanol maako?
elements.question3: What picture does the writer of Ecclesiastes paint of life under the sun? What is your reaction to his description?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Mai rubuta littafin ya zana hoton rayuwa a matsayin fanko da banza gaba ɗaya — duk ƙoƙarin ɗan adam da tafiyarsa na ’ɓata lokaci ne kamar gudu bayan iska,‘ wato neman abin da ba za a taɓa kama shi ko riƙe shi ba. Ayar 15 ta ƙara nuna baƙin cikin: abin da ya tanƙware ba za a miƙa shi ba, abin da ya ɓace ba za a iya ƙidaya shi har gaba ba. A taƙaice, duniya ta lalace ta yadda ƙoƙarin ɗan adam ba zai iya gyara ta ba, kuma hikimar ɗan adam ba za ta iya gano ko ma yawan lalacewarta ba. Mutane da yawa suna ganin wannan bayani yana da gaskiya mai ɓata rai — yana dace da ji wanda ke damun mu cewa komai da muka cimma, wani abu har yanzu yana ɓacewa a rayuwa da ake gudanar da ita ba tare da neman Allah ba.
elements.questionHint1: Harshen ’ɓata lokaci kamar gudu bayan iska’ yana nufin ƙoƙarin kama wani abu — me mai rubuta littafin yake nufi game da ko wannan ƙoƙari zai taɓa yi nasara, kuma shin kana/kina ganin haka yana dace da yadda mutane ke rayuwa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Winndoowo on hollata mbuurnam no nandu e hulaango e jaɓɓere — fuu ko neɗɗo ɗon waɗa e yaadu woni ’ɗon yiɗi moƴƴude hay batte ɗe ngalaa ma’ana,‘ ko misal feewngu ɓurde nganndinnde waɗuki ko heɓaaɗo alaa. Aayi 15 ɓeyda muusnaari: ko talɗinaangal waɗaaka miɗɗinaari, ko ɓooyungal waataa ƙidaade. Ko annde duuniyaaru boli no ɗum ngalaa ko neɗɗo ɗon waɗa feewnira ɗum, e hikma maako boo waataa hittinde ko ɗum waɗata tan. Yimɓe heewɓe mbayi ko e goonga faaɓaari ɗum — ɗum naata e miijo pooɗanngo ɗon nder meeɗen ceɓɓitiiɗo nde fuu ko men ngaɗi, goɗɗum tigi ɓooranaama nder yonki ngolɗum gudditanaaka Allah.
elements.questionHint2: Misal ’ɗon yiɗi moƴƴude hay batte’ hollata yiɗude moƴƴude — ɗume winndoowo on wi’ata dow ko ɗum e ko ɗum ɓuri siftaaɗum tan, e a miijii ko goonga nde mbuurnam yimɓe e ɗum ɗon ngoni?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The writer paints a picture of life as fundamentally empty and futile — all human activity and striving amounts to ’chasing after wind’, which is a vivid image for pursuing something that can never be caught or held. Verse 15 deepens the bleakness: what is bent cannot be straightened, and what is missing cannot even be fully counted. In other words, the world is broken in ways that human effort cannot fix and human wisdom cannot fully diagnose. Many people find this description uncomfortably honest — it matches the nagging sense that however much we achieve, something remains deeply unsatisfying about life lived without reference to God.
elements.questionHint3: The phrase ’chasing after wind’ describes trying to catch something — what is the point the writer is making about whether that chase can ever succeed, and do you think that rings true of how people experience life?

elements.passageText1: <sup>7</sup>Ba dama duniya ta ƙi ku, amma ni take ƙi, don na shaide ta a kan ayyukanta mugaye ne.
elements.passageText3: <sup>7</sup>Duniya ba za tă iya ƙinku ba, amma ni take ƙi domin ina shaida cewa ayyukanta mugaye ne.
elements.passageText4: <sup>7</sup>Jooni duuniyaaru heɓaayi ko wanyirta’on, ammaa e ndu wanyiiyam ngam e mi wi’a kuuɗe mayru ɗe kalluɗe.
elements.passageText5: <sup>7</sup>The world cannot hate you, but it hates me, because I testify about it that its deeds are evil.

elements.question1: Yesu yana cewa menene game da ayyukan duniya? Me ya sa kake/kike ganin duniya ta amseshi da ƙi?
elements.question2: Ɗume Iisa wolwi dow kuuɗe duuniyaaru? Ngam ɗume a miijata duuniyaaru njaabori mo bee kanyam?
elements.question3: What does Jesus say about the deeds of the world? Why do you think the world reacted to him with hatred?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu ya ce ayyukan duniya mugaye ne, kuma wannan shaida ta gaskiya ita ce dalilin da ya sa duniya take ƙinsa. Ba ya magana a matsayin wanda yake ƙin duniya, sai dai a matsayin wanda yake faɗar gaskiya game da ita — kuma gaskiyar ba a karɓarta. Ƙin nan martani ne ga bayyanawa: mutanen da suke son yadda suke rayuwa ba sa son wani ya ce musu ayyukansu mugaye ne. Wannan tsari mun san shi: mukan ƙi mutanen da suke nuna mana hoto game da halayenmu da ba mu son duba su. Don haka ƙiyayyar duniya ga Yesu ita kanta nau’in shaida ce cewa kimansa daidai ne.
elements.questionHint1: Yesu ya ce duniya tana ƙinsa daidai saboda ya shaida game da ayyukanta — me ya sa faɗar gaskiya game da halin wani zai iya sa shi ya amsa da ƙiyayya maimakon godiya?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu wi’i kuuɗe duuniyaaru kalluɗe, e ko seedaare nde goonga nde’e ɗum woni saɗaari nde duuniyaaru wanyiiyam. O ɗon haaldaa ko neɗɗo wanyuɗo duuniyaaru, ammaa ko neɗɗo wi’uɗo goonga dow ɗum — goongal boo haɓɓaayi. Kanyam ngal ngu’uttingo nder faayde: yimɓe ɓe ngoonɗini nguurndam maɓɓe naa’tam naataa yiɗude neɗɗo waɗa innde kallunde nde nguurndam maɓɓe. Ko ngikku mbaɗoyi men: en njenanaa yimɓe ɓe njaattonoo en gando e ngaɗuki ko kalluɗum ngolɗum ngalaa yiɗde ndaara ɗum. Saɗaari duuniyaaru dow Yeesu ko hoore maako seedaare wonde taƴol maako ɓuri goonga.
elements.questionHint2: Yeesu wi’i duuniyaaru wanyiiyam e hoore maako ngam o seedatii dow kuuɗe mayru — ngam ɗume wi’ugo goonga dow nguurndam neɗɗo waɗata mo yiɗugo wanyude ko ɓuri yettugo?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus says the world’s deeds are evil, and that this honest testimony is precisely the reason the world hates Him. He is not speaking as someone who hates the world, but as one who tells the truth about it — and the truth is unwelcome. The hatred is a reaction to exposure: people who are comfortable with their way of living do not want someone to name it as evil. This is a pattern we recognise: we tend to dislike people who hold a mirror up to behaviour we would rather not examine. The world’s hostility to Jesus is therefore itself a kind of evidence that His assessment is accurate.
elements.questionHint3: Jesus says the world hates Him specifically because He testifies about its deeds — why might telling the truth about someone’s behaviour cause them to react with hostility rather than gratitude?

elements.passageText1: <sup>19</sup>Ga shi, duk halitta tana marmari, tana ɗokin ganin bayyanar ’ya’yan Allah. <sup>20</sup>An sarayar da halitta ta zama banza, ba da nufinta ba, amma da nufin wanda ya sarayar da ita. Duk da haka, akwai sa zuciya, <sup>21</sup>gama za a ’yantar da halitta da kanta ma, daga bautar ruɓewa, domin ta sami ’yancin nan, na ɗaukaka na ’ya’yan Allah. <sup>22</sup>Mun dai san dukan halitta tana nishi, na shan azaba kuma irin ta mai naƙuda, har ya zuwa yanzu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>19</sup>Halitta tana jira da marmari ta ƙosa ta ga bayyanuwar ’ya’yan Allah. <sup>20</sup>Gama an sa halitta a cikin wulaƙanci, ba da son ta ba, sai dai ta wurin nufin wannan da ya mai da ita haka, da bege <sup>21</sup>cewa za a ’yantar da halitta kanta daga bautarta ga ruɓewa a kuma kawo ta cikin ’yancin ɗaukakar ’ya’yan Allah. <sup>22</sup>Mun san cewa dukan halitta tana nishi sai ka ce mace mai naƙuda har yă zuwa wannan lokaci.
elements.passageText4: <sup>19</sup>Fuu ko tagaa e heɗii e haaɓi ngam yi’a Allah wanginii ɓiɓɓe muuɗum. <sup>20</sup>Ngam ko tagaa wartiraama meere, naa’ ngam yidde maajum, ammaa non Allah yiɗiri ɗum waɗoroo. Fuu e non e woodi kammunde, <sup>21</sup>ngam ko tagaa e hoore maajum ɗum dimɗinteeɗum daga maccungaaku nyoluki, ɗum heɓa rimɗineeki e teddineeki ɗum yimɓe Allah njeyi. <sup>22</sup>En andi haa jooni fuu ko tagaa e otta ngam nawɗum no debbo piidoowo e nawɗum danyngol ottirta.
elements.passageText5: <sup>19</sup>For the creation eagerly waits for the revelation of the sons of God. <sup>20</sup>For the creation was subjected to futility—not willingly, but because of the one who subjected it—in hope <sup>21</sup>that the creation itself will also be set free from the bondage of decay into the glorious liberty of the children of God. <sup>22</sup>For we know that the whole creation groans and suffers together until now.

elements.question1: Me ya faru da halitta sakamakon zunubi? Menene Bulus ya ce halitta take jiran sa?
elements.question2: Ɗume fe’i dow tagu ngam ayyibe junuuba? Ɗume Bulus wi’ata tagu ɗon heɗita?
elements.question3: What has happened to creation as a result of sin? What does Paul say creation is waiting for?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce dukan halitta an sa ta a cikin wulaƙanci kuma tana bautar ruɓewa — ba ta aiki yadda Allah ya yi niyyar ta aiki ba. Harshen nishi da wahala yana nuna lalacewar da muke gani a duniyar halitta: cuta, mutuwa, bala’i, da ruɓewa. Muhimmin abu shi ne Bulus ya ce wannan ya faru ba da son halitta ba amma sakamakon zunubin ɗan adam — an ja halitta ta shiga Fadin Adam. Sai dai wannan sashe ba shi da ɗimbin baƙin ciki: halitta ba kawai tana shan wahala ba, tana jira da fata. Tana marmarin ganin ranar da ’ya’yan Allah za su bayyana da kuma halitta kanta za a ’yantar da ita daga ruɓewa zuwa sabon nau’in ’yanci mai ɗaukaka.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya ce an sa halitta a cikin wulaƙanci ’ba da son ta ba’ — me hakan ke gaya maka/ki game da ko lalacewar da ke cikin duniyar halitta laifin halitta ne ko sakamakon abin da ɗan adam ya yi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi’i ko tagaa fuu wartiraama e meere e woni e maccungaaku nyoluki — ɗum ɗon wadditaa no Allah yaawani ɗum waɗirde. Haalde otta e nawɗum hollata boli mbayi’oymi men nder tagaari: baasal, mayde, bonannde, e nyoluki. Ko toɗɗiri ɗum Bulus wi’i ɗum waɗaama naa’ ngam yidde tagaari, ammaa saɗaari hakke neɗɗo — tagaari nattiraama nder Yappi Muuɗum. Kono batu ngatu naa’ walaa tammunde tan: tagaari naa’ ɗon nawda tan, ɗon heɗii boo. Ɗon haaɓi e taƴol nde ɓiɓɓe Allah mbayyinee e tagaari hoore maajum dimɗinteeɗum daga nyoluki heɓa njaatam rimɗineeram e teddinoram.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus wi’i tagaari wartiraama e meere ’naa’ ngam yidde maajum’ — ɗume ɗum hollata dow ko boli gonɗi nder tagaari woni aayibe tagaari hoore maajum naa ko saɗaari ko neɗɗo waɗi?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says that the whole of creation has been subjected to futility and is in bondage to decay — it is not functioning as it was originally intended to. The language of groaning and suffering captures the brokenness we see in the natural world: disease, death, disaster, and deterioration. Crucially, Paul says this happened not willingly but as a consequence of human sin — creation was dragged into the Fall. However, the passage is not without hope: creation is not just suffering, it is waiting and longing. It eagerly anticipates the day when God’s children are fully revealed and creation itself is set free from decay into a new kind of glorious freedom.
elements.questionHint3: Paul says creation was subjected to futility ’not willingly’ — what does that tell you about whether the brokenness in the natural world is creation’s own fault or a consequence of something human beings did?

elements.text1: Ya Littafi Mai Tsarki ya yi bayani akan zunubi?
elements.text2: No Binndol Ceniiɗol siffirta junuuba?
elements.text3: How does the Bible describe sin?

elements.passageText1: <sup>6</sup>Dukanmu muna kama da tumakin da suka ɓata,
elements.passageText3: <sup>6</sup>Dukanmu, muna kama da tumakin da suka ɓata,
elements.passageText5: <sup>6</sup>All of us had wandered off like sheep; each of us had turned his own way. The Lord let the guilt of all of us fall on him.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Ishaya ya kwatanta yanayin zunubi ta hanyar amfani da hoton tumaki? Menene ma’anar ’ya juya hanyarsa’?
elements.question2: No Ishaya siffirta sifa junuuba bee huutorgol nandola baali? Ɗume ’huccugo laawol mum’ ma’anti?
elements.question3: How does Isaiah describe the nature of sin using the image of sheep? What does it mean to have ’turned his own way’?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Ba a san tumaki da fitinar da suke yi ba — suna ɓata. Ba sa tafiya da husuma; kawai suna yawo, suna bin duk abin da ya jawo hankalinsu a lokacin, ba su san yadda suka nange daga makiyayi ba kuma ba su sani irin haɗarin da suke ciki ba. Wannan kwatanci yana da ma’ana game da zunubi: mafi yawansa ba taurin kai ne ba sai dai a hankali ba tare da hankali ba ana tafiya daga Allah. ’Ya juya hanyarsa’ na nufin kowane mutum ya tsayar da hanyarsa ba ya bin Allah — ya mai da kansa iko akan yadda za a rayu. Kai tsaye maimakon Allah tsaye, wanda Littafi Mai Tsarki ke ganin shi ne kashin zunubi.
elements.questionHint1: Tumaki ba sa tawayen da aka sa wa ido a hankali — kawai suna yawo a hankali ba su fahimci yadda suke ɓacewa. Ta yaya irin wannan tafiyar hankali take kwatanta da yadda kake/kike tunanin zunubi yawanci, kuma shin yana canza fahimtar ka/ki game da shi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Baali naa’ɓe nganndinaa bee halkiinde sikkunde — ɗe ɗon njaha. Ɗe ngalaa ko ɗe ngaɗa bee yiite; ɗe ɗon njaha tan, ɗe ngoɗɗoo ko yiɗiri ɗe nder sahaa nde, ɗe annduaa no ɗe njaatinike daga gaardiiɗo e no suɗo ɗe nder ngol. Ko misal goongol dow junuuba: heewol junuuba naa’ ko halkiinde toownde tan, ammaa ko yaadu haɓɓundaaɗo haaɓundaaki daga nder Allah. ’Huccugo laawol mum’ ma’anti ko neɗɗo fuu yewtiti laawol muuɗum e wiide Allah — waɗii hoore maako ɓurnoowo haɓɓingol dow ko waɗiraa. Ko hoore mum faalitata e ko Allah faalitata, nde Binndol Ceniiɗol naata ko nguurngu junuuba.
elements.questionHint2: Baali naa’ɓe ngalaa ko ɗe ngaɗa bee halkiinde toownde — ɗe ɗon njaha haɓɓundaaki boo ngannduaa no ɗe ngooɗi ndi ɗon yalta. No yaadu ngolu e nannduri bee no a miijataa junuuba tigi, e ɗum accita faamde maa dow ɗum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Sheep are not known for dramatic rebellion — they wander. They do not storm off defiantly; they simply drift, following whatever attracts them in the moment, unaware of how far they have strayed from the shepherd and how much danger they are in. This is a telling image for sin: much of it is not dramatic wickedness but a gradual, unremarkable drifting away from God. To have ’turned his own way’ means each person has set their own course rather than following God’s — making themselves the authority on how to live. It is self-direction rather than God-direction, which the Bible understands as the essence of sin.
elements.questionHint3: Sheep don’t usually rebel dramatically — they just wander gradually without realising how lost they are becoming. How does that kind of slow drift compare to the way you might normally picture sin, and does it change your understanding of it?

elements.passageText1: <sup>43</sup>Sau da yawa Ubangiji yakan ceci jama’arsa,
elements.passageText3: <sup>43</sup>Sau da yawa ya cece su,
elements.passageText5: <sup>43</sup>Many times he delivered them, but they had a rebellious attitude and were degraded by their sin.

elements.question1: Menene wannan aya ke bayyanawa game da dagewar zunubin ɗan adam da kuma dagewar martanin Allah?
elements.question2: Ɗume ayaare nde hollata dow nannantalla junuuba neɗɗo e nannantalla jaabol Allah?
elements.question3: What does this verse reveal about both the persistence of human sin and the persistence of God’s response?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan aya tana bayyana cewa zunubin ɗan adam yana da dagewa mai ƙarfi: duk da an cece su ’sau da yawa,‘ mutanen sun koma kuma kuma da yawa ga halin taurin kai. Zunubi ba kawai jerin mawuyacin zaɓuka ne ba — aya tana bayyana yanayi na asali na taurin kai wanda yakan sake fitowa. Amma aya ta bayyana cewa martanin Allah yana da dagewa kamar haka: ya cece su ’sau da yawa,‘ ba sau ɗaya ba. Bai yar da su bayan karo na farko, ko na biyu, ko na goma ba. Tsari a gefen ɗan adam ƙasawa ce ta maimaita; tsarin a gefen Allah ceto ne na maimaita. Aya ta kuma lura da raunin da zunubi ke yi — yana rage mutane, ba kawai ta fuskar ɗabi’a ba amma gaba ɗayansu.
elements.questionHint1: Aya tana cewa Allah ya cece su ’sau da yawa’ — me kalmar ’yawa’ ke nuna game da yadda Allah ya yi haƙuri da gazawarsu ta maimaita, kuma tsarin ceto na maimaita yana gaya maka/ki menene game da halinsa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ayaare nde hollata junuuba neɗɗo ɓuuri haɓɓude: kono ɓe hisnaaɓe ’heewi laabi,‘ yimɓe ɓe ɓeydinii e ɓeydinii yaltude e ngorsi halkiinde. Junuuba naa’ ko ɓooyum laabi suɓaɓe kalluɗe tan — ayaare nde hollata yonki halkiinde gonɗo nder yaadu woni ɗon yaltirta hoore. Kono ayaare boo hollata jaabori Allah woni e haɓɓude hay ko nandori: o hisnii ɓe ’heewi laabi’, naa’ laabi gooto tan. O accaaki ɓe caggal faandere aranere, naa’ e ɓolaaɗe, naa’ e sappo. Nguurngu nder baɗal neɗɗo woni faandude caggal caggal; nguurngu nder baɗal Allah woni hisnaade laabi e laabi. Ayaare boo miiɗii teddingol junuuba waɗata — ɗum toɗɗinirta yimɓe on, naa’ haɓɓo tan, ammaa ko fuu ɓe ngoni.
elements.questionHint2: Ayaare nde wi’i Allah hisnii ɓe ’heewi laabi’ — ɗume mbele ’heewi’ hollata dow no Allah saɓɓori faandere maɓɓe caggal caggal, e no hisnaaɗo laabi e laabi hollata no o woni?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The verse reveals that human sin is stubbornly persistent: despite being delivered ’many times’, the people returned again and again to a rebellious attitude. Sin is not merely a series of isolated bad choices — the verse describes an underlying disposition of rebellion that keeps reasserting itself. But the verse equally reveals that God’s response is just as persistent: He delivered them ’many times’, not once. He did not give up after the first failure, or the second, or the tenth. The pattern on the human side is repeated failure; the pattern on God’s side is repeated rescue. The verse also notes the degrading effect of sin — it diminishes the people, not just morally but in their whole condition.
elements.questionHint3: The verse says God delivered them ’many times’ — what does the word ’many’ suggest about how patient God was with their repeated failures, and what does the pattern of repeated rescue tell you about His character?

elements.text1: Wane lokaci ne Zunubi ya fara zuwa duniya?
elements.text2: Nde junuuba fuɗɗi naatugo duuniyaaru?
elements.text3: When did sin first come into the world?

elements.passageText1: <sup>16</sup>Ubangiji Allah ya yi wa mutumin umarni, ya ce, ‘Kana da ’yanci ka ci daga kowane itace da yake a gonar, <sup>17</sup>amma daga itacen sanin nagarta da mugunta ba za ka ci ba, gama a ranar da ka ci shi za ka mutu lalle.
elements.passageText3: <sup>16</sup>Ubangiji  Allah ya umarci mutumin ya ce, Kana da ’yanci ka ci daga kowane itace a cikin lambun, <sup>17</sup>amma kada ka ci daga itacen sanin abin da yake mai kyau da abin da yake mugu, gama a ranar da ka ci shi, lalle za ka mutu.’
elements.passageText5: <sup>16</sup>Then the Lord God commanded the man, ‘You may freely eat fruit from every tree of the orchard, <sup>17</sup>but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, for when you eat from it you will surely die.’

elements.question1: Menene Allah ya umarce shi, kuma wane hukunci ya sanya ga rashin biyayya?
elements.question2: Ko Allah yamiri, nden ɗume o foti waɗugo to neɗɗo jaabaaki mo?
elements.question3: What had God commanded, and what consequence did he attach to disobedience?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Allah ya ba da izini mai karamci — mutumin zai iya cin ’ya’yan kowane itacen gonar da yardar rai — tare da haramci ɗaya a sarari: ya kamata kada ya ci daga itacen sanin nagarta da mugunta. Umarnin ba mai tsanani ba ne ko kadan; itacen da aka hana ɗaya yana tsaye a bango na tanadi mai yawa. Hukuncin da aka sanya ga rashin biyayya shi ne mutuwa: ‘za ka mutu lalle.’ Wannan ba barazana ba ce sai dai bayyana sakamakon — ketare wannan iyaka zai kawo mutuwa a duniya da ta kasance ba ta san mutuwa ba. Umarnin ya kafa cewa kalmar Allah iyaka ce ta rayuwa, kuma fita daga cikinta fita ce zuwa ga mutuwa.
elements.questionHint1: Kafin ya ba da haramcin ɗaya, Allah ya ce mutumin zai iya cin ’ya’yan duk sauran itatuwan da yardar rai — me wannan tsarin karamci yana gaya maka/ki game da irin Allah wanda yake sa wannan iyaka, kuma shin yana canza yadda kake/kike karanta haramcin ɗaya?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Allah hokki yamiroore yaajunde — gorko on waawi nyaami ɓiɓɓe leɗɗe fuu nder laabu nguu bee fartaŋŋe — fawaade e haramtaare gooto sanne: o foti nyaama daga leɗɗe gande ko wooɗi e ko kalluɗi. Yamiroore nde naa’ ɓurtii goɗɗum e ɓurgol; leetel haramteeɗel boo gonngo nder teddungol tanɗingol heewngol. Saɗaari nde nattiraama e toɗɗondirugol yamiroore nde woni mayde: ‘laaɓi-laaɓi a mayraa.’ Naa’ cuɗal tan kono haala saɗaari — yartugo iyol ngol na’ata mayde nder duuniyaaru nde ngolɗum waɗaaka. Yamiroore nde waɗi weltini ko haala Allah woni iyol nguurndam, e seltude daga ɗum woni seltude to mayde.
elements.questionHint2: Caggal hokki haramtaare nde, Allah wi’i gorko on waawi nyaami daga fuu leɗɗe goɗɗe bee fartaŋŋe — ɗume faaynude nguu hollata dow no Allah wa’i nde o fuɗɗita iyol ngol, e ɗum accita fammude haramtaare gooto nde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: God gave a generous permission — the man could freely eat from every tree of the orchard — alongside a single clear prohibition: he must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil. The command was not restrictive in any harsh sense; the one forbidden tree was set against the backdrop of abundant provision. The consequence attached to disobedience was death: ‘you will surely die.’ This was not a threat but a statement of consequence — crossing this boundary would bring death into a world where it had not previously existed. The command established that God’s word was the boundary of life, and to step outside it was to step towards death.
elements.questionHint3: Before giving the one prohibition, God said the man could ’freely eat’ from every other tree — what does that generous framing tell you about the kind of God who was setting this boundary, and does that change how you read the single restriction?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Maciji ya fi kowace dabba da Ubangiji Allah ya yi wayo. Ya ce wa matar, ‘Ko Allah ya ce, ‘Ba za ku ci daga wani itacen da yake a gonar ba?’ ” <sup>2</sup>Sai matar ta ce wa macijin, “Mā iya ci daga cikin itatuwan gonar, <sup>3</sup>amma Allah ya ce, ‘Ba za ku ci daga cikin ’ya’yan itacen da yake tsakiyar gonar ba, ba za ku taɓa shi ba, don kada ku mutu.’ ” <sup>4</sup>Amma macijin ya ce wa matar, “Hakika ba za ku mutu ba. <sup>5</sup>Gama Allah ya sani a ranar da kuka ci daga itacen nan idanunku za su buɗe, za ku kuwa zama kamar Allah, ku san nagarta da mugunta.’ <sup>6</sup>Matar ta ga yadda itacen yana da kyau, ‘ya’yansa kuma kyawawa, abin sha’awa ne kuma ga ido, abin marmari ne domin ba da hikima, ta tsinka daga ’ya’yansa, ta kuwa ci, ta kuma bai wa mijinta waɗansu, shi kuma ya ci. <sup>7</sup>Sai dukansu biyu idanunsu suka buɗe, sa’an nan suka gane tsirara suke, sai suka samo ganyayen ɓaure suka ɗinɗinka suka yi wa kansu sutura.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>To, maciji dai ya fi kowane a cikin namun jejin da  Ubangiji  Allah ya yi wayo. Ya ce wa macen, ‘Tabbatacce ne Allah ya ce, ‘Kada ku ci daga kowane itace a lambu’?” <sup>2</sup>Macen ta ce wa macijin, “Za mu iya ci daga ’ya’ya itatuwa a lambun, <sup>3</sup>amma Allah ya ce, ‘Kada ku ci daga ’ya’yan itacen da yake tsakiyar lambu, kada kuma ku taɓa shi, in ba haka ba kuwa za ku mutu.’ ” <sup>4</sup>Macji ya ce wa macen, “Tabbatacce ba za ku mutu ba. <sup>5</sup>Gama Allah ya san cewa sa’ad da kuka ci daga itacen idanunku za su buɗe, za ku kuma zama kamar Allah, ku san abin da yake mai kyau da abin da yake mugu.’ <sup>6</sup>Sa’ad da macen ta ga ’ya’yan itacen suna da kyau don abinci, abin sha’awa ga ido, abin marmari kuma don samun hikima, sai ta tsinka ta ci, ta kuma ba da waɗansu wa mijinta wanda yake tare da ita, shi ma ya ci. <sup>7</sup>Sa’an nan idanun dukansu biyu suka buɗe, suka kuma gane tsirara suke; saboda haka suka ɗinɗinka ganyayen ɓaure suka yi wa kansu sutura.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>Now the serpent was more shrewd than any of the wild animals the Lord God had made. He said to the woman, ‘Is it really true that God said, ‘You must not eat from any tree of the orchard’?’. . . <sup>6</sup>When the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was desirable for imparting wisdom, she took some of its fruit and ate; and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate. <sup>7</sup>Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they knew they were naked; so they sewed fig leaves together and made coverings for themselves.

elements.question1: Wane dabara ne maciji ya yi amfani da ita don jaraba matar? Menene ya ƙarfafa ta ta ci ’ya’yan itacen?
elements.question2: Ɗume maciji waɗi ngam jartugo debbo o? Ɗume neɗɗi debbo o haa o nyami ɓiɓɓe leɗɗe ɗen?
elements.question3: What strategy did the serpent use to tempt the woman? What motivated her to eat the fruit?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Dabara ta maciji ita ce shuka shakku game da kalmar Allah — ta fara da tambaya wanda ta canza ainihin abin da Allah ya ce (‘Ko Allah ya ce ba za ku ci daga wani itace a gonar ba?’), sannan ya nuna cewa Allah na da niyya ta boye a cikin umarnin. Dabara ta tafi daga tambaya game da kalmar Allah zuwa tambaya game da alherin Allah. Dalilai uku suka ƙarfafa matar ta ci ’ya’yan itacen: ta ga shi mai kyau don abinci (sha’awar jiki), abin farha ga ido (son kyau), da kuma abin marmari domin samun hikima (niyyar zama fiye da haka). Waɗannan dalilai uku — sha’awa, kyau, da girman kai — suna wakiltar tsarin jaraba na ɗan adam wanda ya ci gaba har yau.
elements.questionHint1: Mataki na farko na macijin ba shi ne cewa ’saɓa wa Allah’ ba sai dai ya yi tambaya wanda ya sa a yi shakku game da abin da Allah ya ce — me ya sa sa mutum ya yi shakku game da amincin kalmar Allah mataki mai tasiri na farko don sa shi ya saɓa wa Allah?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Nguurngu mboɗɓe woni fuɗɗugo shakkugo dow haala Allah — fuɗɗugo e ƴamre nde woɗɗitii ko Allah haali goonga (‘Ko goonga Allah wi’i, ha on nyaama daga fuu leɗɗe nder laabu?’), nden faayi wi’ugo Allah nani niyye goɗɗe nder yamiroore nde. Nguurngu nguu yalti daga shakkugo dow haala Allah e shakkugo dow moƴƴere Allah. Niyye ɗiɗi sarɗi debbo on nyamugo ɓiɓɓe leɗɗe ɗen: o yi’i ɗum moƴɓi nyamugo (hankuure reedu), belni gite (hiɗde kyawu), e kadi yiɗi ngam heɓugo hikma (ñaagaari heɓugo fii heewiri). Ɗe niyye ɗiɗi ɗe tati — hankuure, kyawu, e mawninki hoore — hollata nguurngu jarraare neɗɗo ɓurngo fayde e ɓuri siftaade.
elements.questionHint2: Fetel araneel mboɗɓe naa’ wi’ugo ’saɓu Allah’ tan, ammaa ƴamrugo ƴamre nde ɓoorniti ko Allah wi’i — ngam ɗume waɗugo neɗɗo shakkugo dow goonga haala Allah woni fetel feewnuɗo araneetol ngam waɗugo mo saɓu ɗum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The serpent’s strategy was to sow doubt about God’s word — beginning with a question that subtly distorted what God had actually said (‘Did God really say you must not eat from any tree?’), and then implying that God had an ulterior motive in giving the command. The tactic moved from questioning God’s word to questioning God’s goodness. The woman’s motivations for eating the fruit were threefold: she saw it was good for food (physical appetite), a delight to the eyes (aesthetic desire), and desirable for gaining wisdom (the ambition to be more). These three motivations — appetite, beauty, and pride — represent a recognisably human pattern of temptation that has continued ever since.
elements.questionHint3: The serpent’s first move was not to say ’disobey God’ but to ask a question that cast doubt on what God had said — why is making someone question the reliability of God’s word an effective first step towards getting them to disobey it?

elements.text1: Menene zunubi ke haddasa zuwan sa? Daga ina ma ya zo?
elements.text2: Ɗume junuuba woni? Hoto o iwi?
elements.text3: What does sin involve? Where does it come from?

elements.passageText1: <sup>14</sup>Sai ya sāke kiran jama’a, yă ce musu, ‘Ku saurare ni dukanku, ku kuma fahimta. <sup>15</sup>Ba abin da yake shiga mutum daga waje yă ƙazanta shi, sai abin da yake fita, yake ƙazanta shi. <sup>16</sup>[Duk mai kunnen ji, yă ji.]’ <sup>17</sup>Bayan rabuwarsa da taron, da ya shiga gida, almajiransa suka tambaye shi ma’anar misalin. <sup>18</sup>Ya ce musu, ‘Ashe, ku ma ba ku da fahimta ne?
