Ashe, ba ku gane ba, kome ya shiga mutum daga waje, ba yadda zai ƙazanta shi, <sup>19</sup>tun da yake ba zuciya tasa ya shiga ba, cikinsa ne, ta haka kuma zai fita?’ Ta haka Yesu ya halatta kowane irin abinci. <sup>20</sup>Ya kuma ce, ‘Abin da yake fita daga cikin mutum yake ƙazanta shi. <sup>21</sup>Don daga ciki ne, wato daga zuciyar mutum, mugayen tunani yake fitowa, kamar su fasikanci, da sata, da kisankai, da zina, <sup>22</sup>da kwaɗayi, da mugunta, da ha’inci, da fajirci, da kishi, da yanke, da girmankai, da kuma wauta. <sup>23</sup>Duk waɗannan mugayen abubuwa daga ciki suke fitowa, suna kuwa ƙazanta mutum.’
elements.passageText3: <sup>14</sup>Sai Yesu ya sāke kiran taron wurinsa ya ce, ‘Ku saurare ni dukanku, ku kuma fahimci wannan. <sup>15</sup>Ba abin da yake shiga mutum daga waje ba ne yake ƙazantar da shi. Sai dai abin da ya fita daga cikin mutum ne, yake ƙazantar da shi.’ Waɗansu rubuce-rubucen hannu na farko-farko suna da  ’marar tsabta.’  <sup>16</sup>Duk mai kunnuwan ji, yă ji <sup>17</sup>Bayan ya bar taron, ya shiga gida, sai almajiransa suka tambaye shi ma’anar wannan misali. <sup>18</sup>Ya ce, ‘Ashe, ba ku da saurin ganewa? Ba ku gane cewa ba abin da yake shiga mutum daga waje ba ne yake ƙazantar da shi. <sup>19</sup>Gama ba ya shiga cikin zuciyarsa, sai dai cikin tumbinsa, sa’an nan kuma yă fita daga jikinsa.’ (Da faɗin haka, Yesu ya nuna tsabtar kowane irin abinci.) <sup>20</sup>Ya ci gaba, ‘Abin da yake fitowa daga mutum ne yake ƙazantar da shi. <sup>21</sup>Gama daga ciki, daga zuciyar mutane, mugayen tunani suke fitowa, fasikanci, sata, kisankai, zina, <sup>22</sup>kwaɗayi, ƙeta, ruɗu, ƙazantaccen sha’awa, kishi, ɓata suna, girman kai da kuma wauta. <sup>23</sup>Duk waɗannan mugayen abubuwa daga cikin suke fitowa, suna kuma ƙazantar da mutum.’
elements.passageText4: <sup>14</sup>Sey Yeesu fuɗɗiti noddi yimɓe hawrutuɓe, o wi’i, ‘Kettindee, on fuu, paamon. <sup>17</sup>Nde o sendiri e yimɓe fuu, o natti wuro, tokkuɓemo ƴamimo ma’ana ekkitinol ngo’ol. <sup>18</sup>O nootii o wi’i, ‘Onon maa on paamaayi ma’ana ɗu’um? On andaa walaa ko nattata neɗɗo daga yaasi wartiramo mo laaɓaa? <sup>19</sup>Ngam naa’ nder ɓernde ɗum nattata, ammaa nder reedu, nden ɗum wurto ɓandu.’ (Nde Yeesu wi’i ɗu’um, o hollii koo nduye nyamndu ndu lamndu.) <sup>20</sup>O ɓeydi wi’uki, ‘Ko wurtotoo daga nder ɓernde neɗɗo, kanjum waɗata neɗɗo warta mo laaɓaa yeeso Allah. <sup>21</sup>Ngam daga nder ɓernde neɗɗo nyumo kallungo wurtoytoo, e waɗuki njeenu hakkunde yimɓe ɓe ngalaa teegal, e nguyka, e warhoore, e njeenu, <sup>22</sup>e suuno fii duuniyaaru, e halleende, e ƴoyre kallunde, e fuu kuuɗe kalluɗe ɗe ɓerɗe maɓɓe ngiɗi, e lawliiru, e wonnuki inde neɗɗo, e mawninki hoore, e wanyuki waɗuki ko wooɗi. <sup>23</sup>Fuu ɗe’e kuuɗe kalluɗe daga nder ɓernde ɗe mburtoytoo ɗe ngartira neɗɗo mo laaɓaa yeeso Allah.’
elements.passageText5: <sup>14</sup>Then he called the crowd again and said to them, ‘Listen to me, everyone, and understand. <sup>15</sup>There is nothing outside of a person that can defile him by going into him. Rather, it is what comes out of a person that defiles him.’ . . . <sup>21</sup>For from within, out of the human heart, come evil ideas, sexual immorality, <mark>theft</mark>, murder, <sup>22</sup>adultery, greed, evil, deceit, debauchery, envy, slander, pride, and folly. <sup>23</sup>All these evils come from within and defile a person.’

elements.question1: A cewar Yesu, daga ina zunubi ke fitowa? Ta yaya wannan ke ƙalubalanci ra’ayin cewa muhallinmu ko yadda aka tashe mu shi ne babban dalili na halinmu?
elements.question2: Bana ko Iisa wolwi, hoto junuuba iwi? No ɗu’um jaɓitata miijo wonki duuniyaaru naa’ ne’idi woni asuuli kuuɗe men?
elements.question3: According to Jesus, where does sin originate? How does this challenge the idea that our environment or upbringing is primarily responsible for our behaviour?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu ya ƙayyade asalin zunubi ƙwarai daga cikin zuciyar ɗan adam. Mugunta ba ta shiga cikinmu daga waje ba da farko — tana fitowa daga cikinmu. Wannan yana ƙalubalanci ra’ayin mashahuri cewa mutane masu kyau ne a zahiri kuma kawai muhallin wuya, tarbiyyar mara kyau, ko yanayin karya na waje ne ke sa su mugu. Ko da yake waɗannan abubuwa na iya tsara yadda zunubi ke bayyana kansa, Yesu ya ce ba su ne asalinsa ba. Asalin shine zuciyar ɗan adam da kanta. Wannan shi ne musunbari mai tada tunani domin yana nufin ba za a iya warware matsalar da kawai inganta yanayi, ilimi, ko tsarin zamantakewa ba — tushe yana cikin mutum, kuma yana buƙatar mafita da ta fito daga ciki.
elements.questionHint1: Mutane da yawa suna gaskata cewa idan ka ba wa kowa tarbiyyar kyau da muhallin adalci, mutane za su yi kyau da ɗabi’a ta halitta — ta yaya tantauna Yesu a wannan sashe ta yarda ko ta ƙi yarda da wannan ra’ayi, kuma menene tasirin haka ko haka?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu holliti asli junuuba woni nder ɓernde neɗɗo. Ko kalluɗum naa’ nattataa nder meeɗen daga yaasi — ɗum wurtotoo daga nder meeɗen. Ɗum jaɓitata miijo woɗɗitiingo wonde yimɓe boɗɓe e nder maɓɓe, sey tigi e boli muusum, tasee muusum naa’ duuniyaaru bollunde nde ngartira ɗe nder halkiinde. Ko ɗe ndonngondiri faaynooɗi nguurngu junuuba, Yeesu wi’i naa’ ɗen asliji ɗen. Asliji woni ɓernde neɗɗo hoore maajum. Ɗum muusna ngam wi’ata ko mbaasi waɗude kawɗol moƴɓude yonkire, ekkolal naa’ siforde yimiijo tan ndimɗinta — hoore wiinire woni nder neɗɗo, ɗum ɓuri yiɗude hisna daga nder hoore maajum.
elements.questionHint2: Yimɓe heewɓe mbayi ko ɗum neɗɗo rokki tasee moƴɓere e duuniyaaru laawɗere, yimɓe njaɓirta moƴɓude e nder maɓɓe tan — no Yeesu hollita nder batu ngatu yidde naa’ jaaɓaande e miijo ngoo, e ɗume ɗum waɗata?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus locates the origin of sin firmly within the human heart. Evil does not primarily come into us from outside — it comes out of us from within. This directly challenges the popular idea that people are basically good and only made bad by difficult circumstances, poor upbringing, or a corrupt environment. While those factors may shape how sin expresses itself, Jesus says they are not its source. The source is the human heart itself. This is a sobering diagnosis because it means the problem cannot be solved simply by improving conditions, education, or social structures — the root is internal, and it requires an internal solution.
elements.questionHint3: Many people believe that if you could give everyone a good upbringing and a fair environment, people would naturally behave well — how does Jesus’ diagnosis in this passage agree or disagree with that assumption, and what are the implications either way?

elements.question: What comes to mind when you hear the word sin?
elements.answer: For many people, ‘sin’ brings to mind a list of particularly bad actions — murder, theft, serious crimes. By that measure, most of us feel fairly confident we are not sinners. But the Bible uses the word much more broadly. Sin is any thought, word, or action that falls short of God’s standard — and, more than that, it is an attitude of the heart that places self at the centre rather than God. It is rebellion against God’s authority over our lives, often in quiet, respectable ways. When we understand sin this way, the Bible’s claim that ’all have sinned’ (Romans 3:23) starts to make a great deal more sense.

elements.text1: Ya Allah ke ji game da zunubi?
elements.text2: No Allah miijata dow junuuba?
elements.text3: How does God feel about sin?

elements.passageText1: <sup>5</sup>Ubangiji kuwa ya ga muguntar mutum ta ƙasaita a duniya, dukan zace-zacen tunanin zuciyarsa kuma mugunta ne kullayaumin. <sup>6</sup>Ubangiji ya damu da ya yi mutum a duniya, abin ya ɓata masa zuciya ƙwarai.
elements.passageText3: <sup>5</sup>  Ubangiji  kuwa ya lura cewa muguntar mutum ta yi yawa a duniya, kuma kowane abin da yake tunani a zuciyarsa mugu ne kawai a kowanne lokaci. <sup>6</sup>Ubangiji  ya damu da ya yi mutum a duniya, abin ya zafe shi ƙwarai.
elements.passageText5: <sup>5</sup>But the Lord saw that the wickedness of humankind had become great on the earth. Every inclination of the thoughts of their minds was only evil all the time. <sup>6</sup>The Lord regretted that he had made humankind on the earth, and he was highly offended.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Allah ke amsa da ji game da mugunta ɗan adam? Menene wannan ke gaya mana game da yadda Allah ke ɗaukar zunubi da muhimmanci?
elements.question2: No Allah jaaborta bee kanyam haa kalluɗum neɗɗo? Ɗume ɗu’um hollata en dow no Allah ƴettiri junuuba bee caahi?
elements.question3: How does God respond emotionally to human wickedness? What does this tell us about how seriously God takes sin?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Martanin Allah na baƙin ciki ne mai zurfi da fushin ɓacin rai — ya damu da ya yi ɗan adam kuma ya ɓace masa ƙwarai game da abin da ya gani. Waɗannan su ne kalmomin ƙwazo masu ji waɗanda ke gaya mana cewa zunubi ba komai ne a gare Allah ba. Ba mai kallo ne kawai wanda ke ɗaga kafaɗa game da mugunta ɗan adam ba; abin yana baƙin cikinsa da gaske. Wannan yana da muhimmanci domin yana nuna cewa zunubi ba kawai karya ƙa’idoji ba ne — yana karya dangantaka. Allah ya yi ɗan adam domin kusanci da kansa, kuma ganin su da gaba ɗayansu an ba da su ga mugunta kawai yana haifar masa da ciwon gaske. Zurfin martaninsa na ji shine ma’aunin yadda yake ɗaukar zunubi da muhimmanci.
elements.questionHint1: Wannan sashe ya ce Allah ya ’damu’ da ya yi ɗan adam kuma ya ’ɓata masa zuciya ƙwarai’ — me waɗannan kalmomin masu ji ke gaya maka/ki game da ko Allah yana ganin zunubi a matsayin ɗan tsangwami ko wani abu da ya shafi shi da gaske?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Jaabori Allah woni nawɗum toowndi e muusneeki — o damu waɗugo yimɓe e duuniyaaru e muusnaama ɗum ɓurdi dow ko o yi’i. Ko kelmeeji toowndi njanginɗi junuuba naa’ woni goɗɗum walaa ma’ana Allah. O naa’ neɗɗo ndaaroowo tan ngolɗum ɓurtata dow halluɗum yimɓe; ɗum waɗata mo nawɗum tigi. Ɗum waɗi ngam hollata junuuba naa’ ɓe ɓurta ƴoyre dow laawol tan — ɗum bollita cimooje. Allah tagi neɗɗo ngam seddeendo e muuɗum hoore maajum, e yi’ugo ɓe nattinaaɓe e halkiinde tan (‘halkiinde tan sahaa fuu’) waɗata mo nawɗum goonga. Jomtannde nawɗum maako hollata jom ɓurtu haɓɓude ƴettugo junuuba bee caahi.
elements.questionHint2: Baatu nguu wi’i Allah ’damu’ waɗugo yimɓe e muunii maɓɓe toowundi sanne — ɗume kelmeeji nawɗi ɗii hollata dow ko Allah naata junuuba ko goɗɗum walaa ma’ana naa ko toɗɗi mo tigi?
elements.sampleAnswer3: God’s response is one of grief and deep offence — He regretted making humankind and was highly offended by what He saw. These are strong emotional terms that tell us sin is not a matter of indifference to God. He is not a detached observer who shrugs at human wickedness; He is personally grieved by it. This matters because it shows that sin is not primarily about breaking rules — it is about breaking relationship. God made human beings for closeness with Himself, and seeing them given over entirely to evil (‘only evil all the time’) causes Him genuine pain. The depth of His emotional response is a measure of how seriously He takes sin.
elements.questionHint3: The passage says God ’regretted’ making humankind and was ’highly offended’ — what do those emotional words tell you about whether God sees sin as a minor inconvenience or something that genuinely affects Him?

elements.passageText1: <sup>18</sup>Gama ana bayyana fushin Allah daga Sama, a kan dukan rashin bin Allah, da aikin mugunta na mutanen da suke danne gaskiya, ta aikin muguntarsu,
elements.passageText3: <sup>18</sup>Ana bayyana fushin Allah daga sama a kan dukan rashin sanin Allah da kuma muguntar mutanen da suke danne gaskiya ta wurin muguntarsu,
elements.passageText4: <sup>18</sup>Monnere Allah e wanga daga dow dow yimɓe ɓe kulataa Allah e dow kuuɗe maɓɓe kalluɗe ɗe ɓe nyo’’irta goonga.
elements.passageText5: <sup>18</sup>For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of people who suppress the truth by their unrighteousness,

elements.question1: Bulus ya ce menene ake bayyanawa daga sama? Menene ma’anar ’danne gaskiya’?
elements.question2: Ɗume Bulus wi’i wanginaama daga dow? Ɗume ’nyo’’ugo goonga’ ma’anti?
elements.question3: What does Paul say is revealed from heaven? What does it mean to ’suppress the truth’?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce ana bayyana fushin Allah daga sama game da dukan rashin bin Allah da mugunta. Wannan ba barazana ta gaba kawai ba ne — wani abu da ake bayyanawa yanzu — kin Allah mai tsarki da ka’idar zunubi aiki ne da yake aiki a duniya a yanzu. ’Danne gaskiya’ na nufin riƙe ƙasa ko hana abin da mutum ya san shi na gaskiya — ba jahilci ba, amma jurewa da gangan gaskiyar da take samuwa. Muhawarar Bulus a ayoyin da ke kewaye ita ce cewa kasancewar Allah da yanayin sa suna gani a cikin halitta, don haka ba za mutane su sami uzuri ba. Danne gaskiya na nufin zaɓar ƙin abin da za a iya sani game da Allah don a rayu ba tare da neman shi ba.
elements.questionHint1: Kalmar ’danne’ tana nuna cewa gaskiyar tana nan kuma an san ta — ba za a iya danne wani abu da ba a taɓa samu ba. Menene wannan ke gaya maka/ki game da zato Bulus yana yi game da nawa mutane suka riga sani game da Allah kafin a gaya musu bishara?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi’i monnere Allah e wanga daga dow dow fuu kulataa Allah e kalluɗum yimɓe. Naa’ cuɗal tan ngolɗum woni — goɗɗum e hollitaaka jooni — humpitaare Allah ceniiɗo e laamu hakke woni goonga gonɗo e duuniyaaru jooni. ’Nyo’ugo goonga’ ma’anti haɓɓugo naa’ reƴɓugo ko neɗɗo andaa goonga — naa’ ko anndaa tan, ammaa sabataari dow goonga ngolɗum heɓaaɗo. Muhawaara Bulus nder ayaaɗe dawruɗe nde woni wonde Allah e yiɗaari maako hollitaa e nder tagaari, saɗi yimɓe ngalaa caaɗi. Nyo’ugo goonga ma’anti suɓugo reƴɓugo ko waawi anndaago dow Allah ngam nguurnude e walaa ƴiiɓugo mo.
elements.questionHint2: Mbele ’nyo’ugo’ hollata goonga gonɗo e heɓaaɗo — waataa nyo’ugo goɗɗum ngolɗum mbele on njii ɗum. Ɗume ɗum hollata dow ko Bulus miijata dow heewum yimɓe ɓe ngandi dow Allah caggal ɓe nanngi Habaaru Moƴɓere?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says that the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness. This is not a future threat only but something already being revealed — God’s settled, holy opposition to sin is an active reality in the world now. To ’suppress the truth’ means to hold down or restrain what one knows to be true — not ignorance, but deliberate resistance to truth that is available. Paul’s argument in the surrounding verses is that the existence and nature of God is visible in creation, so that people are without excuse. Suppressing the truth means choosing to push away what can be known about God in order to live without reference to Him.
elements.questionHint3: The word ’suppress’ implies that the truth is already present and known — you cannot suppress something you have never encountered. What does that tell you about Paul’s assumption regarding how much people already know about God before they are told the gospel?

elements.text1: A duniya yau wanene ya ke da kasawa ta zunubi
elements.text2: Nder duuniyaaru jonta, moye woodi ayyibe junuuba?
elements.text3: In the world today, who is guilty of sinning?

elements.passageText1: <sup>9</sup>To, ƙaƙa? Mu Yahudawa mun fi sauran ne? A’a, ko kaɗan! Don dā ma mun ɗora wa Yahudawa da al’ummai laifi, cewa dukkansu zunubi yana iko da su. <sup>10</sup>Kamar yadda yake a rubuce cewa, <sup>11</sup>Babu wani mai fahimta, babu wani mai neman Allah. <sup>12</sup>Duk sun bauɗe, sun zama marasa amfani baki ɗaya, <sup>23</sup>gama ’yan adam duka sun yi zunubi, sun kasa kuma ga ɗaukakar Allah.
elements.passageText3: <sup>9</sup>To, me za mu ce ke nan? Mun fi su ke nan? Ina, ko kaɗan! Mun riga mun zartar cewa da Yahudawa da Al’ummai duk suna a ƙarƙarshin zunubi. <sup>10</sup>Kamar yadda yake a rubuce cewa, <sup>11</sup>babu wani mai fahimta, <sup>12</sup>Duk sun kauce gaba ɗaya, <sup>23</sup>gama duka sun yi zunubi suka kuma kāsa ga ɗaukakar Allah,
elements.passageText4: <sup>9</sup>Too, ɗume mbi’eten? Enen Yahuudanko’en en ɓurii yimɓe naa’ Yahuudanko’en won ɗon? Goonga naa’ non. Mi yaadake mi hollii wiiki Yahuudanko’en e yimɓe naa’ Yahuudanko’en ɓe fuu ley laamu hakke ɓe ngoni. <sup>10</sup>Non no ɗum windiraa, <sup>11</sup>walaa gooduɗo faamu, <sup>12</sup>Yimɓe fuu ngaylitanii Allah ɓaawo, ɓe ngartii walaa ko ɓe nafi. <sup>23</sup>Koo moye neɗɗo waɗi hakke woɗɗitake ko Allah yiɗi o waɗa.
elements.passageText5: <sup>9</sup>What then? Are we better off? Certainly not, for we have already charged that Jews and Greeks alike are all under sin, <sup>10</sup>just as it is written: ‘There is no one righteous, not even one, <sup>11</sup>there is no one who understands, there is no one who seeks God. <sup>12</sup>All have turned away, together they have become worthless; there is no one who shows kindness, not even one.’ . . . <sup>23</sup>for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,

elements.question1: Bulus ya ce babu mai adalci ko ɗaya. Ta yaya kake/kike amsa wannan ikirari? Shin yana ji kamar da yawa?
elements.question2: Bulus wi’i walaa neɗɗo laaɓuɗo. No njaabor-ɗaa ɗum? A miijake ɗum ɓurtii ɗuɗugo naa?
elements.question3: Paul says there is no one who is righteous. How do you respond to that claim? Does it feel too sweeping?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Ikirarin Bulus yana da ƙarko kuma gama-gari da gaske — ‘ko ɗaya ba’ ba ya barin kowany banda. Martanin da mutane ke yi da ɗabi’a shi ne wannan yana ji kamar yawa: tabbas wasu mutane masu kyau ne da gaske? Amma Bulus ba ya cewa ba wanda ke yi wa wani abu na karamci ko abin yabo. Yana cewa ba wanda, a ma’aunin Allah, mai adalci ne — ba wanda ke neman Allah da cikakken rai, ba wanda yake aikata abin da yake daidai a idon Allah kullayaumi. Harshen ’sun kasa ga ɗaukakar Allah’ a aya ta 23 yana kama wannan: ba cewa wasu suna kasa kaɗan wasu kuma da yawa — mu duka muna kasa. Manufar ba yin lissafin halin ɗan adam ba ne sai dai kafa cewa matsalar zunubi ta gama kowa ne, wanda yake zama muhimmin tushe don fahimtar dalilin da ya sa bishara ta shafi kowa.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya ce ’ko ɗaya ba’ mai adalci — ba yana kwatanta mutane da junansu ba sai dai da ma’aunin Allah. Ta yaya ƙarshe za ta canza idan sandararren abu shine cikakkiyar halin Allah maimakon kwatanci da sauran ɗan adam?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Fawi Bulus woni ɓurtor e jamaa goonga — ‘hay gooto’ naa’ faayde hay hiirol. Jaabori ɗomngo yimɓe heewɓe woni ɗum yiɗani ɓurtugo: tigi yimɓe goɓɓe moƴɓe ngoni? Ammaa Bulus naa’ wi’ata walaa neɗɗo waɗata goɗɗum moƴɓum naa’ ngontam yettugo. O wi’ata walaa neɗɗo, yeeso Allah sanni ɗum, woni laaɓuɗo — walaa neɗɗo ɗon yiɗunde Allah e ɓernde maajum fuu, walaa neɗɗo ɗon waɗiroo ko Allah yiɗi sahaa fuu. Haala ’woɗɗitaade ko Allah yiɗi’ nder aayi 23 hollita ɗum: naa’ ko goɓɓe woɗɗitike haako e goɓɓe toowndi — en fuu mbaɗi hakke. Fawi nguu naa’ woni jeydaago nguurndam yimɓe tan, ammaa hobbaago wonde matsala junuuba woni jamaa, ngam ko fayde ngoo hollita ngam ɓesnde Habaaru Moƴɓere toɗɗi yimɓe fuu.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus wi’i ’hay gooto’ laaɓuɗo — o naa’ ɗon sendinde yimɓe hakkunde maɓɓe, ammaa e Allah sanni ɗum. No ƴetoore ɓuri accitago ɗum ɗum mbele sandararren woni cikakkuru Allah ko ɓurtii sendinde e goɗɗi yimɓe?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul’s claim is deliberately absolute and universal — ‘not even one’ leaves no room for exceptions. Many people’s instinctive reaction is that this feels too sweeping: surely some people are genuinely good? But Paul is not saying no one ever does anything kind or admirable. He is saying that no one, measured against God’s standard, is righteous — no one fully seeks God, no one consistently does what is right in God’s eyes. The phrase ’fall short of the glory of God’ in verse 23 captures this: it is not that some people fall slightly short and others massively so — all of us fall short. The point is not to rank human behaviour but to establish that the problem of sin is universal, which is the necessary foundation for understanding why the gospel applies to everyone.
elements.questionHint3: Paul says ’not even one’ is righteous — he is not comparing people to each other but to God’s standard. How might the conclusion change if the measuring stick is God’s perfect character rather than a comparison with other human beings?

elements.text1: Menene sakamakon zunubin mu?
elements.text2: Ɗume woni njoɓdi junuubaaji meeɗen?
elements.text3: What is the consequence of our sin?

elements.passageText1: <sup>23</sup>Gama sakamakon zunubi mutuwa ne, amma baiwar Allah ita ce rai madawwami a cikin Almasihu Yesu Ubangijinmu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>23</sup>Gama sakamakon zunubi mutuwa ne, amma kyautar Allah rai madawwami ne a cikin Kiristi Yesu Ubangijinmu.
elements.passageText4: <sup>23</sup>Ngam njoɓaari hakke ɗum mayde, ammaa caahu Allah woni yonki ki re’ataa keɓeteeki barka sumpootirki e Yeesu Almasiihu Joomiraawo meeɗen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>23</sup>For the payoff of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

elements.question1: Bulus ya ce zunubi yana samu menene? Wane bambanci ya zana a ƙarshen wannan aya?
elements.question2: Ɗume Bulus wi’i junuuba heɓata? Ɗume o sendindiri haa sakkitorde ayaare nde’e?
elements.question3: What does Paul say sin earns? What contrast does he draw at the end of the verse?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce sakamakon — albashi da aka samu — na zunubi mutuwa ce. Yana amfani da harshen albashi da gangan: mutuwa ba azaba ta bazata ba ce sai dai canjin adalci na rayuwa ta zunubi, abin da zunubi ke cancanta kuma ya samu. Bambancin da ya zana a ƙarshen aya shine mai ban mamaki kuma an gina shi da kyau: a kan albashin zunubi (wani abu da aka samu da aka cancanta) ya sanya kyautar Allah (wani abu da aka ba da kyauta ba a samu ba). Mutuwa abin da muke samu ne; rai madawwami abin da Allah ke bayarwa ne. Gefen biyu na aya ba za su iya bambanta fiye da haka ba — ɗayan adalci ne, ɗayan alheri ne. Dukkansu sun tattara cikin mutumtakar Yesu Almasihu, cikin sunansa aka ba da kyautar.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus da gangan ya yi amfani da kalmar ’sakamakon’ ko ’albashi’ don abin da zunubi ke haifar — wani abu da aka samu — sannan ya kwatanta shi da ’kyauta’ don abin da Allah ke bayarwa. Me ya sa bambancin tsakanin samun da karɓar a matsayin kyauta yana da muhimmanci ga aikin da yake yi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi’i njoɓaari — ko alɓasal heɓaaɗo — junuuba ɗum mayde. O huutori haala alɓasal bee annde: mayde naa’ daawal walaa laawol tan ammaa ko njoɓaari goonga yonki junuuba, ko junuuba fayi e heɓugo. Sendinnde nde o waɗi e sakkitorde ayaare nde’e woni toowndi e feewnaama: fawaade e njoɓaari junuuba (goɗɗum heɓaaɗo e yiɗiraangal) o dawi bii caahu Allah (goɗɗum hokkaangal bee fartaŋŋe e heɓaaki). Mayde ko men heɓirta; yonki ki re’ataa ko Allah hokkata. Ɓolal ɗiɗal ayaare nde’e naa’ mbaawi sendinde haa ɗon — gooto ko ladal, goɗɗo ko moƴƴere. E ɗiɗon ɓe ngontondiri e neɗɗaagu Yeesu Almasiihu, nder innde muuɗum caahu nguu hokketee.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus e annde huutori mbele ’njoɓaari’ naa ko junuuba waɗata — goɗɗum heɓaaɗo — nden sendini ɗum e ’caahu’ ngam ko Allah hokkata. Ngam ɗume sendinnde hakkunde heɓugo e heɓugo ko caahu toɗɗi ko o wi’ata?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says the payoff — the earned wage — of sin is death. He uses the language of wages deliberately: death is not an arbitrary punishment but the just return for a life of sin, what sin deserves and earns. The contrast he draws at the end of the verse is striking and carefully constructed: against the wages of sin (something earned and deserved) he places the gift of God (something freely given and unearned). Death is what we earn; eternal life is what God gives. The two sides of the verse could not be more different — one is justice, the other is grace. And both are concentrated in the person of Jesus Christ, in whose name the gift is given.
elements.questionHint3: Paul deliberately uses the word ’payoff’ or ’wages’ for what sin produces — something that is earned — and then contrasts it with a ’gift’ for what God offers. Why is that distinction between earning and receiving as a gift so important to the point he is making?

elements.text1: Wacce sa zuciya ko bege ya rage mana?
elements.text2: Ɗume woni tammunde meeɗen tan?
elements.text3: What is the only hope for us?

elements.passageText1: <sup>21</sup>Sai na ga, ashe, ya zama mini ka’ida, in na so yin abin da yake daidai, sai in ga mugunta tare da ni. <sup>22</sup>Ni kam, a birnin zuciyata, na amince da shari’ar Allah. <sup>23</sup>Amma ina ganin wata ka’ida dabam a gaɓoɓina, wadda take yaƙi da ka’idar da hankalina ya ɗauka, har tana mai da ni bawan ƙa’idar nan ta zunubi wadda take zaune a gaɓoɓina. <sup>24</sup>Kaitona! Wa zai cece ni daga jikin nan, wanda yake kai ni ga mutuwa? <sup>25</sup>Godiya tā tabbata ga Allah, akwai, ta wurin Yesu Almasihu Ubangijinmu! Ashe kuwa, ni a kaina, da hankalina, hakika Shari’ar Allah nake bauta wa, amma da jikina ka’idar zunubi nake bauta wa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>21</sup>Saboda haka na gane wannan ƙa’ida ce take aiki a cikina. Sa’ad da nake so in yi abu mai kyau, sai in ga cewa mugun nan ne nake yi. <sup>22</sup>Gama a ciki-cikina ina farin cikin da dokar Allah; <sup>23</sup>doca tana aiki a gaɓoɓin jikina, tana yaƙi da dokar hankalina, tana kuma sa in zama ɗaurarren dokar zunubin da take aiki a gaɓoɓin jikina. <sup>24</sup>Kaitona! Wane ne zai cece ni daga wannan jiki mai mutuwa? <sup>25</sup>Godiya ga Allah ta wurin Yesu Kiristi Ubangijinmu!
elements.passageText4: <sup>21</sup>Ɗum wartaniiyam ngikku, fuu carel ko ngiɗumi waɗuki goɗɗum boɗɗum, sey goɗɗum kalluɗum gonɗum nder am haɗayam waɗukiɗum. <sup>22</sup>Daga nder ɓernde am e mi nana belɗum dooka ƴiwoyɗum daga to Allah. <sup>23</sup>Ammaa nder ɓandu am mi yi’ii goɗɗum dooka feere e honida e dooka ɗum ɓernde am yiɗi. Ɗum wartiriiyam maccuɗo hakke gonɗum nder ɓandu am. <sup>24</sup>Mi neɗɗo jurminiiɗo! Moye hisnatayam daga ndu’u ɓandu am pooɗanaynduyam halkere? <sup>25</sup>Yettoore tabbita to Allah barka Joomiraawo meeɗen Yeesu Almasiihu!
elements.passageText5: <sup>21</sup>So I find this law at work: When I want to do good, evil is present with me. <sup>22</sup>For I delight in the law of God in my inner being. <sup>23</sup>But I see a different law in my members waging war against the law of my mind and making me captive to the law of sin that is in my members. <sup>24</sup>Wretched man that I am! Who will rescue me from this body of death? <sup>25</sup>Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then, I myself serve the law of God with my mind, but with my flesh I serve the law of sin.

elements.question1: Bulus yana bayyana fadan ciki. Shin taɓa samun abin da yake bayyanawa — son yin nagarta amma ka/ki sami kanka/kanki kina yin wani abu? Daga ina ya ce ceto ke fitowa?
elements.question2: Bulus ɗon sifa honnere gonnde nder ɓernde. A meunii heɓugo ko o sifata ɗon — a yiɗi waɗugo ko wooɗi ammaa a tawi a ɗon waɗa ko feere? Hoto o wi’i kisndam ɗam iwi?
elements.question3: Paul describes an internal battle. Have you ever experienced what he describes — wanting to do good but finding yourself doing otherwise? Where does he say the rescue comes from?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana bayyana abin da mutane da yawa ke gane: giɓin mai takaici tsakanin sanin abin da yake daidai da aikata shi, tsakanin niyyoyi da ayyuka. Ba yana bayyana mutumin da ba ya damu da nagarta ba — yana bayyana wanda ke son yin nagarta da gaske kuma yana farin cikin shari’ar Allah, amma ya gano karfi mai gaba da shi cikinsa yana cin yakin. Inafinsa ’Kaitona!’ ɗaya ne daga cikin mafi fasaha a Littafi Mai Tsarki. Muhimmin abu shi ne ba ya neman ƙarfin nufin kai, ƙa’idar kai, ko ƙoƙarin ɗabi’a don ceto — tambayarsa ita ce ’wa zai cece ni?’, ba ’me zan yi?’ ba. Amsar tana nan jiya: ceto yana fitowa ta Yesu Almasihu. Mafita ga fadan ciki ba ƙoƙari mafi ƙarfi ba ne sai dai wanda wani ya cece ka/ki.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya tsara tambayarsa a matsayin ’wa zai cece ni?’ maimakon ’me zan yi don inganta kaina?’ — menene zaɓin kalmar ’ceto’ ke gaya maka/ki game da ko Bulus yana ganin amsar zunubi tana cikinsa ko waje da shi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus sifirta goɗɗum yimɓe heewɓe nganndi: laangel pijirɗel hakkunde andugol ko wooɗi e waɗugo ɗum, hakkunde niyye e kuuɗe. O naa’ sifirta neɗɗo ngolɗum walaa ko yiɗu moƴɓude — o sifirta neɗɗo yiɗuɗo tigi waɗugo ko moƴɓi e nana belɗum dooka Allah, kono yi’irii e hoore maajum nguurngu keewgu feere ɓurgu jangoode. Fawi maako ’mi neɗɗo jurminiiɗo!’ ko gooto e mawɗi e Binndol Ceniiɗol hollitɗi tigi. Ko toɗɗiri Bulus naa’ ɗon yiɗude e himmere hoore maajum, laawol dawruɗo naa’ faaluki ɗabi’a ngam kisnaaɗo — ƴamre maako woni ’moye hisnayam?’, naa’ ’ɗume foti mi waɗugo?’ Jaabori nde maayi hedde: kisnaaɗo wana daga Yeesu Almasiihu. Jaabori battingol nder ɓernde naa’ faaluki heewiri, ammaa heɓugo kisnaaɗo daga neɗɗo goɗɗo.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus simmbi ƴamre maako no ’moye hisnayam?’ e ko ɓuri ’ɗume foti mi waɗugo ngam moƴɓude hoore am?’ — ɗume suɓugo mbele ’kisnaaɗo’ hollata dow ko Bulus miijata jaabori junuuba woni nder hoore maajum naa wano?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul describes an experience many people recognise: the frustrating gap between knowing what is right and actually doing it, between intentions and actions. He does not describe a person indifferent to goodness — he describes someone who genuinely wants to do good and delights in God’s law, yet finds an opposing force within them winning the battle. His cry of ’wretched man that I am!’ is one of the most honest outbursts in the Bible. Crucially, he does not look to willpower, self-discipline, or moral effort for rescue — his question is ’who will rescue me?’, not ’what must I do?’ The answer is immediate: rescue comes through Jesus Christ. The solution to the internal battle is not trying harder but being rescued by another.
elements.questionHint3: Paul frames his question as ’who will rescue me?’ rather than ’what should I do to improve myself?’ — what does the choice of that word ’rescue’ tell you about whether Paul thinks the answer to sin lies within himself or outside himself?

elements.text1: Mabuɗin gaskiya: Zunubi tayarwa ce ga Allah.
elements.text2: Nandola ɗo’o na naangi ɓernde — ammaa woni goonga. En mbaawaa feewnugo ɗum bee hoore meeɗen. Habaru belɗo woni Allah yoppaayi en walaa tammunde, nden ɗon ɗon janngirde tokkoore fuɗɗata.
elements.text3: The picture here is bleak — but it is honest. We cannot fix this ourselves. The good news is that God does not leave us without hope, which is exactly where the next study begins.

elements.text1: Gaskiya al’amari
elements.text2: Goongaaji mawninɗi
elements.text3: Key truths

elements.text1:
Zunubi tayarwa ce ga Allah. Ya kuma shigo ne sanadiyar mutanen farko, Adamu da Hauwa’u, a yayi da dagangan cikin sakaci suka ƙi bin umarnin Allah.

’Matar ta ga yadda itacen yana da kyau, ‘ya’yansa kuma kyawawa, abin sha’awa ne kuma ga ido, abin marmari ne domin ba da hikima, ta tsinka daga ’ya’yansa, ta kuwa ci, ta kuma bai wa mijinta waɗansu, shi kuma ya ci.’

Farawa 3:6
elements.text2:
Junuuba woni caluuru dow Allah. O natti duuniyaaru bee neɗɗo arandeejo e debbo, Aadama e Hawwa’u, nde ɓe calii tokkugo yamiroore Allah bee andal maɓɓe.

’Nde debbo on yi’i ɓiɓɓe leɗɗe ɗen ina nandi e ko nyaamete, e kadi ina belni gite, ina yiɗini kadi ngam heɓugo andal, o itti ɓe, o nyaami. O hokki kadi gorko maako gonɗo bee maako, kanko boo o nyaami.’ — Fuɗɗoode 3:6
elements.text3:
Sin is rebellion against God. It came into the world through the first man and woman, Adam and Eve, as they deliberately disobeyed God.

’When the woman saw that the fruit of the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eye, and also desirable for gaining wisdom, she took some and ate it. She also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it.’ — Genesis 3:6

elements.text1:
Duk wani ɗan adam in dai har ya taɓa rayuwa to ya yi wa Allah zunubi.

’…gama ’yan adam duka sun yi zunubi, sun kasa kuma ga ɗaukakar Allah….’

Romawa 3:23
elements.text2:
Koo moye neɗɗo gonɗo e yonki waɗi junuuba dow Allah.

’...ngam yimɓe fuu mbaɗi junuuba, ɓe njaasake boo manngu Allah...’ — Romanko’en 3:23
elements.text3:
Every human being who has ever lived has sinned against God.

’...for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God...’ — Romans 3:23

elements.text1: Abubuwa tunani akai
elements.text2: Ko miijoto-ɗaa dow mu’un
elements.text3: To think about

elements.question1: Tayaya zaka/ki iya yiwa aboki/ƙawa bayanin ma’anar zunubi bisa ga koyarwar Littafi Mai Tsarki?
elements.question2: No mballir-ɗaa soobaajo ma ma’ana junuuba bana ko Deftere Allah ekkitinta?
elements.question3: How would you explain to a friend the meaning of sin according to what the Bible teaches?

elements.question1: Menene abokan ka/ki suke tunani game da zunubi? Shin suna tunanin su kansu sun yi zunubi?
elements.question2: Ɗume sooba’en ma miijata dow junuuba? Ina ɓe miijata kanko’en boo ɓe ɗon mbaɗa junuuba?
elements.question3: What do your friends think about sin? Do they think that they sin themselves?

elements.text1: A binciken mu na ƙarshe mun koyi abinda Allah ya ke tunani game da zunubi da kuma sakamakon zunubin mu. Cikin wannan binciken zamu fara duba shirin Allah domin ceton mu, maimakon hukunta mu.
elements.text2: Nder jannde men ɓennunde, en nji’ii mawnugo junuuba e ko o danyata. Nandola man ɗonno niɓɓi. Ammaa Allah yoppaayi en ɗon. Jannde nde’e woni hoto habaru belɗo fuɗɗata — en ɗon ndaara feere Allah mawnunde, naa’ ngam o jukka en, ammaa ngam o hisna en.
elements.text3: In the last study we saw the seriousness of sin and its consequences. The picture was bleak. But God does not leave us there. This study is where the good news begins — we look at God’s extraordinary plan not to punish us, but to rescue us.

elements.text1: Menene Allah yake tunani game da duniyar nan tasa?
elements.text2: Ɗume Allah miijata dow duuniyaaru maako?
elements.text3: What does God think about His world?

elements.passageText1: <sup>16</sup>’Saboda ƙaunar da Allah ya yi wa duniya har ya ba da makaɗaicin Ɗansa, domin duk wanda ya gaskata da shi kada ya hallaka, sai dai ya sami rai madawwami.
elements.passageText3: <sup>16</sup>Gama Allah ya ƙaunaci duniya sosai har ya ba da wanda yake Ɗaya kuma Makaɗaicin Ɗansa domin duk wanda ya gaskata da shi kada yă hallaka sai dai yă sami rai madawwami.
elements.passageText4: <sup>16</sup>Ngam yidde nde Allah yiɗi duuniyaaru haa o hokkii Ɓiɗɗo maako bajjo: Bajjo woni ɓiɗɗo danyaaɗo kanko tan. O walaa minyiraawo koo mawniraawo. , ngam koo moye kooliiɗomo to’ halka ammaa heɓa yonki ki re’ataa.
elements.passageText5: <sup>16</sup>For this is the way God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

elements.question1: Wannan aya tana ce mana me game da halin Allah game da duniya, duk da zunubin da muka duba a cikin binciken da ya gabata?
elements.question2: Ɗume ayaare nde’e wolwata dow no Allah yi’ata duuniyaaru, koo nde en ngi’ii mawnugo junuuba nder jannde ɓennunde?
elements.question3: What does this verse tell us about God’s attitude towards the world, despite the sin we looked at in the last study?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Duk da zunubin duniya, halin Allah yana nuna ƙauna mai zurfi — ba fushi ko ƙi ba. Ƙaunarsa ta kasance mai girma sosai har ta sa shi ya yi aiki: ya ba da mafi ƙarancin abu da ya mallaka, wato makaɗaicin Ɗansa. Kuma manufar wannan kyauta ba azaba ba ce, sai dai ceto — domin duk wanda ya gaskata kada ya hallaka sai ya sami rai madawwami. Don haka, amsa Allah ga zunubin ɗan adam ba yanke hukunci ba ce, sai dai tayin ceto.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya yi amfani da wacce kalma don nuna ƙarfin ƙaunar Allah, kuma wannan ƙauna ta sa shi ya yi me?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Koo dow mawnugo junuuba duuniyaaru, haala Allah woni yidde mawnunde — wano kuuɓungal koo haylugal. Yidde maako mawnii haa o waɗi golle: o hokkii ko ɓuri teddugo e nder ko o arani, Ɓiɗɗo maako bajjo. Nden anniya ko hokki ɗum waa’ jukkere, ammaa hisneede — ngam koo moye kooliiɗo to halkataa ammaa heɓa yonki ngiinaaka. Laawol Allah joofingal e hakke neɗɗo ɗum, to wonaa kiita, ammaa hokkoore kisinii.
elements.questionHint2: Ɗume kalma Yahaya juɓɓinii hollitugol fii sabaabu Allah waɗi, nden yidde nde’e yahii haa ɗume?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Despite the world’s sin, God’s attitude is one of deep love — not anger or rejection. His love was so great that it moved him to action: he gave the most precious thing he had, his one and only Son. And the purpose of that gift was not punishment but rescue — so that anyone who believes would not perish but have eternal life. God’s response to human sinfulness is therefore not condemnation but an offer of salvation.
elements.questionHint3: What word does John use to describe God’s motivation, and what did that motivation lead God to do?

elements.text1: Menene Allah ya sa ya aiki Ɗan sa, Yesu, cikin duniya?
elements.text2: Ngam ɗume Allah lili Ɓiɗɗo maako, Yeesu, nder duuniyaaru?
elements.text3: Why did God send His Son, Jesus, into the world?

elements.passageText1: <sup>45</sup>Domin Ɗan Mutum ma ya zo ba domin a bauta masa ba, sai dai domin shi ya yi bautar, ya kuma ba da ransa fansa saboda mutane da yawa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>45</sup>Gama ko Ɗan Mutum ma, bai zo domin a bauta masa ba, amma don yă yi bauta, yă kuma ba da ransa fansa saboda mutane da yawa.’
elements.passageText4: <sup>45</sup>Ngam Ɓii Neɗɗo warii naa’ ngam o jagganee, ammaa ngam o jagganoo yimɓe, o hokka yonki maako ngam rimɗinki ɗuɗɓe.’
elements.passageText5: <sup>45</sup>For even the Son of Man did not come to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Yesu ya bayyana nufin zuwansa? Kalmar ’fansa’ tana nuna mana me game da halinmu?
elements.question2: No Yeesu siffata anniya maako? Ɗume kalma ’rimɗinki’ (fansa) hollata dow alhaali meeɗen?
elements.question3: How does Jesus describe his own purpose? What does the word ’ransom’ imply about our situation?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu ya bayyana nufin zuwansa a matsayin ba ya zo domin a bauta masa ba, sai dai domin ya yi bauta, musamman ya ba da ransa fansa saboda mutane da yawa. Kalmar ’fansa’ tana nuna cewa muna cikin yanayin bauta ko bashi da ba za mu iya biyansa da kanmu ba — an ɗaure wani, kuma dole a biya farashi don a ’yantar da shi. Idan aka yi amfani da wannan ga mu, yana nufin muna da ɗaurewa saboda zunubi da hukumcinsa, kuma Yesu da son ransa ya ba da ransa a matsayin kuɗin fansa wanda ya ’yantar da mu.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da yadda ake amfani da fansa a rayuwar yau da kullum — wannan yana nuna mana me game da halin mutanen da Yesu ya zo don taimakonsu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu siffitirii anniya maako bana o warii naa’ ngam o jagganee, ammaa ngam o jagganoo yimɓe, kadi ngam o hokka yonki maako rimɗinki ɗuɗɓe. Kalma ’rimɗinki’ hollata en ngoɗɗen e yanaaji jippaaki koo bornuuki ngam meeɗen keɓataa sooditugol — neɗɗo jogitaama, farilla keɓetee ngam yoɓooyrude mo. To nde ɗum yottiniraa meeɗen, ɗum hollata en ngonɗen nder jippaari junuuba e jukkere nde junuuba danyata, nden Yeesu e hol hoore maako hokkii yonki maako ngam kuugal ngal winnditini en.
elements.questionHint2: Miijo dow ɗume rimɗinki waɗiraa ngam nder nguurndam hannde — ɗume ɗum hollata en dow alhaali yimɓe ɓen Yeesu warii wallitudɓe?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus describes his purpose as coming not to be served but to serve, and specifically to give his life as a ransom for many. The word ’ransom’ implies that we are in a state of captivity or debt that we cannot pay ourselves — someone is held, and a price must be paid to secure their release. Applied to us, it means we are bound by sin and its penalty, and Jesus willingly gave his own life as the payment that sets us free.
elements.questionHint3: Think about what a ransom is used for in everyday life — what does that tell you about the condition of the people Jesus came to help?

elements.passageText1: <sup>8</sup>Amma kuwa Allah yana tabbatar mana da ƙaunar da yake yi mana, wato tun muna masu zunubi, Almasihu ya mutu dominmu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>8</sup>Amma Allah ya nuna mana ƙaunarsa a wannan. Tun muna masu zunubi tukuna, Kiristi ya mutu dominmu.
elements.passageText4: <sup>8</sup>Ammaa laawol ngol Allah hollii’en yidde maako woni, carel ko ngonɗen nder hakke, Almasiihu maayii ngam meeɗen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>But God demonstrates his own love for us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

elements.question1: Me ya sa ƙaunar Allah a nan ta yi fice musamman, bisa ga abin da Bulus ya faɗa?
elements.question2: Ɗume waɗata yidde Allah ɗon mawnugo haa ɗon, bana no Bulus wolwi?
elements.question3: What makes God’s love here particularly striking, according to Paul?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Abin da ya sa ƙaunar Allah ta yi fice musamman shi ne lokacin da aka nuna ta: Almasihu ya mutu dominmu tun muna masu zunubi tukuna. Ba mu gyara halayenmu ba, ba mu juya zuwa ga Allah ba, kuma ba mu samu tagomashi nasa ta wata hanya ba. Yawanci muna iya tsammani cewa wani zai yiwa mutumin mai kyau ko mai cancanta sadaukarwa — amma Allah ya nuna ƙaunarsa a daidai lokacin da ba mu cancanta ta ba. Wannan ƙauna ba ta da sharadi kuma ta fito ne daga Allah ɗaya kaɗai; tana fitowa ne daga halin Allah, ba daga kome a cikinmu ba.
elements.questionHint1: Lura da kalmar ’tun’ — a wane lokaci na halinmu na ɗabi’a ne Almasihu ya mutu, kuma don me wannan lokacin yana da muhimmanci?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ko waɗii yidde Allah ɓurɗi mawnugo woni sahaa nde holliraa: carel ko ngonɗen nder hakke, Almasiihu maayii ngam meeɗen. En ngoppaaki golle ɓurɗe, en ngurtaaki haa Allah, kadi en keɓaaki barke maako e laawol fuu. Ko addata, ko tawata neɗɗo wadday hoore maako ngam neɗɗo moƴƴo koo wooɗɗo yoɓde — ammaa Allah hollii yidde maako e sahaa nde ngonɗen maa ɓurɗi tooɗugo. Yidde nde’e waa’ e haala e tagoore; ɗum yoɓɓii daga ɓernde Allah, naa daga ko fuu e meeɗen.
elements.questionHint2: Firo kalma ’carel’ — to huunde bonde ngonɗon e nder ɗuum nde Almasiihu maayii, nden ngam ɗume sahaa nde’e woodani?
elements.sampleAnswer3: What makes God’s love striking is the timing: Christ died for us while we were still sinners. We had not cleaned up our act, turned to God, or earned his favour in any way. Normally we might expect someone to sacrifice themselves for a good or deserving person — but God demonstrated his love at the very point when we were least deserving of it. The love is unconditional and entirely one-directional; it comes from God’s character, not from anything in us.
elements.questionHint3: Notice the word ’while’ — at what point in our moral condition did Christ die, and why does the timing matter?

elements.passageText1: <sup>15</sup>Maganar nan tabbatacciya ce, ta kuma cancanci a karɓe ta ɗungum cewa, “Almasihu Yesu ya shigo duniya ne domin ceton masu zunubi,“ ni ne kuwa babbansu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>15</sup>Ga wata magana tabbatacciya wadda ta cancanci cikakkiyar karɓa. Kiristi Yesu ya zo duniya domin ceton masu zunubi, wanda nake mafi muni a cikinsu.
elements.passageText4: <sup>15</sup>Yeesu Almasiihu warii nder duuniyaaru ngam hisna gaɗooɓe hakke. Haala ka’a ka tabbutuka ka hewtii ɗum jaɓaka. Min boo, mi ɓurii koo moye waɗuki hakke.
elements.passageText5: <sup>15</sup>This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance: ‘Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners’—and I am the worst of them!

elements.question1: Bulus ya kira kansa ’mafi muni’ a cikin masu zunubi. Don me kuke tsammanin ya faɗi haka? Wannan yana nuna mana me game da mutanen da Yesu ya zo don cetarsu?
elements.question2: Bulus noddi hoore maako ’ɓurɗo mawnugo’ junuuba. Ngam ɗume a miijata o wolwi ɗum? Ɗume ɗum hollata en dow yimɓe ɓe Yeesu wari hisnugo?
elements.question3: Paul calls himself ’the worst’ of sinners. Why do you think he says this? What does it tell us about who Jesus came to save?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus mai yiwuwa yana magana game da zamaninsa na tsanantawa a kan Kiristoci da ƙarfi — wanda ya bi da kuma tsare mabiya Yesu. Bai faɗi haka don karya tawali’u ba, sai dai don ya nuna wata muhimmiyar hujja: idan Almasihu ya zo don cetarsa ma, to babu wanda ya wuce iyaka. Wannan yana nuna mana cewa Yesu bai zo don cetarwa masu kyaun ɗabi’a kawai ko masu zunubi kaɗan ba — ya zo don ceton masu zunubi, a taƙaice. Faɗin ceto yana da girma kamar zurfi na zunubin ɗan adam.
elements.questionHint1: Idan ko ’mafi muni’ a cikin masu zunubi an haɗa shi cikin nufin ceton Almasihu, to wannan yana nuna me game da ko wani zai iya ɗaukaka ya wuce iyaka saboda zunubinsa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus fii ɓuri gooto to o woni gaɗo Almasiihu ɓe, neɗɗo torrinooɗo goonɗinɓe e bawɗe — ɗum woni o yaaɓirii Yeesu goonɗinɓe ngonnoɗo o ɗaggoo ɓe e kaso. O wolwaa’ ɗum ngam fenanɗam, ammaa ngam o hollita haala mawnuunde: to Almasiihu warii hisnude kadi mo, to walaa mo yahrataa oon falnde. Ɗum hollata en Yeesu waraayi tan ngam hisnude ceniiɓe koo goonɗinɓe semmbe — o warii ngam hisna gaɗooɓe hakke, ɗum ɓuri fuu. Faɗi kisineeji ɓurii mawnugo bana mawnugo junuuba yimɓe.
elements.questionHint2: To kadi ’ɓurɗo mawnugo’ e hakke kawtaama nder anniya Almasiihu kisinirde, ɗume ɗum hollata e woodugol neɗɗo ɓurɓe mawnugo e hakke haa o hisinataake?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul is probably referring to his past as a violent persecutor of Christians — someone who had actively hunted down and imprisoned followers of Jesus. He does not say this to be falsely modest, but to make a point: if Christ came to save even him, then no one is beyond reach. It tells us that Jesus did not come only for the morally respectable or the mildly flawed — he came to save sinners, full stop. The scope of salvation is as wide as the depth of human sinfulness.
elements.questionHint3: If even the ’worst’ sinner is included in Christ’s saving purpose, what does that imply about whether anyone could be considered too sinful to be saved?

elements.text1: Sakamakon mutuwar Yesu
elements.text2: Ɗume woni danyeeki mayde Yeesu?
elements.text3: What is the result of Jesus’ death?

elements.passageText1: <sup>9</sup>To, tun da yake a yanzu an kuɓutar da mu ta wurin jininsa, ashe kuwa, ta wurinsa za mu fi samun kārewa daga fushin Allah ke nan.
elements.passageText3: <sup>9</sup>Da yake yanzu mun sami zama marasa laifi ta wurin jininsa, ashe kuwa za a cece mu daga fushin Allah ta wurinsa!
elements.passageText4: <sup>9</sup>Nde jooni en ngartii aadili’en barka ƴiiƴam:  Waato mayde maako.  Almasiihu, fii ɓurɗum tabbutuki woni o hisnay’en daga monnere Allah garaynde.
elements.passageText5: <sup>9</sup>Much more then, because we have now been declared righteous by his blood, we will be saved through him from God’s wrath.

elements.question1: Bisa ga Romawa 5:9, wanene ake kira su game da waɗanda suke amincewa da Almasihu, kuma an cece su daga me?
elements.question2: Bana ko woni nder Romanko’en 5:9, ɗume nanndi e yimɓe hooliiɓe Almasiihu, nden hoto ɓe kisinirtee?
elements.question3: According to Romans 5:9, what are those who trust in Christ declared to be, and what are they saved from?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bisa ga Romawa 5:9, waɗanda suke amincewa da Almasihu an yanke musu hukunci na maras laifi — hukuncin kotun da jinin Yesu (mutuwarsa mai kafara) ya sa ya yiwu. Kuma sakamakon wannan hukunci, an cece su daga fushin Allah. Wannan yana da muhimmanci: fushin Allah shi ne hukuncin adalcinsa ga zunubi, wanda dukkanmu mun cancanta. Amma ta wurin Almasihu, an magance wannan hukunci, kuma masu imani ba su ƙarƙashin barazanarsa.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya yi amfani da kalmar kotun — wane hukunci ne aka yanke wa masu imani, kuma wane hadari ne wannan hukunci ya kare su daga gare shi?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bana no woni nder Romanko’en 5:9, ɓen kooliɓe Almasiihu ngartaa aadili’en — kiita ngal danyaaɗangal e ƴiiƴam Yeesu (mayde maako danyoore kafara). Nden e saabii kiita ngal, ɓe kisinirtee daga monnere Allah. Ɗum mawnii: monnere Allah woni kiita makka goonga e dow hakke, ko fuu meeɗen njaarataa. Ammaa e laawol Almasiihu, kiita ngal waɗiraa, kadi goonɗinɓe ngonaa ɗon e saare monnere maako.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus juɓɓinii kalma nde woni e cukaɗirgol kiita — ɗume woni kiita nde taƴiraa e dow goonɗinɓe, nden ɗume woni haaɓuru nde kiita nde’e ɓannginii ɓe?
elements.sampleAnswer3: According to Romans 5:9, those who trust in Christ are declared righteous — a legal verdict made possible by Jesus’ blood (his atoning death). And as a result of that declaration, they are saved from God’s wrath. This is significant: God’s wrath is his righteous judgment against sin, which we all deserve. But through Christ, that judgment has been dealt with, and believers are no longer under its threat.
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses a courtroom-style word — what verdict is pronounced over believers, and what danger does that verdict protect them from?

elements.passageText1: <sup>16</sup>’Saboda ƙaunar da Allah ya yi wa duniya har ya ba da makaɗaicin Ɗansa, domin duk wanda ya gaskata da shi kada ya hallaka, sai dai ya sami rai madawwami. <sup>17</sup>Gama Allah bai aiko Ɗansa duniya domin yă yanke mata hukunci ba, sai dai domin duniya ta sami ceto ta wurinsa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>16</sup>Gama Allah ya ƙaunaci duniya sosai har ya ba da wanda yake Ɗaya kuma Makaɗaicin Ɗansa domin duk wanda ya gaskata da shi kada yă hallaka sai dai yă sami rai madawwami. <sup>17</sup>Gama Allah bai aiki Ɗansa duniya don yă yi wa duniya hukunci ba, sai dai domin yă ceci duniya ta wurinsa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>16</sup>Ngam yidde nde Allah yiɗi duuniyaaru haa o hokkii Ɓiɗɗo maako bajjo:  Bajjo woni ɓiɗɗo danyaaɗo kanko tan. O walaa minyiraawo koo mawniraawo. , ngam koo moye kooliiɗomo to’ halka ammaa heɓa yonki ki re’ataa. <sup>17</sup>Ngam Allah liloyaayi Ɓiɗɗo muuɗum nder duuniyaaru ngam o taƴanandu kiita. Ammaa o liloyiimo ngam o hisna duuniyaaru e laawol maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>16</sup>For this is the way God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life. <sup>17</sup>For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but that the world should be saved through him.

elements.question1: Yesu ya yi wa duk waɗanda suka gaskata da shi alƙawarin me, bisa ga Yahaya 3:16-17?
elements.question2: Ɗume Yeesu fodani koo moye kooliiɗo mo, bana no woni nder Yahaya 3:16-17?
elements.question3: What does Jesus promise to all who believe in him, according to John 3:16-17?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu ya yi alƙawarin cewa duk wanda ya gaskata da shi ba zai hallaka ba sai ya sami rai madawwami. Aya ta 17 ta ƙara haske ta hanyar nuna nufin Allah: bai aiko Ɗansa domin hukunta duniya ba, sai don ceton ta. Don haka masu imani sun sami kyautar da ta yi kyau — rai madawwami — da kuma tsira daga sakamakon da ya yi muni — la’ana da hallaka. Alƙawarin yana buɗe ga kowa (‘duk wanda ya gaskata’), kuma yana zuwa ne kai tsaye ta hanyar bangaskiya ga Ɗan Allah.
elements.questionHint1: Yesu ya nuna sakamakon biyu ga waɗanda suka gaskata — abu ɗaya da suka tsira daga gare shi da kuma abu ɗaya da suka samu. Kuna iya gano su duka?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu fodanii koo moye kooliiɗo mo to halkataa ammaa heɓay yonki ngiinaaka. Aaya 17 ɓetii ɗum e hollitugol anniya Allah: o liloyaayi Ɓiɗɗo maako ngam o taƴanando duuniyaaru, ammaa ngam o hisna duuniyaaru e laawol maako. Sabu ɗum goonɗinɓe keɓata hokkuure moƴƴunde — yonki ngiinaaka — kadi ɓannginirte daga danyeeki bonki — kiita e halka. Fodaari nde’e udditii ngam fuu (‘koo moye kooliiɗo’), nden ɗum yottii tan e laawol goonɗingol e Ɓii Allah.
elements.questionHint2: Yeesu hollita danyeeki ɗiɗi ngam kooliiɓe — huunde goo ɓe ɓannginirtee e nde’e, nden huunde goo ɓe keɓata. Mbele mbaawi siftorude fuu ɗiɗi?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus promises that everyone who believes in him will not perish but will have eternal life. Verse 17 adds to this by clarifying God’s intention: he did not send his Son to condemn but to save. So believers gain both a positive gift — eternal life — and an escape from a negative outcome — condemnation and perishing. The promise is open to everyone (‘everyone who believes’), and it comes directly through faith in God’s Son.
elements.questionHint3: Jesus gives two outcomes for those who believe — one thing they escape and one thing they receive. Can you identify both?

elements.text1: Menene Littafi Mai Tsarki yace game da waɗan da suka ƙi bada gaskiya ga Yesu?
elements.text2: Ɗume Deftere Allah wolwata dow ɓen ɓe koolaaki Yeesu?
elements.text3: What does the Bible say about those who do not believe in Jesus?

elements.passageText1: <sup>18</sup>Duk wanda ya gaskata da shi, ba za a yi masa hukunci ba. Wanda kuwa bai ba da gaskiya ba, an riga an yi masa hukunci, domin bai gaskata da sunan makaɗaicin Ɗan Allah ba.
elements.passageText3: <sup>18</sup>Duk wanda ya gaskata da shi ba za a yi masa hukunci ba, amma duk wanda bai gaskata ba an riga an yi masa hukunci domin bai gaskata da sunan wanda yake Ɗaya kuma Makaɗaicin Ɗan Allah ba.
elements.passageText4: <sup>18</sup>’Koo moye kooliiɗomo taƴantaake kiita, ammaa mo hoolaakimo o yaadake o taƴanaama kiita, ngam o hoolaaki Ɓiɗɗo Allah bajjo.
elements.passageText5: <sup>18</sup>The one who believes in him is not condemned. The one who does not believe has been condemned already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.

elements.question1: Yesu yana nuna bambancin sosai a nan. Wane bambanci ne ke tsakanin halin mai gaskata da wanda bai gaskata ba?
elements.question2: Yeesu ɗon holla ceergal mawnunggal ɗon. Ɗume woni ceergal caka alhaali kooliiɗo e mo hoolaaki?
elements.question3: Jesus draws a sharp distinction here. What is the difference between the situation of the one who believes and the one who does not?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Mai gaskata ba a hukunta shi ba — an ɗaga masa hukuncin ta wurin bangaskiya ga Yesu. Amma mai rashin bangaskiya, an riga an hukunta shi — ba a matsayin abin da zai zo a nan gaba ba, sai dai a matsayin gaskiyar halin da yake ciki yanzu. Abin lura shi ne, la’anan ba saboda jerin zunubai na musamman ba ne, sai dai saboda batun ɗaya kaɗai: rashin gaskatawa da sunan makaɗaicin Ɗan Allah. Yesu yana nuna waɗannan a matsayin matsayin daban guda biyu a gaban Allah, kuma bangaskiya cikinsa shi ne abin da ya raba su.
elements.questionHint1: Lura cewa Yohanna ya ce wanda bai gaskata ba ’an riga an hukunta shi’ — wane lokaci ne wannan jumlar take nuna cewa hukuncin ya fara aiki?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Kooliiɗo taƴantaake kiita — kiita dow makko ittiraa e laawol goonɗingol e Yeesu. Ammaa mo hoolaakimo, o yaadii o taƴanaama kiita — naa fii huunde waddata caggal, ammaa bana haqiqa alhaali makko jooni. Ko mawnii, taƴinde nde’e waɗirtaake ngam tiinde junuuba ɗuɗɓe, ammaa ngam huunde gootunde: koolaaki Ɓiɗɗo Allah bajjo. Yeesu hollita ɗiɗi ɗii bana yanaaji ɗiɗi ɗoɗɗi yeeso Allah, nden goonɗingol mo woni ko ittata ɓe.
elements.questionHint2: Firo no Yahaya wi’i mo hoolaakimo ’o taƴanaama kiita jooni’ — ɗume sahaa ngal hollita dow nde kiita nde’e fuɗɗii waɗde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The believer is not condemned — the verdict against them has been lifted through faith in Jesus. The non-believer, however, stands condemned already — not as a future possibility but as a present reality. Crucially, the condemnation is not because of a list of specific sins but because of a single issue: not believing in the name of God’s one and only Son. Jesus presents these as two completely different standing before God, with belief in him as the decisive factor that separates them.
elements.questionHint3: Notice that John says the unbeliever ’has been condemned already’ — what does the tense of that phrase suggest about when this judgment takes effect?

elements.passageText1: <sup>5</sup>Wannan kuwa ita ce tabbatar hukunci mai adalci na Allah, domin a lasafta ku a cikin waɗanda suka cancanci zama a Mulkin Allah, wanda kuke shan wuya dominsa, <sup>6</sup>domin kuwa Allah ya ga adalci ne, yă yi sakamokon ƙunci ga waɗanda suke ƙuntata muku, <sup>7</sup>yă kuma ba ku hutu har da mu ma, ku da ake ƙuntata wa ɗin, a ranar bayyanar Ubangiji Yesu daga Sama tare da manyan mala’ikunsa, a cikin wuta mai ruruwa, <sup>8</sup>yana ta sāka wa waɗanda suke ƙi sanin Allah, da kuma waɗanda suka ƙi bin bisharar Ubangijinmu Yesu. <sup>9</sup>Za su sha hukuncin madawwamiyar hallaka, suna a ware da zatin Ubangiji, da kuma, maɗaukakin ikonsa, <sup>10</sup>wato, a lokacin da ya dawo, tsarkakansa su ɗaukaka shi, duk masu ba da gaskiya kuma su yi al’ajabinsa a ranar nan, don kun gaskata shaidar da muka yi muku.
elements.passageText3: <sup>5</sup>Dukan wannan yana tabbatar cewa hukuncin Allah daidai ne, saboda haka kuma za a lissafta ku a kan kun cancanci mulkin Allah, wanda dominsa ne kuke shan wahala. <sup>6</sup>Allah mai adalci ne, Zai sāka wa masu ba ku wahala da wahala <sup>7</sup>yă kuma sauƙaƙa muku ku da kuke wahala, har ma da mu. Wannan zai faru sa’ad da aka bayyana Ubangiji Yesu daga sama cikin wuta mai ci tare da mala’ikunsa masu iko. <sup>8</sup>Zai hukunta waɗanda ba su san Allah ba da kuma waɗanda ba su yi biyayya da bisharar Ubangijinmu Yesu ba. <sup>9</sup>Za a hukunta su da madawwamiyar hallaka a kuma kau da su daga gaban Ubangiji da kuma daga ɗaukakar ikonsa <sup>10</sup>a ranar da zai dawo don a ɗaukaka shi a cikin mutanensa masu tsarki, yă kuma zama abin banmamaki ga dukan waɗanda suka gaskata. Wannan ya haɗa har da ku, domin kun gaskata shaidar da muka yi muku.
elements.passageText4: <sup>5</sup>Fuu ɗu’um tabbitinii kiita Allah e woodi goonga, ngam maajum ɗum wartiray’on kewtuɓe jooɗaaki nder laamu maako, ngam dow maajum njaroton torra. <sup>6</sup>Allah o gaɗoowo ko haani. O waatanay ɓen ɗon torrayɓe’on e torra, <sup>7</sup>o waltinay ɓerɗe mooɗon onon torreteeɓe. Minon maa o waltinay ɓerɗe amin. O waɗay non nyalnde nde Yeesu Almasiihu wangata daga dow nder hiite hawti e malaa’ika’en maako gooduɓe bawɗe. <sup>8</sup>Ganyuɓe Allah e ganyuɓe ngaɗa ko Habaru Belɗum ɗum Joomiraawo meeɗen Yeesu wi’i, Allah waatanayɓe halkere kallunde. <sup>9</sup>Ɓe njaray torra halkere nde haa abadaa, ɗum ittayɓe daga yeeso Joomiraawo e yeeso mangu bawɗe maako. <sup>10</sup>Halkere nden wangay nyalnde nde o wartoyta. O wartoyay ngam ceniiɓe maako teddinamo fuu kooliiɓemo mawninamo. Onon maa on kawtay e maɓɓe, ngam on njaɓii habaru ɗum min kokki’on.
elements.passageText5: <sup>5</sup>This is evidence of God’s righteous judgment, to make you worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you are suffering. <sup>6</sup>For it is right for God to repay with affliction those who afflict you, <sup>7</sup>and to give relief to you who are afflicted and to us as well, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels. <sup>8</sup>With flaming fire he will mete out punishment on those who do not know God and do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus. <sup>9</sup>They will undergo the penalty of eternal destruction, away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his strength, <sup>10</sup>when he comes to be glorified among his saints and admired on that day among all who have believed—and you did believe our testimony to you.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Bulus ya bayyana ƙaddarar waɗanda ba su san Allah ba kuma waɗanda suka ƙi yin biyayya da bisharar? Ta yaya wannan ke zaune a wurinka?
elements.question2: No Bulus siffata danyeeki ɓen ɓe anndaa Allah, ɓe ɗowtaaki Habaru Belɗo boo? No ɗum woniri e ma?
elements.question3: How does Paul describe the fate of those who do not know God and do not obey the gospel? How does this sit with you?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya bayyana ƙaddarar su a matsayin ’madawwamiyar hallaka, suna a ware da zatin Ubangiji da kuma maɗaukakin ikonsa.’ Wannan ba lalacewa gaba ɗaya ba ne, sai dai rabe-raben har abada daga Allah — tushen duk abin da ke da kyau, haske, da rai. Wannan bayanin yana tayar da damuwa sosai, kuma mutane da yawa suna jin takaici ƙwarai. Yana tayar da tambayoyin wahala game da adalci da halin Allah. Duk da haka Bulus yana shimfiɗa shi a cikin adalcin Allah: sakamakon da ya dace ne ga waɗanda suka ƙi Allah da bisharar tasa. Yadda yake zaune a wurinmu kowane ɗaya daban, kuma wannan amsa ta gaskiya tana cancancin bincike.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus yana amfani da wata jumla ta musamman don bayyana abin da wannan hallaka ke nufi a bangaren hulɗa — an rabe su daga me, kuma don me wannan cikakken bayani ya fi muhimmanci fiye da kalmar ’hallaka’ ita kaɗai?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus siffitii danyeeki maɓɓe bana ’halkere nde haa abadaa, ɗum ittayɓe daga yeeso Joomiraawo e yeeso mangu bawɗe maako.’ Ɗum waa’ weeteede ŋooŋee fuu, ammaa hareeji haa abadaa daga Allah — ɓe waɗo ɓuri moƴƴugo fuu, henni e yonki. Siftal nde’e ngonndi darɗori, kadi yimɓe ɗuɗɓe njii’ata ɗum kaaɗi. Ɗum nawraa faamu wanntoonde e yidde Allah. To wonaa non, Bulus daritirii ɗum e kiita goonga Allah: ɗum woni jaɓiraaɗe haani ngam ɓe ɓe gangii Allah e Habaru maako Belɗo. No ɗum woniri e meeɗen fuu ɗum hollata, nden jaabawol goonga ɗum wooɗi nder miijo.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus juɓɓinii haala keewnde sifitirde ko halkere nde’e hollita e laawol potal — ɓe ittirtee daga ɗume, nden ngam ɗume habaru ngal ɓuri teddude e kalma ’halkere’ ɗum kaɗum?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul describes their fate as ’eternal destruction, away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his strength.’ This is not annihilation but permanent separation from God — the source of all goodness, light, and life. It is a sobering description, and many people find it deeply uncomfortable. It raises hard questions about fairness and the character of God. Yet Paul frames it within God’s righteous justice: it is a right response to those who have rejected both God and the gospel. How it sits with us personally will vary, and that honest reaction is worth exploring.
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses a specific phrase to describe what this destruction means relationally — what are those people separated from, and why might that detail matter more than the word ’destruction’ alone?

elements.text1: Tayaya mutuwar Yesu ta cece mu?
elements.text2: No mayde Yeesu hisnirta en?
elements.text3: How does Jesus’ death save us?

elements.passageText1: <sup>46</sup>Wajen ƙarfe uku kuma sai Yesu ya ɗaga murya da ƙarfi ya ce, ‘Eloi, Eloi, lama sabaktāni?’ Wato, ‘Ya Allahna, ya Allahna, don me ka yashe ni?’
elements.passageText3: <sup>46</sup>Wajen sa’a ta tara, Yesu ya ɗaga murya da ƙarfi ya ce,  ‘Eloi, Eloi, lama sabaktani?’  (Wato,  ‘Ya Allahna, ya Allahna, don me ka yashe ni?’ ).
elements.passageText4: <sup>46</sup>E njamndi tati ndin, sey Yeesu wi’i e sembe, ‘Eloy, Eloy, lama sabattani?’ waato, ‘Yaa Allah am, Yaa Allah am, ngam ɗume cendirɗaa e am?’
elements.passageText5: 46At about three o’clock Jesus shouted with a loud voice, ‘Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?’ (that is, ‘My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?’).

elements.question1: Yesu yana ta ihu me daga gicciye? Menene ma’anar cewa aka ’yashe’ Ɗan Allah daga Ubansa a wannan lokaci?
elements.question2: Ɗume Yeesu fiirtata dow gaafaangal? Ɗume ɗum nandi nde Ɓiɗɗo Allah ’yoppaa’ e Baba maako nder sa’aare nde’e?
elements.question3: What does Jesus cry out from the cross? What might it mean that the Son of God was ’forsaken’ by his Father at this moment?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu yana ta ihu da cewa ’Ya Allahna, ya Allahna, don me ka yashe ni?’ — kukan rabuwa na gaskiya, ba kawai wahala ba. Ga Ɗan madawwami wanda ya kasance cikin haɗin kai cikakke da Uba koyaushe, wannan yashewar tana wakiltar wani abu da ba za a iya tunani da shi ba: fashewar wannan alaƙa. A ilimin tauhidi, yana nuna cewa Yesu yana ɗaukar cikakken nauyin sakamakon zunubi — rabe-rabe daga Allah — a maimakonmu. Hukuncin da ya kamata ya fado a kanmu saboda zunubin namu ya fado a kansa maimakon haka, shi ya sa ya dandani abin da muke cancanta.
elements.questionHint1: Da la’akari da abin da muka riga muka gani game da sakamakon zunubi (rabe-rabe daga Allah), yana nuna me cewa Yesu ya dandani wannan rabe-raben guda ɗaya a kan gicciye?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu wi’i e sembe ’Yaa Allah am, Yaa Allah am, ngam ɗume cendirɗaa e am?’ — haala yoppitaare goonga, naa tan haajande. Ngam Ɓiɗɗo ngiinaaka ngonɗo tawi jogaama e Baaba maako e haɓɓere timmiinde haa abadaa, yoppitaare nde’e hollita huunde maa miijaaka faa: ɓirnugol pocce potal ngal. E kulminol, ɗum hollita Yeesu jaɓi nauɗe mawnugo sakaare junuuba — ittugol daga Allah — ngam meeɗen. Kiita ndee waajudii dow meeɗen ngam junuubi meeɗen waɗi dow makko e ɗum addani mo anndugol ko meeɗen njaarake.
elements.questionHint2: To en ngi’ii ko Bulus hollitii dow sakaare junuuba (ittugol daga Allah), ɗume ɗum hollita nde Yeesu anndii ittugol gootol oo dow gaafaangal?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus cries out ’My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?’ — a cry of real abandonment, not just distress. For the eternal Son who had always existed in perfect unity with the Father, this forsakenness represents something almost unthinkable: a rupture in that relationship. Theologically, it points to Jesus bearing the full weight of sin’s consequence — separation from God — in our place. The judgment that should fall on us for our sin fell on him instead, which is why he experienced what we deserved.
elements.questionHint3: Given what we have already seen about the consequence of sin (separation from God), what might it mean that Jesus experienced that same separation on the cross?

elements.passageText1: <sup>21</sup>Shi da bai san zunubi ba, Allah ya mai da shi zunubi saboda mu, domin mu mu sami adalcin Allah ta wurinsa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>21</sup>Shi da bai san zunubi ba, Allah ya mai da shi kamar hadayar zunubi saboda mu, domin mu sami adalcin Allah ta wurinsa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>21</sup>Almasiihu meeɗaayi waɗuki hakke, ammaa Allah yowii fuu hakkeeji meeɗen dow maako, ngam laawol maako ngarten aadili’en yeeso Allah.
elements.passageText5: <sup>21</sup>God made the one who did not know sin to be sin for us, so that in him we would become the righteousness of God.

elements.question1: Wane musanyar Bulus ya bayyana a wannan aya? Yesu ya karɓi me, kuma masu imani suna samun me?
elements.question2: Ɗume woni waylowaylo ko Bulus siffata nder ayaare nde’e? Ɗume Yeesu jaɓi dow hoore maako, nden ɗume kooliiɓe keɓata?
elements.question3: What exchange does Paul describe in this verse? What did Jesus take on, and what do believers receive?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya bayyana musanyar biyu mai ban mamaki. Yesu, wanda ba shi da zunubinsa na kansa (‘wanda bai san zunubi ba’), an mai da shi zunubi dominmu — ya karɓi zunubin namu da duk sakamakonsa. Maimakon haka, masu imani suna samun adalcin Allah — ba adalcin da suka samu ba, sai dai wanda ya kasance na Allah kansa, an lissafta masu ta wurin haɗin kai da Almasihu. Musanya ce ta abubuwa masu bambanci: zunubin namu yana je kansa; adalcin nasa yana zuwa gare mu.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya ce Yesu ’bai san zunubi ba’ — don haka abin da ya bayyana ya yi mamaki ta wace hanya, kuma kowane ɓangare na musanyar yana ba da me kuma yana samun me?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus siffitirii waylowaylo mawɗo ɗiɗi. Yeesu, ɗum mo meeɗaayi waɗuki hakke (‘Almasiihu meeɗaayi waɗuki hakke’), Allah yowii fuu hakkeeji meeɗen dow maako — o jaɓi hakkeeji meeɗen e danyeeki mum fuu. E ɗum, kooliiɓe keɓata aadilaaku Allah — naa aadilaaku ɓe keɓi, ammaa aadilaaku nanngaaɗo daga Allah hoore maako, tokkaaɗo e maɓɓe e laawol kawtugol e Almasiihu. Ɗum woni waylowaylo ɗiɗi bertaaɗi: hakkeeji meeɗen yahata dow makko; aadilaaku maako warta dow meeɗen.
elements.questionHint2: Bulus wi’i Yeesu ’meeɗaayi waɗuki hakke’ — sabu ɗum, ɗume waɗata waylowaylo o siffitirii mawɗo, nden ɗume koo fuu nder waylowaylo nde hokkata e keɓata?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul describes a remarkable double exchange. Jesus, who had no sin of his own (‘the one who did not know sin’), was made to be sin for us — he took on our sin and its full consequence. In return, believers receive the righteousness of God — not a righteousness they earned, but one that belongs to God himself, credited to them through union with Christ. It is a swap of opposites: our sin goes to him; his righteousness comes to us.
elements.questionHint3: Paul says Jesus ’did not know sin’ — so what makes the exchange he describes so surprising, and what does each party in the exchange give and receive?

elements.passageText1: <sup>13</sup>Almasihu ya fanso mu daga la’anar nan ta Shari’a, da ya zama abin la’ana saboda mu, domin a rubuce yake cewa, ‘Duk wanda aka kafa a jikin itace, la’ananne ne.’
elements.passageText3: <sup>13</sup>Kiristi ya fanshe mu daga la’anar Doka ta wurin zama la’ana saboda mu, gama a rubuce yake cewa, ‘La’ananne ne duk wanda aka rataye a kan itace.’
elements.passageText4: <sup>13</sup>Yeesu Almasiihu rimɗinii’en daga laaneeki ki Attawra waddi, nde o warti ko laanaa ngam meeɗen. Ngam aayaare wi’ii, ‘Koo moye baraaɗo dow lekki:  Waato gaafaangal.  o laanaaɗo.’
elements.passageText5: <sup>13</sup>Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us (for it is written, ‘Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree’);

elements.question1: Wane ’la’ana’ ne Bulus ya ce Almasihu ya karɓa? Menene ma’anar cewa ya ’fanso’ mu daga gare ta?
elements.question2: Hoto woni ’laana’ ka Bulus wi’i Almasiihu jaɓi? Ɗume woni ma’ana o ’rimɗinii’ en diga maaka?
elements.question3: What ’curse’ does Paul say Christ took on? What does it mean that he ’redeemed’ us from it?
elements.sampleAnswer1: La’anar da Bulus yana magana a kanta ita ce la’anar Shari’a — hukunci da la’anar da suke fado a kan kowa wanda ya kasa kiyaye dokar Allah da cikakkiyar kiyayewa (wanda shine dukkanmu). Almasihu ya karɓi wannan la’anar a kansa ta hanyar ratayewa a kan gicciye, wanda Tsohon Alkawari ya ɗaura musamman da kasancewa a la’ana ta Allah. ’Fanso’ yana nufin sayen mu fita daga wannan yanayi — kamar sayan ’yancin bawa. Almasihu ya biya farashin la’anar domin mu kada mu kasance ƙarƙashinta.
elements.questionHint1: Menene ma’anar ’fanso’ a rayuwar yau da kullum (yi tunani game da fanso dukumin tallace-tallace ko saye wani abu baya), kuma ta yaya wannan ke bayyana mana abin da Almasihu ya yi dominmu?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Laana Bulus wi’i yoni laaneeki ki Attawra addiri — kiita e laanee nde fooɗata dow fuu mo waawaaki ɗowtaade suudu Attawra timmiinde (ko fuu meeɗen). Almasiihu jaɓi laana nde’e dow hoore maako nde o barɗaa dow gaafaangal, ko Attawra Mawniiɗo siftirii ko gooto e laanee Allah. ’Rimɗinii’ hollata sooditugol en daga yanaaji nde’e — bana soodugol yimɓe jeyaaɓe. Almasiihu yoɓii farilla laana nde ngam meeɗen ngonaa ɗon ɓanndiiɓe e nder nde.
elements.questionHint2: Ɗume woni ma’ana ’rimɗinki’ e nguurndam hannde (miijo dow rimɗinki bono koo soodugol huunde baya), nden no ɗum hollita en ko Almasiihu waɗi ngam meeɗen?
elements.sampleAnswer3: The curse Paul refers to is the curse of the law — the judgment and condemnation that falls on everyone who fails to keep God’s law perfectly (which is all of us). Christ took that curse on himself by hanging on a cross, which the Old Testament specifically associated with being cursed by God. To ’redeem’ us means to buy us out of that condition — like purchasing the freedom of a slave. Christ paid the price of the curse so that we would no longer be under it.
elements.questionHint3: What does it mean to ’redeem’ something in everyday life (think of redeeming a voucher or buying something back), and how does that shed light on what Christ did for us?

elements.passageText1: <sup>24</sup>Shi kansa ya ɗauke zunubanmu a jikinsa a kan gungume, domin mu yi zamanmu matattu ga zunubi, rayayyu kuma ga adalci. Da raunukansa ne aka warkar da ku.
elements.passageText3: <sup>24</sup>Shi kansa ya ɗauki zunubanmu a jikinsa a kan itace, domin mu mutu ga zunubai mu kuma rayu ga adalci; ta wurin raunukansa ne kuka sami warkarwa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>24</sup>Kanko e hoore maako jaɓi o yowanee hakke ɗum ngaɗuɗen o ɓilee dow gaafaangal. O waɗu non ngam laawol mayde maako alen waɗuki hakke, njooɗooɗen jonde aadilaaku. No aayaare wi’i, ‘Barka nawnooɗe maako on njamɗitii.’
elements.passageText5: <sup>24</sup>He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we may cease from sinning and live for righteousness. By his wounds you were healed.

elements.question1: Abin da Yesu ya ɗauka a kan gicciye bisa ga Bitrus, kuma Bitrus ya ce menene manufar wannan a gare mu?
elements.question2: Ɗume Yeesu jaɓi dow gaafaangal bana no Bitrus wi’i, nden ɗume Bitrus wi’i woni anniya ɗum haa meeɗen?
elements.question3: What did Jesus bear on the cross according to Peter, and what does Peter say the purpose of this was for us?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bitrus ya ce Yesu ya ɗauki zunubanmu a jikinsa a kan gicciye — ya ɗauki su ta hanyar zahiri da na mutuntaka. Manufar ita ce ta biyu: domin mu mutu ga zunubi (tsirin daga ikon zunubi) da kuma mu rayu ga adalci (sabon alkiblar rai mai kyau). Bitrus ya ƙara bayani cewa ta wurin raunukan Yesu ne aka warkar da mu — yana amfani da harshen warkarwa don bayyana abin da gicciye ke cika ta ruhaniya. Gicciye ba kawai game da gafartawa zunuban da suka gabata ba ne, sai dai kuma game da sauyi a nan gaba.
elements.questionHint1: Bitrus ya ambaci sakamako guda biyu na ɗaukar Yesu zunubanmu — ɗaya yana game da tsayar wani abu kuma ɗayan yana game da farawa da wani abu. Kuna iya gano me ya ƙunshi kowane?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bitrus wi’i Yeesu jaɓi e hoore maako hakke ɗum ngaɗuɗen o ɓilee dow gaafaangal — o jaɓi ɗum e nder junge maako e laawol goonga e neɗɗotaare. Anniya nde woni ɗiɗi: ngam laawol mayde maako alen waɗuki hakke (winnditugol daga bawɗe junuuba) kadi njooɗooɗen jonde aadilaaku (laawol ngenndi moƴƴi nguurndam). Bitrus ɓetii ɗum e wi’ugol barka nawnooɗe Yeesu ngartiri’on — juɓɓinii haala jamɗitaare ngam sifitirde ko gaafaangal ngal waɗi e ruuhu. Gaafaangal ngal waa’ tan e baylugol faggunde junuuba teeŋti ɓennunde, ammaa kadi e waylooji nguurndam caggal ɗum.
elements.questionHint2: Bitrus hollitii danyeeki ɗiɗi saabii Yeesu jaɓugol hakkeeji meeɗen — gooto e nder joppoore huunde nden gooto e fuɗɗoode huunde. Mbele mbaawi siftorude ɗume koo fuu e maɓɓe woni?
elements.sampleAnswer3: Peter says Jesus bore our sins in his body on the cross — he physically and personally carried them. The purpose was twofold: that we might cease from sinning (freedom from sin’s power) and live for righteousness (a new positive direction of life). Peter also adds that by Jesus’ wounds we were healed — using the language of healing to describe what the cross accomplishes spiritually. The cross is not only about forgiveness of past sin but about transformation for the future.
elements.questionHint3: Peter mentions two results of Jesus bearing our sins — one is about stopping something and the other is about starting something. Can you identify what each is?

elements.text1: Ayoyin da ke sama sun bayyana abinda Yesu ke yi a kan gicciye. Ba mutuwa ta al’ada ba ce.
elements.text2: Aayaje dow ɗee ɗon manti ko Yeesu waɗata dow gaafaangal. O maayaayi bana neɗɗo meere koo yeru moƴƴo tan — O jaɓi jukkere hakkeeji meeɗen dow hoore maako aiyee meeɗen. Ɗum woni ko waɗi mayde maako woni feere e mayde koo moye feere nder taariiki.
elements.text3: The verses above describe what Jesus was doing on the cross. He was not simply dying as a martyr or a good example — He was taking the penalty for our sin in our place. This is what makes His death different from any other death in history.

elements.text1: Don mai mutuwa bata yi nasara da Yesu ba?
elements.text2: Ngam ɗume mayde waawaayi fooluki Yeesu?
elements.text3: Why was Jesus not defeated by death?

elements.passageText1: <sup>24</sup>Amma Allah ya tashe shi bayan ya ɓalle masa ƙangin mutuwa, domin ba shi yiwuwa mutuwa ta riƙe shi.
elements.passageText3: <sup>24</sup>Amma Allah ya tashe shi daga matattu, ya ’yantar da shi daga azabar mutuwa, don ba ya yiwuwa mutuwa ta riƙe shi.
elements.passageText4: <sup>24</sup>Ammaa Allah umminiimo daga mayde, wurtiniimo yenaande, ngam mayde waawataa jogaakimo.
elements.passageText5: <sup>24</sup>But God raised him up, having released him from the pains of death, because it was not possible for him to be held by it.

elements.question1: Allah ya yi me, kuma don me Bitrus ya ce mutuwa ba za ta iya riƙe Yesu ba?
elements.question2: Ɗume Allah waɗi, nden ngam ɗume Bitrus wi’i mayde waawaayi jogitaki Yeesu?
elements.question3: What did God do, and why does Peter say death could not hold Jesus?
elements.sampleAnswer1: Allah ya tashe Yesu, ya ’yantar da shi daga mutuwa. Dalilim Bitrus ya bayyana yana da ban mamaki: kawai ba shi yiwuwa mutuwa ta riƙe shi. Mutuwa ba ta da iƙirarin ƙarshe akan wanda shi kansa tushen rai ne kuma ba shi da zunubinsa na kansa. Don haka tashin matattu ba mamaki na baya ba ne, sai dai abin da ya zama tilas — mutuwa ba ta da ikon halal a kan Yesu lokacin da aikin ɗaukar zunubi ya ƙare.
elements.questionHint1: Bitrus ya ce ’ba ya yiwuwa’ mutuwa ta riƙe Yesu — wannan kalmar mai ƙarfi tana nuna me game da alaƙar da ke tsakanin Yesu da mutuwa?
elements.sampleAnswer2: Allah umminiimo Yeesu daga mayde, wurtiniimo yenaande. Saabii Bitrus holliti mawnii: mayde waawataa tan jogaakimo. Mayde walaa iƙirari timmiinde dow mo woni hoore maako ɓuri nguurndam fuu, ɗum mo meeɗaayi waɗuki hakke maako. Sabu ɗum, umminirde nde waa’ yeccitaare maayaande, ammaa huunde waajundaande — mayde walaa bawɗe goonga dow Yeesu nde golle jaɓugol hakke timmiima.
elements.questionHint2: Bitrus wi’i ’waawataa’ mayde jogitaki Yeesu — ɗume kalma mawnde nde’e hollita dow pocce Yeesu e mayde?
elements.sampleAnswer3: God raised Jesus up, releasing him from death. Peter’s reason is striking: it was simply not possible for death to hold him. Death has no ultimate claim over the one who is the source of life itself and who had no sin of his own. The resurrection is therefore not a surprise reversal but an inevitability — death had no rightful power over Jesus once the work of bearing sin was complete.
elements.questionHint3: Peter says it was ’not possible’ for death to hold Jesus — what does that strong word suggest about the relationship between Jesus and death?

elements.text1: Ya Bulus ya yi bayani akan Yesu?
elements.text2: No Bulus siffitiri Yeesu?
elements.text3: How is Jesus described by Paul?

elements.passageText1: <sup>5</sup>Domin Allah ɗaya ne, matsakanci kuma ɗaya ne, a tsakanin Allah da mutane, Almasihu Yesu, mutum, <sup>6</sup>wanda ya ba da kansa fansa saboda kowa da kowa. An kuwa yi shaidar wannan a daidai lokacinsa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>5</sup>Gama Allah ɗaya ne, matsakanci kuma ɗaya tsakanin Allah da mutane, shi ne kuwa Kiristi Yesu, <sup>6</sup>wanda ya ba da kansa fansa domin dukan mutane, shaidar da aka bayar a daidai lokaci.
elements.passageText4: <sup>5</sup>Ngam Allah o go’oto, nden ciryootirɗo hakkunde Allah e yimɓe o go’oto. Kanko woni Yeesu Almasiihu, <sup>6</sup>kokkuɗo hoore maako ngam o rimɗina yimɓe. Kanjum woni ceyda ɗum um hokki nde carel waɗi.
elements.passageText5: <sup>5</sup>For there is one God and one intermediary between God and humanity, Christ Jesus, himself human, <sup>6</sup>who gave himself as a ransom for all, revealing God’s purpose at his appointed time.

elements.question1: Wane abubuwa guda biyu ne Bulus ya ce su kaɗai ne ’ɗaya’ bisa ga wannan sashe? Wane rawar da Yesu ke cika tsakanin Allah da mutane?
elements.question2: Ɗume woni kujuuje ɗiɗi ɗe Bulus wi’i ngoni ’go’otel’ tan bana no woni nder aayaje ɗee? Ɗume woni kuugal ngal Yeesu waɗata hakkunde Allah e yimɓe?
